name: CSV Parts Reader
description: Read an Albano-format 2D packing benchmark CSV  (num,polygon rows where polygon is a JSON-ish [[x,y], ...])  and emit one closed PolylineCurve per part (with the row's  multiplicity respected).

inputs.name: CSV Path
inputs.desc: Absolute path to a benchmark CSV (Albano/Blaz/Dagli/Jakobs  format: header 'num,polygon', then rows with an integer  multiplicity and a JSON-ish polygon vertex list).

inputs.name: Scale
inputs.desc: Per-coordinate scale factor (e.g. set 0.001 to convert  millimetre Albano coordinates to metres).

inputs.name: Expand Multiplicity
inputs.desc: If True, each row is emitted `num` times. If False, the  row is emitted once and the multiplicity is exposed  verbatim in the Counts output.

outputs.name: Parts
outputs.desc: One closed PolylineCurve per emitted part.

outputs.name: Counts
outputs.desc: Per-row multiplicity from the CSV.

outputs.name: Row Indices
outputs.desc: 0-based source row index per emitted part (lines up with  the canonical benchmark numbering).

outputs.name: Total Parts
outputs.desc: Total number of curves emitted.

outputs.name: Report
outputs.desc: One-line summary of the read.

name: Floor Tile (Boundary-Trimmed)
description: Divide a floor boundary into standard stone tiles on a module grid (tile face + grout joint) by  straight full-span (guillotine) lines, trimming the perimeter tiles to the boundary. Choose the  start: a corner, a picked point, or a centred/symmetric layout that balances the border cuts  equally on opposite walls. The ANSI half-tile no-sliver rule is enforced by auto-centring (the  grid shifts by half a module to split a thin sliver into two larger border cuts). Each tile  carries a GRAIN DIRECTION, output both as a direction line (the feature) and as a texture-mapping  frame: feed the tile meshes to a Custom Preview Material with a scanned stone image and the grain  follows. Set Continuous for a slip-match (the floor reads as one slab). Deterministic.

algorithm.title: Floor setting-out: balanced/centred layout and the ANSI half-tile no-sliver rule
algorithm.citation: ANSI A108.02 4.3.2 (centre and balance tile, no cuts smaller than half size); CTEF/TCNA tile layout practice

inputs.name: Boundary
inputs.desc: Closed planar floor outline curve (the room edge), in a WorldXY-parallel plane.

inputs.name: Holes
inputs.desc: Optional closed obstacle/hole curves inside the floor (columns, openings); tiles are trimmed around them.

inputs.name: TileX
inputs.desc: Tile face width (model units).

inputs.name: TileY
inputs.desc: Tile face height (model units).

inputs.name: Joint
inputs.desc: Grout joint width; module pitch = tile + joint. Stone 2-6, rectified 3.

inputs.name: Start
inputs.desc: Start mode: 0 = corner (full tile at the boundary min corner), 1 = picked point (Anchor),  2 = centred/symmetric (equal border cuts on opposite walls).

inputs.name: Anchor
inputs.desc: Lattice origin for Start = 1 (picked point); the corner of one full tile.

inputs.name: Grain
inputs.desc: Grain/vein direction in DEGREES (the grain feature; also rotates the texture mapping).

inputs.name: GrainField
inputs.desc: Grain pattern: 0 = monolithic (all one way), 1 = quarter-turn (checkerboard 0/90), 2 = random.

inputs.name: Sliver
inputs.desc: No-sliver acceptance: perimeter cuts must be >= this fraction of the tile (0.5 ANSI, 0.333 fallback).

inputs.name: Match
inputs.desc: Texture continuity: 0 = per-tile (each tile shows the whole image, rotated to its grain),  1 = slip-match (UVs flow across the floor so it reads as one slab), 2 = book-match (adjacent  tiles mirror so the veins meet at the joints).

inputs.name: Stagger
inputs.desc: Running-bond row offset: 0 = stack bond, 1 = 1/3 offset, 2 = 1/2 offset. Large-format tiles  (a side > 380) auto-cap at 1/3 to control lippage.

inputs.name: Image
inputs.desc: Optional stone-texture image file path. When supplied, the floor is DRAWN on screen with the  image mapped to the grain (no extra wiring); also emitted as Material for Custom Preview / baking.

inputs.name: Rates
inputs.desc: Optional cost rates (defaults kept where omitted). Order: material/m2, overage frac, cut/tile,  set-out stack, set-out matched, lay/m2, lay/tile, large-format/m2, premium[per,slip,book],  matchLabour[per,slip,book]/m2. Drives the Cost/m2 and Costing outputs.

outputs.name: Tiles
outputs.desc: Trimmed tile boundary polylines (full tiles + cut perimeter tiles).

outputs.name: Direction
outputs.desc: Per-tile grain direction line from the tile centre (the grain feature; draw as arrows).

outputs.name: Full
outputs.desc: True for a full module tile, false for a cut perimeter tile.

outputs.name: TexMesh
outputs.desc: Per-tile mesh carrying grain-aligned texture coordinates. Feed a Custom Preview Material with a  scanned stone image and the texture maps per the grain direction.

outputs.name: MapFrame
outputs.desc: Per-tile texture-mapping plane (origin = tile centre, X rotated by the grain). Use with Rhino's  planar TextureMapping (CreatePlanarMapping + SetTextureMapping) for object-level mapping.

outputs.name: Report
outputs.desc: Tile counts (full/cut), coverage, smallest perimeter cut vs the no-sliver threshold, grain field,  match mode and row offset.

outputs.name: Cost/m2
outputs.desc: Estimated installed cost per square metre of floor for the current config (material + cutting +  set-out + laying + matching, on the Rates). Illustrative; override Rates with local prices.

outputs.name: Costing
outputs.desc: Cost report: the material vs operation breakdown and the total $/m2 for the current config, plus a  match sweep (PerTile/Slip/Book at this layout) and a size sweep (re-packed tile-size ladder).

Supports organic sheet outlines, hole avoidance, spacing, rotation search, and non-blocking solve execution. Implements NFP-assisted bottom-left nesting (Burke et al. 2007).

name: Freeform Sheet Nest (Exact NFP)
description: Packs closed planar parts into freeform sheets using an exact No-Fit-Polygon  Bottom-Left-Fill solver. The feasible region for each part is the inner-fit polygon  minus the union of no-fit polygons of placed parts and holes, so parts never overlap  by construction (a hard constraint, not a trim). Implements bottom-left-fill  (Burke et al. 2006) over Minkowski-sum NFP/IFP (Bennell & Oliveira 2009) on a Clipper2  back-end. Sibling of the V506 nester; V506 is unchanged.

algorithm.title: Exact No-Fit-Polygon Bottom-Left-Fill (hard non-overlap by construction)
algorithm.citation: Burke, E.K., Hellier, R., Kendall, G., Whitwell, G. (2006). "A New Bottom-Left-Fill Heuristic Algorithm for the Two-Dimensional Irregular Packing Problem." Operations Research 54(3):587-601

inputs.name: Parts
inputs.desc: Closed planar part curves to pack.

inputs.name: Sheet Outlines
inputs.desc: Closed planar sheet boundary curves.

inputs.name: Sheet Holes
inputs.desc: Hole curves as a tree. Branch {i} = sheet i.

inputs.name: Spacing
inputs.desc: Clearance between parts and boundaries.

inputs.name: Rotations
inputs.desc: Allowed rotation angles in degrees.

inputs.name: Sort Mode
inputs.desc: 0 UserOrder, 1 Area↓, 2 Width↓, 3 Height↓, 4 MaxDim↓.

inputs.name: Tolerance
inputs.desc: Geometric tolerance. 0 (default) = AUTO: use the active document's absolute tolerance (mm doc -> mm tol, m doc -> m tol). Set a positive value to override.

inputs.name: Seed
inputs.desc: 0 = deterministic. Non-zero changes tie-break.

inputs.name: Run
inputs.desc: Set true to execute packing.

outputs.desc: Placed part curves.

description: Generate a diagnostic no-fit polygon from two closed planar polylines. Implements no-fit polygon construction (Burke et al. 2007).

algorithm.title: No-fit polygon construction (orbital / boundary slide)
algorithm.citation: Burke, E., Hellier, R., Kendall, G., Whitwell, G. (2007). "Complete and robust no-fit polygon generation for the irregular stock cutting problem." Eur. J. Oper. Res.

inputs.name: Stationary
inputs.desc: Stationary closed polygon.

inputs.name: Sliding
inputs.desc: Sliding closed polygon.

inputs.name: Tolerance
inputs.desc: Geometric tolerance.

inputs.name: Max Iterations
inputs.desc: Reserved for future full concave NFP implementation.

inputs.name: Rectangle Shortcut
inputs.desc: Reserved for future rectangle-specific NFP implementation.

outputs.desc: No-fit polygon curve.

outputs.name: Error Code
outputs.desc: 1 convex OK, 2 approximation, 0 failed.

outputs.name: Message

name: Residual Voids
description: Detect 2D residual voids inside a sheet polygon not covered by any  placed part. Uses cell-grid sampling + 4-neighbour connected-component  labelling. Reports each void's bounding rectangle and approximate area;  small voids below MinArea are filtered. Frahan-original method.

algorithm.title: Grid sampling + connected-component void detection
algorithm.citation: Frahan-original

inputs.name: Sheet
inputs.desc: Closed planar curve representing the sheet outline.

inputs.name: Placed Parts
inputs.desc: Closed planar curves representing already-placed parts.

inputs.name: Cell Size
inputs.desc: Sampling cell size in model units. Smaller = more accurate, slower.

inputs.name: Min Void Area
inputs.desc: Minimum reportable void area in model-unit-squared.  Smaller voids are filtered.

inputs.name: Discretisation Tolerance
inputs.desc: Tolerance used when discretising the sheet and parts to polylines.

outputs.name: Void Bounds
outputs.desc: Axis-aligned bounding rectangle of each detected void.

outputs.name: Void Areas
outputs.desc: Approximate area of each detected void.

outputs.name: Cell Counts
outputs.desc: Cells per detected void.

outputs.name: Total Void Area
outputs.desc: Sum of all reported void areas.

outputs.name: Report
outputs.desc: Human-readable summary.

name: Sheet Nest (Hole-Aware)
description: Deterministic hole-aware 2D nester: parts are placed on a sheet with defects (holes) by  exact no-fit-polygon bottom-left-fill, and smaller parts are nested INSIDE the holes of  larger placed parts via the inner-fit region. No-fit and inner-fit polygons are built  exactly as Clipper2 Minkowski sums/erosions (Bennell & Oliveira 2009) and placement is  bottom-left-fill (Burke et al. 2006), so layouts are 0-overlap by construction.  Rotations are contact-adaptive: the uniform base set is extended with edge-alignment  angles against the sheet, the latest neighbour, and host holes so parts seat flush.  Returns valid hole-aware layouts where hole-blind nesters fail; an exact rectangle  shelf fast-path accelerates all-rectangle instances. Deterministic: the same inputs  always reproduce the same cut layout.

algorithm.title: No-fit-polygon / inner-fit-polygon via Minkowski sum
algorithm.citation: Bennell, J.A. & Oliveira, J.F. (2009). "A tutorial in irregular shape packing problems." J. Oper. Res. Soc. 60(S1):S93-S105

inputs.name: Sheets
inputs.desc: Closed planar sheet boundary curve(s). Multiple sheets nest by greedy overflow: sheet 0  fills first, unplaced parts carry to sheet 1, and so on. Sheets stay at their drawn  positions.

inputs.name: Sheet Holes
inputs.desc: Closed sheet defect/hole curves (flat list or tree). Each hole is routed to whichever sheet  geometrically CONTAINS it (tree path {s} is only the fallback) — no tree matching or  grafting required; sheets without holes need nothing.

inputs.name: Parts
inputs.desc: Closed planar part outline curves to nest.

inputs.name: Part Holes
inputs.desc: Part hole curves (flat list or tree). Each hole is routed to the SMALLEST part outline that  geometrically CONTAINS it (tree path {i} -> Parts[i] is only the fallback) — no tree  matching or grafting required; parts without holes need nothing. Parts with holes are  placed first as hosts, then smaller parts nest into their holes via the inner-fit region.

inputs.name: Spacing
inputs.desc: Clearance between parts and boundaries.

inputs.name: BaseRotations
inputs.desc: Uniform base rotation count (4 = 0/90/180/270 degrees).

inputs.name: ContactRotations
inputs.desc: Longest-edge count per polygon used to build contact (edge-alignment) rotation angles.

inputs.name: Resolution
inputs.desc: SOLVER sampling resolution for smooth curves: uniform-by-length vertices per closed curve  (16..200, default 24). This ONLY sets the collision proxy — the Placed output is always the  exact ORIGINAL curve, transformed — so there is no output-quality reason to raise it. Solve  time grows ~QUADRATICALLY with this while packing density is nearly flat (benchmark: 48 verts  was ~10-20x slower than 24 for <2% density gain). Raise it ONLY when small parts must seat  into tight CONCAVE notches; otherwise leave it low for fast nesting.

inputs.name: MultiStart
inputs.desc: Number of deterministic part orders the general engine tries per sheet, keeping the densest  valid layout (1..4; default 4). Orders: area / max-dimension / width / height, all descending.  1 = the original single largest-first pass. Higher values raise irregular-outline density at a  near-linear wall-time cost (4 orders is ~4x the solve time of 1) and never reduce placements or  validity. The exact rectangle fast-path ignores this (it is already optimal). Output stays  deterministic: identical inputs always reproduce the same layout.

outputs.name: Placed
outputs.desc: The ORIGINAL part curves at full resolution, moved to their placed positions (placement  order). The solver works on coarse collision proxies internally; output geometry stays  exact for fabrication.

outputs.name: Source
outputs.desc: For each placed curve, the index of the source curve in the Parts input (labeling/etching map).

outputs.name: Transform
outputs.desc: For each placed curve, the rigid placement transform (rotation about the world Z origin,  then translation). Apply it to the original part curve, its holes, or any decoration.

outputs.name: Nested
outputs.desc: True where the corresponding placed part was nested into a host part's hole.

outputs.name: Report
outputs.desc: Placed count, part-holes filled, density, engine note, elapsed ms, valid flag.

outputs.name: Density
outputs.desc: Placed part material area / net sheet area (sheet minus its holes).

outputs.name: Valid
outputs.desc: True when the final layout passed the independent boolean (path-free) validation.

outputs.name: Placed Holes
outputs.desc: The placed parts' own hole curves at full resolution, moved with their parts: branch path  {i} holds the hole curves of Placed[i]. Subtract them from Placed[i] for the true cut profile.

outputs.name: Sheet
outputs.desc: For each placed curve, the index of the sheet it landed on (greedy overflow order).

name: Sheet Pack (Unified Async)
description: Async variant of Frahan Sheet Pack (Unified). Same Variant routing  as the sync version but runs on a background thread so Grasshopper  stays responsive during long packs. Pick the variant with the Variant  input; default is V506. Implements NFP-assisted bottom-left nesting (Burke et al. 2007).

algorithm.title: NFP-assisted bottom-left irregular nesting
algorithm.citation: Burke, E., Hellier, R., Kendall, G., Whitwell, G. (2007). "Complete and robust no-fit polygon generation for the irregular stock cutting problem." Eur. J. Oper. Res.

inputs.name: Parts

inputs.desc: Closed planar sheet boundary curves.

inputs.name: Sheet Holes

inputs.desc: 0 UserOrder, 1 Area↓, 2 Width↓, 3 Height↓, 4 MaxDim↓.

inputs.name: Seed
inputs.desc: 0 = deterministic; non-zero changes tie-breaking.

inputs.desc: Set to true to execute packing.

name: Sheet Pack (Unified)
description: Unified entry point for Frahan's four 2D irregular-sheet solver  variants (V1 / V2 / V3 / V506). Pick the variant with the Variant  input; default is V506. Synchronous solve only - for the async  variant, use 'Frahan Sheet Pack (Unified Async)' / FreeNestUA. [Burke et al. 2007]

algorithm.title: No-fit polygon construction
algorithm.citation: Burke, Hellier, Kendall, Whitwell 2007, European Journal of Operational Research 179(1):27-49 Complete and robust no-fit polygon generation for the irregular stock cutting problem

inputs.name: Parts
inputs.desc: Closed planar part curves to pack.

inputs.name: Sheet Outlines
inputs.desc: Closed planar sheet boundary curves.

inputs.name: Sheet Holes
inputs.desc: Hole curves as a tree. Branch {0} = sheet 0, {1} = sheet 1, etc.

inputs.name: Spacing
inputs.desc: Clearance between parts and between parts and boundaries.

inputs.name: Rotations
inputs.desc: Allowed rotation angles in degrees (default 0, 90, 180, 270).

inputs.name: Sort Mode
inputs.desc: 0 UserOrder, 1 Area↓, 2 Width↓, 3 Height↓, 4 MaxDim↓.

inputs.name: Tolerance
inputs.desc: Geometric tolerance for containment and collision. 0 (default) =  AUTO: use the active document's absolute tolerance, so a millimetre  document gets a millimetre tolerance and a metre document a metre  tolerance (no manual per-scale tuning). Set a positive value to override.

inputs.name: Seed
inputs.desc: 0 = deterministic; non-zero changes tie-breaking.

inputs.name: Run
inputs.desc: Set to true to execute packing.

inputs.name: Max Candidates
inputs.desc: Candidate budget per part per rotation.

inputs.name: Corner Mode
inputs.desc: 0 BottomLeft, 1 BottomRight, 2 TopLeft, 3 TopRight.

inputs.name: Variant
inputs.desc: 0 V506 (default, recommended), 2 V2 freeform (recommended; V506  delegates to this engine). 1 V1 polyline and 3 V3 adaptive  non-convex are RETAINED FOR REPRODUCIBILITY ONLY: the 2026-06-05  --packbench benchmark measured V1 at 44.6% fill with 9 overlap  pairs and 3166 ms, and V3 at 21/24 placed (dominated by V2's  24/24 at the same fill). Prefer 0 or 2 for new work. See  outputs/2026-06-05/keep_or_cut/PACKING_BENCHMARK.md.

inputs.name: Boundary Mode
inputs.desc: 0 = off (geometric only).  1 = boundary-aware bias: parts with edges matching the sheet  outline / hole edges are placed first AND auto-rotated to align  with the matched boundary tangent; all candidate sources (boundary  anchors + interior grid) used.  2 = strict two-phase ring/interior: boundary-worthy parts use only  boundary-anchor candidates (true ring), then non-boundary parts  fill the interior. Falls back to all candidates if a phase is  saturated.  3 = uniform curve division: divide each boundary curve by arc  length, place each part at its assigned position with longest  edge tangent to the curve. Most predictable ring layout. Min  Boundary Affinity is ignored in this mode.  V506 only — other variants ignore.

inputs.name: Min Boundary Affinity
inputs.desc: Edge-match score at or above which an edge is considered  boundary-worthy. Range [0, 1]; default 0.5. Only applies when  Boundary Mode > 0.

inputs.name: Discretization Tolerance
inputs.desc: ToPolyline tolerance for both sheet boundaries and part curves.  Set to a positive value to control polyline density independently  of the geometric Tolerance. Default -1 (means: use Tolerance).  Lower = finer polylines, more detail captured but more matching  work. Higher = coarser, faster, but may miss small features.

inputs.name: Trim Tolerance
inputs.desc: Maximum part-to-part overlap depth (in document units) allowed  during placement. After all parts are placed, overlapping pairs  are boolean-differenced — the EARLIER-placed part wins, the  later-placed part loses material at the contact. Sheet outline  and holes are NEVER trimmed (only part-to-part collisions).  0 = trim off (strict no-overlap; THIS IS THE DEFAULT, so packed  parts never overlap out of the box). Set > 0 to allow  overlap-then-trim (the earlier-placed part wins); for meter-scale  shared-contact masonry coursing try 0.003–0.01. Most useful with  Boundary Mode > 0 where parts get pushed close together along the  boundary; the trim cleans the contacts.

outputs.name: Packed Curves
outputs.desc: Placed part curves.

outputs.name: Transforms
outputs.desc: Placement transforms applied to each source curve.

outputs.name: Source Indices
outputs.desc: Original input curve index for each packed curve.

outputs.name: Sheet Indices
outputs.desc: Sheet index used for each packed curve.

outputs.name: Unplaced
outputs.desc: Curves that could not be placed.

outputs.name: Failure Reasons
outputs.desc: Reason for each unplaced curve.

outputs.name: Sheet Preview
outputs.desc: Outer sheet and hole preview curves.

outputs.name: Report
outputs.desc: Packing report.

outputs.name: Variant Used
outputs.desc: Which variant actually ran (echoes the requested Variant input).

outputs.name: Trimmed Curves
outputs.desc: Per-part post-trim curves. Same length as Packed Curves. When  Trim Tolerance == 0, this output is empty. When > 0, each  entry is either the original packed curve (no trim happened)  or the boolean-difference result from being trimmed by an  earlier-placed neighbor.

outputs.name: Trim Adjacency
outputs.desc: DataTree per packed part: branch i lists the SOURCE indices  of earlier-placed parts that trimmed Trimmed Curves[i]. Empty  branches indicate parts that were not trimmed.

name: Packed-Pile Stability
description: Geometric stability proxy for a 3D packed pile. A stone is  marked stable when its centre of mass either rests inside  its own footprint on the floor, or lies inside the union of  the XY footprints of the stones it rests on. Quick check;  for full RBE physics use Frahan Masonry Stability (RBE). Stability per Heyman 1966 limit state.

algorithm.title: Limit-state CoM-over-support
algorithm.citation: Heyman, J. (1966), The Stone Skeleton, Int. J. Solids Struct. 2(2):249-279

Physically settles an already-placed pack of stone meshes into real 3D contact  with a Bullet rigid-body simulation (convex-decomposition collision, gravity,  friction). Compose after any 3D packer to turn a heightmap/proxy placement into a  settled, stable, non-interpenetrating pile of real geometry. Bullet backend  (better than Kangaroo for stacking); needs libbulletc.dll beside the .gha. [Zhuang et al. 2024]

name: Boundary Rail Index
description: Diagnostic component. Build a boundary-rail index from one or  more boundary curves; each curve is sliding-window-sampled into  (length, tangent angle, curvature) buckets and stored as a  BoundaryIntervalInfo. The unified Frahan Sheet Pack now builds  this index internally when Boundary Mode is on; this standalone  component is kept for index inspection and ad-hoc analysis. Frahan-original method.

inputs.desc: Per-boundary zone bucket. Used to group boundaries (sheets, regions).  If shorter than the boundary list, the last value is repeated.  Defaults to all-zeros if omitted.

inputs.name: Step Length
inputs.desc: Sliding-window step (model units).

inputs.name: Curvature Bucket Size
inputs.desc: EdgeKey curvature bucket size (1 / radius).

outputs.name: Index
outputs.desc: Populated BoundaryRailIndex<BoundaryIntervalInfo> (opaque).

outputs.name: Key Count
outputs.desc: Distinct EdgeKey buckets in the index.

outputs.name: Known Zones
outputs.desc: Distinct zone buckets observed.

outputs.name: Report

The unified Frahan Sheet Pack now  matches internally when Boundary Mode is on; use this component  to inspect scores externally. Frahan-original method.

name: Algebraic Convex Polyhedron
description: Build a convex polyhedron from N half-space inequalities  Nx*x + Ny*y + Nz*z <= b (Zhang 2024 parity, synthesis I14).  Each parallel-list row defines one face's outward normal  and offset. Returns a triangulated Rhino Mesh. Frahan-original method.

algorithm.title: Half-space intersection convex polyhedron
algorithm.citation: Frahan-original

inputs.name: B
inputs.desc: Right-hand side b per inequality.

inputs.name: Nx
inputs.desc: Outward-normal X per inequality.

inputs.name: Ny
inputs.desc: Outward-normal Y per inequality.

inputs.name: Nz
inputs.desc: Outward-normal Z per inequality.

name: Bed Block Layout
description: Lay marketable dimension blocks (catalogue) into the intact layers between fracture beds,  cut along the beds. Inputs the bench box + the kriged bed surfaces; builds one layer per  inter-bed gap (Oblique = full bed spacing / bed-following; off = flat dip-safe envelope) and  tiles each layer with the block catalogue under a cost-to-volume objective. Volume Weight W  sweeps the plan: 0 = max cost (fewer big high-value blocks), ~500 = balanced, large = max  volume (fill). Outputs the blocks + net value + recovered volume. Reproduces the example-08  Botticino marble study. Facade over Core CatalogueBlockLayout.

algorithm.title: Cost/volume dimension-block catalogue layout, per bed-bounded layer, exact guillotine tiling
algorithm.citation: Elkarmoty et al. 2020 (block recovery on bedded stone); guillotine cutting stock (Gilmore & Gomory 1965)

inputs.name: Bench
inputs.desc: Bench bounding box (m). The XY footprint + Z range to lay blocks in.

inputs.name: Bed Surfaces
inputs.desc: Fracture bed surfaces (from GPR Fracture Surfaces 3D). One layer is built per gap between  consecutive beds (and bench top/bottom).

inputs.name: Volume Weight
inputs.desc: Cost-to-volume objective weight ($/m3 added to each block's price). 0 = max COST (fewer big  high-value blocks, lower volume, higher net); ~500 = balanced; large (e.g. 3000) = max VOLUME  (fill the layers). Default 0.

inputs.name: Oblique
inputs.desc: TRUE (default) = bed-following: each layer is as thick as the full bed spacing (recovers the  dip wedge; needs georeferenced sloped cuts to execute). FALSE = flat dip-safe envelope (top =  deepest point of the upper bed, bottom = shallowest of the lower bed; fabricable on any gangsaw  today, but the wedges are waste).

inputs.name: Cut Cost
inputs.desc: Diamond-saw cost (USD/m2 of sawn block face). Default 200.

inputs.name: Keep-out

outputs.name: Blocks
outputs.desc: Placed dimension blocks. With Oblique on these are bed-bounded HEXAHEDRA: each block's top  face rides the upper bed and its bottom face rides the lower bed (sheared to the dip), so no  block crosses a fracture and the layout follows the real bed dip. Oblique off = flat boxes.

name: Fracture Block Pack
description: Pack fixed-size dimension blocks into each fracture-bounded slab (bin): tree-pack  coarse subdivision of the AABB + irregular-boundary fit to the slab mesh. Reports  per-bin yield. Managed.

inputs.name: Container Meshes
inputs.desc: Fracture-bounded slab meshes (closed). Each is one BIN. From the split mesh bench /  fracture surfaces.

inputs.name: Block Length
inputs.desc: Dimension-block length (m).

inputs.name: Block Width
inputs.desc: Dimension-block width (m).

inputs.name: Block Height
inputs.desc: Dimension-block height (m).

inputs.name: Kerf
inputs.desc: Saw-cut gap between blocks (m).

inputs.name: Fracture Clearance
inputs.desc: Extra inward margin (m) every block must keep from the fracture boundary. Set it to the  fracture position sigma (GPR Fracture Surfaces 3D) for uncertainty-safe blocks. Default 0.

inputs.name: Run
inputs.desc: Compute the packing.

inputs.name: Uncertainty Safe
inputs.desc: Toggle the deep-fracture safety allowance. FALSE = geometric yield (clearance ignored, the  optimistic number). TRUE = enforce the Fracture Clearance (wire it to the fracture sigma  from GPR Fracture Surfaces 3D) so no block sits within the measured GPR uncertainty of a  fracture -> uncertainty-safe yield. Default false.

inputs.name: Packer
inputs.desc: Packing strategy. 0 = fixed axis grid. 1 = best-of (6 orientations x grid phase). 2 =  combined multi-size on a global grid. 3 = VOXEL-DLBF: per-block deepest-bottom-left-first  placement on a lattice (each block lands independently, conforming to the wavy boundary --  adopted after a head-to-head where a Mosch-style voxel greedy beat the global grid). 4 =  VOXEL-DLBF + multi-size (max-yield mesh-bench algorithm, DEFAULT): per-block placement plus  the 1.0/0.66/0.5 marketable fill ladder, strict 8-corner irregular fit + kerf. Tops the  head-to-head vs Kim forest and the Mosch-style greedy, but is NOT guaranteed saw-separable.  5 = GUILLOTINE multi-size (MANUFACTURABLE): recursive full-span 3D guillotine so every block  is separable by edge-to-edge saw cuts; reports cutting-surface-area + cut count (Jalalian  I11 / saw-path cost). Trades a little yield for full manufacturability.

outputs.name: Blocks
outputs.desc: Placed dimension-block meshes (closed boxes).

outputs.name: Bin Index
outputs.desc: Container/bin index of each placed block.

outputs.name: Block Count
outputs.desc: Blocks placed per bin.

outputs.name: Recovered Volume
outputs.desc: Recovered block volume per bin (m^3).

outputs.name: Yield
outputs.desc: Recovered / intact volume per bin (0..1).

outputs.name: Report
outputs.desc: Per-bin yield summary.

name: Fracture Bounded Slabs
description: Cut a bench box into the closed inter-bed SLABS that FOLLOW the kriged fracture surfaces.  The beds are single-valued depth surfaces, so each slab is built by stitching the sampled  height fields of two consecutive beds (height-field stitch, no CGAL boolean): one slab per  gap between consecutive beds and the bench top/bottom. Each slab follows the wavy beds, so a  block packed inside it never crosses a fracture. Feed the slabs into Fracture Block Pack  (packer 5, staged guillotine) -> the paper's manufacturable bed-following layout.

algorithm.title: Fracture-bounded slabs by height-field stitch between single-valued kriged bed surfaces
algorithm.citation: ordinary-kriging bed surfaces (Cressie 1993); guillotine bed-cut sequence (Gilmore and Gomory 1965)

inputs.name: Bench
inputs.desc: Bench bounding box (m). XY footprint + the Z range to slab.

inputs.name: Bed Surfaces
inputs.desc: Kriged fracture bed surfaces (from GPR Fracture Surfaces 3D). Single-valued depth surfaces;  one slab is built per gap between consecutive beds.

inputs.name: Grid Res
inputs.desc: Stitch grid resolution along the longer footprint axis (the other axis scales to keep cells  near-square). Higher = finer wavy-bed fidelity. Default 26.

inputs.name: Keep-out
inputs.desc: Inward Z margin (m) kept from each bed (the GPR position keep-out). Default 0.

outputs.name: Slabs
outputs.desc: The closed fracture-bounded slab meshes, one per inter-bed layer (shallow -> deep). Feed into  Fracture Block Pack > Container Meshes.

outputs.name: Thickness
outputs.desc: Mean thickness (m) of each slab, aligned to Slabs.

outputs.name: Report
outputs.desc: Per-slab summary.

description: Cuts a convex quarry Slab into a list of smaller convex Slabs  by an orthogonal grid of fracture planes. Output flows into  Ashlar Pack. Frahan-original method. Selection: convex pieces  -> By CoACD; plane-bounded cuts -> By Mesh (CGAL); cell  partition -> By Voronoi.

name: Quarry Decompose By CoACD
description: Decomposes a quarry mesh into nearly-convex blocks via  CoACD (Wei et al, SIGGRAPH 2022). Concavity-driven — block  count and shape come from the input geometry, not a user  grid. Use when the goal is approximate convex pieces for  downstream packing or collision physics. Implements Collision-Aware Approximate Convex Decomposition (Wei 2022).  Selection: convex pieces -> By CoACD; plane-bounded cuts -> By Mesh (CGAL); cell partition -> By Voronoi.

algorithm.title: Collision-Aware Approximate Convex Decomposition
algorithm.citation: Wei, Liu, Wang et al. 2022, Approximate Convex Decomposition for 3D Meshes with Collision-Aware Concavity and Tree Search, SIGGRAPH 2022

inputs.name: Quarry
inputs.desc: Quarry mesh. Must be 2-manifold for the lightweight CoACD  build (no OpenVDB preprocess); pre-clean with Mesh Repair  (CGAL) if needed.

inputs.name: Threshold
inputs.desc: Concavity threshold. Lower = more pieces, tighter fit.  Default 0.05.

inputs.name: Real Metric
inputs.desc: True = treat Threshold as metres rather than normalized  [0..1] units. Recommended for statue-scale input.

inputs.name: Max Pieces
inputs.desc: Cap on output piece count. -1 = unlimited.

inputs.name: Seed
inputs.desc: RNG seed for reproducibility.

inputs.name: Run
inputs.desc: Set true to compute. Decomposition is heavy.

outputs.name: Blocks
outputs.desc: One nearly-convex mesh per piece.

outputs.name: Count
outputs.desc: Number of pieces.

outputs.name: Available
outputs.desc: True iff frahan_coacd.dll is loadable.

outputs.name: Report
outputs.desc: Diagnostic report.

name: Quarry Decompose By Mesh (CGAL)
description: Decomposes a (possibly non-convex) quarry mesh into blocks  by intersecting it against a 3D grid of box cells via CGAL.  Empty cells are dropped automatically. Use this when the  plane-based Quarry Decompose does not apply because the  quarry mesh is not convex. Implements CGAL PMP corefinement.  Selection: convex pieces -> By CoACD; plane-bounded cuts -> By Mesh (CGAL); cell partition -> By Voronoi.

algorithm.title: CGAL Polygon Mesh Processing corefinement
algorithm.citation: CGAL Polygon Mesh Processing (corefine_and_compute, EPECK/EPICK hybrid kernel)

inputs.name: Quarry
inputs.desc: Quarry mesh. Must be closed and manifold (run Mesh Repair  (CGAL) upstream if in doubt). Need not be convex.

inputs.name: Grid Box
inputs.desc: Oriented box that defines the grid extent + orientation.  If empty (Box.Empty / Box.Unset), the world-aligned bounding  box of the Quarry mesh is used.

inputs.name: nX
inputs.desc: Grid divisions along the box's local +X axis (>= 1).

inputs.name: nY
inputs.desc: Grid divisions along the box's local +Y axis (>= 1).

inputs.name: nZ
inputs.desc: Grid divisions along the box's local +Z axis (>= 1).

inputs.name: Hybrid Kernel
inputs.desc: True (default) = HYBRID kernel for robustness on every  cell intersection. False = EPICK only (fastest).

inputs.name: Run
inputs.desc: Set true to compute. Cost scales with nX*nY*nZ CGAL calls.

outputs.name: Blocks
outputs.desc: One mesh per non-empty grid cell intersection (Quarry ∩ cell).

outputs.name: Cell Index
outputs.desc: Flat (i + j*nX + k*nX*nY) cell index for each output block,  parallel to the Blocks list. Lets the caller correlate  outputs with their originating cell.

outputs.name: Backend
outputs.desc: Which kernel ran on the most recent cell.

outputs.name: Available
outputs.desc: True iff the CGAL native shim is loadable.

outputs.name: Report
outputs.desc: Diagnostic report (cells visited / kept / dropped, runtime).

name: Quarry Decompose By Tet
description: Decomposes a quarry mesh into tetrahedra via Geogram.  Fine-grained, fracture-pattern style. Requires the  Geogram shim to be built with GEOGRAM_WITH_TETGEN=ON  (off by default — TetGen is non-commercial-use). When  off, the component surfaces a clear error and produces no  blocks; use Quarry Decompose By CoACD instead. Implements Geogram tetrahedralisation (Lévy, Geogram v1.9.9).

algorithm.title: Geogram tetrahedralisation
algorithm.citation: Lévy, B. Geogram v1.9.9 (GEO::mesh_tetrahedralize), BSD-3

inputs.name: Quarry
inputs.desc: Closed manifold quarry mesh.

inputs.name: Preprocess
inputs.desc: Run mesh preprocess (manifold-isation, hole fill) inside  Geogram before tetrahedralizing.

inputs.name: Refine
inputs.desc: Refine the tet mesh via Delaunay refinement after the  initial tetrahedralization. Increases tet count.

inputs.name: Quality
inputs.desc: Tet quality bound for refinement (radius-edge ratio).  Default 1.4. Lower is stricter / more tets.

inputs.name: Run
inputs.desc: Set true to compute.

outputs.name: Tets
outputs.desc: One closed tetrahedron mesh per output cell.

outputs.name: Count
outputs.desc: Number of tets.

outputs.name: Available
outputs.desc: True iff frahan_geogram.dll is loadable.

outputs.name: Report
outputs.desc: Diagnostic report. Reports the TetGen-disabled state when  applicable.

name: Quarry Decompose By Voronoi
description: Decomposes a (possibly non-convex) quarry mesh into solid  Voronoi blocks. Seeds are sampled inside the quarry and  Lloyd-relaxed for a more uniform cell-area distribution.  Each cell is then CGAL-intersected against the quarry  for the final block geometry. Realistic stone-fracturing  look; seed count + relaxation iterations are user dials. Implements restricted Voronoi + Lloyd relaxation (Geogram; Lloyd 1982).  Selection: convex pieces -> By CoACD; plane-bounded cuts -> By Mesh (CGAL); cell partition -> By Voronoi.

algorithm.title: Restricted Voronoi diagram
algorithm.citation: Lévy, B. Geogram v1.9.9 restricted Voronoi, BSD-3

inputs.name: Quarry
inputs.desc: Closed manifold quarry mesh.

inputs.name: Seed Count
inputs.desc: Number of Voronoi seeds = number of output blocks.  Default 30. Typical 20–200 for masonry-scale work.

inputs.name: Lloyd Iters
inputs.desc: Lloyd-relaxation iterations on the interior seeds. 0 =  raw rejection-sampled seeds; 5–10 = visibly more uniform.

inputs.name: Seed
inputs.desc: RNG seed for reproducibility. Default 1.

inputs.name: Hybrid Kernel
inputs.desc: True (default) = CGAL HYBRID kernel for the cell ×  quarry intersection. False = EPICK only (faster, less robust).

inputs.name: Run
inputs.desc: Set true to compute.

outputs.name: Blocks
outputs.desc: One mesh per non-empty Voronoi cell ∩ quarry intersection.

outputs.name: Seeds
outputs.desc: The relaxed seed positions actually used (parallel to Blocks).

outputs.name: Available
outputs.desc: True iff CGAL shim is loadable.

outputs.name: Report
outputs.desc: Diagnostic report.

name: Slab Cut By Fractures
description: Cuts a list of Slabs by a list of oriented fracture planes.  Each Rhino Plane is interpreted as an infinite plane (Origin, Normal).  Output Slabs carry the input-list parent index so callers can  track 'this fragment came from quarry block #N'.  Managed path is Frahan-original; opt-in CGAL backend uses CGAL PMP booleans (CGAL_PMP).

algorithm.title: Convex polyhedron half-space clipping (managed)
algorithm.citation: Frahan-original

inputs.name: Mesh
inputs.desc: Convex meshes to cut. Standard Rhino mesh wires; the cutter  converts to its internal Slab DTO automatically. Multi-shell  meshes should be split with Mesh Shell Split first.

inputs.name: Plane
inputs.desc: Oriented infinite fracture planes. Accepts the Frahan  FracturePlane DTO (from any *Fracture Planes generator) OR a  Rhino Plane (origin + normal). The two are interchangeable.

inputs.name: Eps
inputs.desc: Vertex-classification tolerance. Default 1e-9 works for most  metric inputs; raise to 1e-6 for non-metric or noisy meshes.

inputs.name: Use CGAL
inputs.desc: Backend. False (default) = managed convex SlabCutter (fast, but  convex-only and explodes combinatorially on large slabs with many  planes). True = route the cut through the CGAL boolean kernel  (CgalMeshBoolean): robust on non-convex / large slabs. CGAL path  returns meshes (the Slab output is empty). Falls back to managed  if the CGAL shim is not loaded.

outputs.name: Slab
outputs.desc: Output Slabs after cutting.

outputs.name: Parent
outputs.desc: Per-output parent index (0-based) into the input Slab list.

outputs.name: TotalVolume
outputs.desc: Sum of signed volumes of all output Slabs (sanity check).

outputs.name: Count
outputs.desc: Number of resulting Slabs.

outputs.name: Mesh
outputs.desc: Output Slabs as Rhino Meshes (parallel to the Slab list). Wire  into native components (Move, Bake, Boolean, Volume, etc.).

name: Slab Cut By Tool Mesh (CGAL)
description: Cuts a slab/block mesh by an arbitrary tool mesh via CGAL  exact-predicate booleans. Outputs the outside half  (slab − tool), the inside half (slab ∩ tool), or both.  Use this for non-convex slabs or curved/sculpted fracture  tools where the plane-based cutter does not apply.  Implements CGAL PMP corefinement booleans (CGAL_PMP).

algorithm.title: Exact-predicate mesh-mesh CSG via corefinement
algorithm.citation: CGAL Polygon Mesh Processing corefine_and_compute_difference/intersection (EPICK+EPECK Hybrid kernel)

inputs.name: Slab
inputs.desc: Slab/block mesh to cut. Must be closed and manifold for  predictable output (run Mesh Repair (CGAL) upstream if in  doubt).

inputs.name: Tool
inputs.desc: Tool mesh used as the cutter. Closed manifold mesh.

inputs.name: Mode
inputs.desc: 0 = Outside only (slab − tool).  1 = Inside only  (slab ∩ tool).  2 = Both halves (default).

inputs.name: Hybrid Kernel
inputs.desc: True (default) = HYBRID — EPICK storage + EPECK intersection  construction. Robust on near-tangent contacts and multi-cut  chains at a 2–5x speed cost.  False = EPICK only — fastest, fine for well-conditioned inputs.

inputs.name: Run
inputs.desc: Set true to compute. Heavy operation on large inputs.

outputs.name: Outside
outputs.desc: Slab − Tool. The portion of the slab outside the tool.  Empty mesh when the tool fully contains the slab.

outputs.name: Inside
outputs.desc: Slab ∩ Tool. The portion of the slab inside the tool.  Empty mesh when the tool misses the slab entirely.

outputs.name: Backend
outputs.desc: Which kernel ran: 'CGAL' or 'ManagedBsp' (BSP fallback).

outputs.name: Available
outputs.desc: True iff the CGAL native shim is loadable.

outputs.name: Report
outputs.desc: Diagnostic report (mode, kernel, vertex/face counts, runtime).

name: Slab From Mesh
description: Wraps a Rhino mesh into a Slab DTO. Quads stay as quads.  Mesh must have at least 4 vertices and 4 faces. Slab assumes  the input is CONVEX; convexity is not verified here.

inputs.name: Mesh
inputs.desc: Rhino mesh defining a CONVEX polyhedral slab. Quads are  preserved as quads; triangles stay as triangles.

outputs.name: Slab
outputs.desc: Slab DTO. Wire into Slab Cut By Fractures or downstream masonry.

outputs.name: Mesh
outputs.desc: Slab as a Rhino Mesh (re-emitted). Identical geometry to the  input Mesh but fan-triangulated from each polygonal face.

name: Slab Yield Optimizer
description: Pick the best SlabPlan (axis + thickness) for one block.  Enumerates three axis-aligned candidates at the given  thickness; score = yield - conflictPenalty * crackConflicts.

algorithm.title: Per-block slab-plan yield maximisation
algorithm.citation: Frahan-original spec 09 section 2 conflict-penalised yield score

inputs.name: Block
inputs.desc: Convex block mesh.

inputs.name: Fracture Planes
inputs.desc: Optional List<FracturePlane>. Wire from Frahan Mesh → Fracture Planes.

inputs.name: Thickness (m)
inputs.desc: Target slab thickness.

inputs.name: Kerf (m)
inputs.desc: Saw kerf.

inputs.name: Conflict Penalty
inputs.desc: Score penalty per aligned fracture inside the block.

inputs.name: Alignment Tol (deg)
inputs.desc: Normal-axis alignment tolerance for conflict detection.

outputs.name: Best Plan
outputs.desc: SlabPlan with the highest score.

outputs.name: Slab Count
outputs.desc: Slabs the block produces under this plan.

outputs.name: Yield Fraction
outputs.desc: slab_total_volume / block_volume.

outputs.name: Conflicts
outputs.desc: Crack conflicts counted.

outputs.name: Score
outputs.desc: Yield − penalty × conflicts.

outputs.name: Cut Planes
outputs.desc: FracturePlanes that materialise the winning plan (feed Slab Cut By Fractures).

name: Vertical Fracture Planes From Curves
description: Turn plan-view fracture trace curves (e.g. from Vector Fractures  Loader on a real fracture shapefile) into VERTICAL cutting planes  for Slab Cut By Fractures. Per Segment = a plane per polyline segment  (faithful to wiggly traces); off = one best-fit vertical plane per curve.

algorithm.title: Vertical plane per fracture trace
algorithm.citation: Frahan-original: plan-view trace -> vertical cutting plane (contains the trace direction + Z)

inputs.name: Curves
inputs.desc: Fracture trace curves (plan-view).

inputs.name: Per Segment
inputs.desc: TRUE = one vertical plane per polyline segment (faithful to curved traces);  FALSE (default) = one best-fit vertical plane per curve (start->end direction).

outputs.name: Planes
outputs.desc: Vertical fracture planes (feed Slab Cut By Fractures 'Plane').

outputs.name: Count
outputs.desc: Number of planes produced.

name: Adaptive Block Match 3D
description: 3D sibling of Component C. Given two scanned stone blocks where  one is oversized for its slot, find the best mating pose via  Block Pair Match 3D, then carve a minimum volume from the candidate  (CGAL/Geogram boolean diff) to make it fit. Mirrors the Clifford- McGee 2017 Cyclopean Cannibalism overlap-then-carve discipline and  the UCL Devadass 2025 minimum-machining principle.

algorithm.title: Block Pair Match 3D
algorithm.citation: See BlockPairMatch3DComponent for B3D pipeline references

inputs.name: Slot
inputs.desc: Target slot mesh (the neighbour the candidate must mate against).

inputs.name: Candidate
inputs.desc: Oversized candidate mesh to trim.

inputs.name: Trim Style
inputs.desc: 0=Planar cut (single plane; saw / stone-friendly, default per UCL SS2.7).  1=Polyline / free-form sculpt (router / wood / swarf-machining).

inputs.name: Max Trim Volume Ratio
inputs.desc: Trim volume budget as a fraction of candidate volume.  Component rejects placement if trim would exceed this. Default 0.1 (10 %).

inputs.name: Match Tolerance
inputs.desc: Post-trim joint Hausdorff tolerance (mm).

outputs.name: Placed Block
outputs.desc: Trimmed and placed candidate mesh.

outputs.name: Trim Diff
outputs.desc: The carved-away material (CGAL boolean difference result).

outputs.name: Trim Volume
outputs.desc: Volume carved away (cubic mm).

outputs.name: Trim Ratio
outputs.desc: Trim volume as fraction of source candidate volume.

outputs.name: Joint Residual
outputs.desc: Post-trim Hausdorff residual at the matched face pair (mm).

outputs.name: Remarks
outputs.desc: Diagnostic notes -- which face-pair matched, whether the trim  stayed inside the volume budget, etc.

name: Block Chain Along Thrust Line
description: Bidirectional 3D walker placing scanned stones along a designer- supplied thrust line (catenary, parabola, spline). One stone per  station; Block Pair Match 3D is the per-station atomic call.  Strategy=Pareto runs NSGA-II on three UCL-paper objectives  (angle deviation / Cg deviation / endpoint deviation). The  canonical implementation of the UCL Bartlett 18-stone arch  workflow (em_3d_chain_ucl_bartlett HITL card-set).

algorithm.title: Bidirectional rail walker
algorithm.citation: Frahan-original sequential placement state machine

inputs.name: Stone Inventory
inputs.desc: Filtered list of scanned-stone meshes. Apply area + internal-angle  filters upstream (UCL Devadass 2025 SS2.4) before wiring here.

inputs.name: Thrust Curve
inputs.desc: Designer-supplied thrust line (catenary, parabola, spline). The walker  places one stone per station evaluated along this curve.

inputs.name: Strategy
inputs.desc: 0=Greedy, 1=Beam (default), 2=Pareto (NSGA-II three-objective).

inputs.name: Direction
inputs.desc: 0=Forward, 1=Backward, 2=Bidirectional (default, OQ1-locked 2026-05-31).

inputs.name: Match Tolerance
inputs.desc: Per-station Hausdorff match tolerance (mm).

inputs.name: Beam Width
inputs.desc: Beam width (Strategy=Beam) or population size (Strategy=Pareto).

outputs.name: Placed Stones
outputs.desc: Per-station placed stone meshes (transformed).

outputs.name: Stone Indices
outputs.desc: Per-station inventory index used.

outputs.name: Per-Station Residual
outputs.desc: Per-station match residual (mm).

outputs.name: Pareto Front
outputs.desc: Strategy=Pareto only: 3D points where (x=angle deviation,  y=Cg deviation, z=endpoint deviation) per solution.

outputs.name: Endpoint Deviation
outputs.desc: Final placed-chain endpoint distance from designed thrust-line endpoint (mm).

outputs.name: Remarks
outputs.desc: Per-station diagnostic notes + strategy convergence flags.

name: Block Pair Match 3D
description: First-cut matcher: VSA segmentation + plane-to-plane mating scored by sampled  Hausdorff distance. The full exhaustive face-pair search is a planned refinement.  For a practically-tested matcher use Stone-Cell Match (Λ)  (ETH1100 Lambda=0.194, card 27_07).  Atomic 3D edge-matching primitive: given two scanned stone meshes,  find the rigid 3D pose where their planar face patches mate.  VsaSegmenter -> face filtering -> per-pair PhaseCorrelator +  ConstrainedIcp3D refinement -> top-N candidates ranked by  patch-pair Hausdorff residual + match-length. Foundational  primitive for the 3D EdgeMatch family (Block Chain, Adaptive  Block Match, Template Block Match, Cyclopean Recipe Coursing). [Cohen-Steiner et al. 2004]

algorithm.title: Phase correlator FFT (3D)
algorithm.citation: Classical cross-correlation lag estimation

inputs.name: Block A
inputs.desc: First scanned-stone mesh. Closed manifold preferred; algorithm tolerates  open meshes but face-pair coverage may suffer.

inputs.name: Block B
inputs.desc: Second scanned-stone mesh. Same constraints as Block A.

inputs.name: Min Face Area
inputs.desc: Minimum face-patch area (mm^2) below which patches are dropped.  Default 15,000 mm^2 per UCL Devadass 2025 SS2.4.1 (stability filter).

inputs.name: Normal Merge Angle
inputs.desc: VSA segmenter's adjacent-normal merge angle threshold (radians).  Coarser = fewer larger patches. Default 0.35 rad (~20 deg).

inputs.name: Max Candidates
inputs.desc: Maximum number of MatchResult candidates to emit (sorted by residual ascending).

inputs.name: Match Tolerance
inputs.desc: Match residual cutoff (mm). Candidates with residual > this are rejected.

outputs.name: Transforms
outputs.desc: Per-candidate rigid 3D transform that places Block B against Block A.

outputs.name: Residuals
outputs.desc: Per-candidate Hausdorff residual on the matched patch pair (mm).

outputs.name: Match Areas
outputs.desc: Per-candidate area of the matched patch (square mm).

outputs.name: Remarks
outputs.desc: Per-candidate diagnostic notes (which face-pair matched, etc.)  plus rejection reasons if no candidate exceeds the tolerance.

name: Cyclopean Recipe Coursing
description: The bottom-up 3D peer with no 2D analog. Encodes the Clifford- McGee 2017 Cyclopean Cannibalism 8-step recipe verbatim. Inputs:  scanned rubble inventory + wall envelope + variable-thickness  back-plane + course height. Outputs: placed stones with  trapezoid/parallelogram/keystone recipe-step tags + Utah-detail  scribe curves per bed joint + dowel insertion vectors. The 3D  flagship bottom-up component, mirroring cyclopean masonry  principles per Libish 2026-05-31 directive.

algorithm.title: Largest 4-sided inscribed polygon
algorithm.citation: Clifford 2017 The Cannibal's Cookbook pp. 118-120 recursive algorithm

inputs.name: Rubble Inventory
inputs.desc: List of scanned-stone meshes (demolition rubble, quarry off-cuts).

inputs.name: Wall Envelope
inputs.desc: Primary wall Brep (front face). The form the recipe builds against.

inputs.name: Back Plane
inputs.desc: Variable-thickness back-plane offset Brep. The recipe's stones  extend from Wall Envelope to Back Plane; thickness varies by location.

inputs.name: Course Height
inputs.desc: Average course height (mm). Drives the number of horizontal rows. Default 400 mm.

inputs.name: Min Face Area
inputs.desc: VsaSegmenter min-face-area threshold (mm^2) for shape classification. Default 15,000 mm^2.

inputs.name: Strategy
inputs.desc: 0=Greedy recipe, 1=Pareto (NSGA-II three-objective: coverage / stability / variable-thickness fit). Default 0.

inputs.name: Allow Trim
inputs.desc: If true, calls Component C3D (Adaptive Block Match 3D) for the  overlap-then-carve recipe step 7. If false, stones placed without trim.

outputs.name: Placed Stones
outputs.desc: Per-stone placed and trimmed mesh (transformed into wall position).

outputs.name: Recipe Step
outputs.desc: Per-stone recipe-step tag: 'trapezoid_seed' / 'parallelogram_fill' / 'keystone_inverted_trapezoid'.

outputs.name: Utah Curves
outputs.desc: Per-joint Utah-detail scribe curves (the bed-joint signatures  carved into the course below). Empty if Allow Trim = false.

outputs.name: Dowel Positions
outputs.desc: Stainless-steel structural alignment dowel insertion points  (3-inch cord-drilled holes per Quarra Emanuel 9 + Cyclopean Cannibalism).

outputs.name: Dowel Vectors
outputs.desc: Per-stone dowel insertion vectors (vertical setting direction).

outputs.name: Stone Indices
outputs.desc: Per-stone inventory-index used (parallels Placed Stones).

outputs.name: Coverage
outputs.desc: Fraction of Wall Envelope's projected area covered by placed stones (0-1).

outputs.name: Remarks
outputs.desc: Per-stone diagnostic notes + recipe-rule-violation flags +  stability-check results.

name: EdgeMatch Options
description: Bundle the EdgeMatch solver's advanced AssemblyOptions flags  (assembly mode, scale-relative gates, partial sub-segment  matching, overlap resolve, Soft-ICP rim-contact refine, and the  WIP projection bootstrap) into one AssemblyOptions DTO. Wire into  EdgeMatch Solve's optional Opt input. Every input is optional and  defaults to the Core default, so an empty component emits the  default options (unchanged behaviour).

algorithm.title: Agglomerative pair-graph assembly
algorithm.citation: Frahan-original minimum-residual spanning tree

inputs.name: Agglomerative
inputs.desc: Assembly mode. FALSE (default) = FrameAnchored beam (2D Trencadís;  every existing canvas + test path). TRUE = Agglomerative pairwise  spanning-tree assembly for free 3D fragment reassembly.

inputs.name: Non-Crossing Max Gap
inputs.desc: Index-band bound for the monotone non-crossing DP. 0 (default) =  unbounded. Only consulted when Non-Crossing is on at the solver.

inputs.name: Phase Score Threshold
inputs.desc: Minimum phase-correlator similarity to accept a candidate pair.  Default 0.5 (the original hardcoded gate).

inputs.name: Residual Threshold Factor
inputs.desc: When > 0 the residual gate becomes factor * objectScale (bbox  diagonal), making acceptance scale-relative. 0 (default) keeps the  absolute Residual Threshold from the solver. Suggested ~0.01.

inputs.name: Emit Partials
inputs.desc: When TRUE the segmenters also emit shorter sub-windows so a long  edge can mate a short complementary edge. FALSE (default) =  candidate generation identical to before.

inputs.name: Partial Fractions
inputs.desc: Partial window lengths as fractions of each base segment span.  Default {0.5, 0.25}. Only consulted when Emit Partials is on.

inputs.name: Partial Stride Fraction
inputs.desc: Stride between consecutive partial windows as a fraction of the  window length. Default 1.0 (non-overlapping tiling). Only consulted  when Emit Partials is on.

inputs.name: Overlap Penalty
inputs.desc: When > 0 a candidate's score is penalised by penalty * overlap  area fraction, lowering overlapping placements. 0 (default) = no  penalty. A working value is ~1.0.

inputs.name: Edge Exclusivity
inputs.desc: When TRUE a placed panel's matched segment is consumed so two  pieces cannot snap to the same placed edge. FALSE (default) = a  segment can be reused (existing behaviour).

inputs.name: Resolve Overlap
inputs.desc: When TRUE the caller runs a post-solve 2D rigid depenetration  polish (translation only, anchor-locked) until pairwise overlap is  within tolerance. FALSE (default) = no polish.

inputs.name: Resolve Overlap Tolerance
inputs.desc: Target max pairwise overlap area as a fraction of the smaller  contour, for the Resolve Overlap polish. Default 0.001 (0.1%).

inputs.name: Resolve Overlap Iterations
inputs.desc: Max relaxation iterations for the Resolve Overlap polish.  Default 50.

inputs.name: Resolve Overlap Relaxation
inputs.desc: Per-iteration step factor in (0,1] for the Resolve Overlap polish.  Lower = stabler but slower. Default 0.5.

inputs.name: Soft-ICP Refine
inputs.desc: When TRUE the caller runs the Soft-ICP refiner after the solve to  pull open-mesh rims into contact with a non-penetration hinge.  FALSE (default) = no refine. Only the keys below are exposed; other  SoftIcpOptions fields keep their Core defaults.

inputs.name: Soft-ICP Tau0 Factor
inputs.desc: Initial CPD temperature tau0 = factor * (median rim spacing)^2.  Larger = softer / wider start. Default 4.0.

inputs.name: Soft-ICP Tau Anneal
inputs.desc: Geometric anneal factor applied to tau each iteration, in (0,1).  Default 0.8.

inputs.name: Soft-ICP Correspondence Radius Factor
inputs.desc: Contact correspondence radius = factor * (median rim spacing).  Neighbours beyond contribute zero weight. Default 3.0. 0 = no cutoff.

inputs.name: Soft-ICP Contact Weight
inputs.desc: Weight w_contact of the contact term. Default 1.0.

inputs.name: Soft-ICP Penetration Weight
inputs.desc: Hinge weight w_pen / lambda for the non-penetration term.  Default 1.0.

inputs.name: Soft-ICP Max Iterations
inputs.desc: Max outer EM iterations for the Soft-ICP refiner. Default 40.

inputs.name: Projection Bootstrap
inputs.desc: WIP / UNVERIFIED. When TRUE the caller bootstraps 3D candidate  pairs by per-facet 2D projection + lift (agglomerative 3D path  only). FALSE (default) = no projection bootstrap. Needs its own  HITL before any visual-correctness claim.

inputs.name: Projection Sample Spacing Factor
inputs.desc: Resample spacing for the projected 2D rim as a fraction of the  loop bbox diagonal. Default 0.02.

inputs.name: Projection Planarity Factor
inputs.desc: Planarity flag threshold for a projected rim as a fraction of the  loop bbox diagonal. Default 0.05.

inputs.name: Projection Verify Factor
inputs.desc: 3D verification gate for a lifted pair as a fraction of the  projected-rim scale. Default 0.12.

outputs.name: Options
outputs.desc: AssemblyOptions DTO bundling the advanced EdgeMatch flags. Wire  into EdgeMatch Solve's optional Opt input. When wired, the solver  copies these advanced fields onto the options it builds from its  simple inputs; the simple inputs keep owning the basic fields.

name: EdgeMatch Segments
description: Run the EdgeMatch boundary segmenter on one curve and expose  the per-segment polylines and signatures. Auto-dispatches between  the 2D planar segmenter and the 3D Frenet-invariant segmenter  based on the curve's best-fit planarity.

algorithm.title: Boundary segmenter
algorithm.citation: Frahan-original arc-length curvature/torsion signature

inputs.name: Curve
inputs.desc: Closed planar or spatial polyline-convertible curve.

inputs.name: Planarity Tolerance
inputs.desc: RMS planarity threshold (mm) deciding the 2D vs 3D path.

inputs.name: Sample Spacing
inputs.desc: Arc-length sample spacing (mm).

inputs.name: Break Angle
inputs.desc: Curvature break threshold in degrees per window.

inputs.name: Min Segment Length
inputs.desc: Below this chord length a segment is treated as noise.

inputs.name: Signature Bins
inputs.desc: Resampled signature length (power of 2 recommended).

outputs.name: Segments
outputs.desc: One polyline per detected segment.

outputs.name: Chord Lengths
outputs.desc: Per-segment chord length.

outputs.name: Total Turning
outputs.desc: Per-segment signed turning integral.

outputs.name: Sign
outputs.desc: +1 convex (relative to panel interior), -1 concave.

outputs.name: Turning Signatures
outputs.desc: One branch per segment; resampled signed-turning signal.

outputs.name: Curvature Signatures
outputs.desc: One branch per segment; |turning| for the planar path or discrete Frenet curvature for the 3D path.

outputs.name: Torsion Signatures
outputs.desc: One branch per segment; populated only when the curve takes the 3D path.

outputs.name: Mode
outputs.desc: Detected panel mode: Planar2D or Spatial3D.

outputs.name: Planarity RMS
outputs.desc: Best-fit plane RMS (mm) for the input curve.

name: EdgeMatch Solve
description: Edge-matching beam search for Trencadís shards or live-edge planks.  Anchors against a frame curve, places each candidate using ICP-refined  complementary-edge matches, and emits the placement transform set.

algorithm.title: Boundary segmenter
algorithm.citation: Frahan-original arc-length curvature/torsion signature

inputs.name: Frame
inputs.desc: Closed boundary curve. Anchored at the identity transform; shards match against it first.

inputs.name: Shards
inputs.desc: Closed shard / plank curves to place.

inputs.name: Substrate
inputs.desc: Optional curved substrate Brep. Only consulted by the 3D ICP path; pass nothing for flat assemblies.

inputs.name: Planarity Tolerance
inputs.desc: RMS planarity threshold (mm). Below this, panels take the 2D path.

inputs.name: Sample Spacing
inputs.desc: Arc-length sample spacing along each contour (mm).

inputs.name: Break Angle
inputs.desc: Curvature break-point threshold in degrees per window.

inputs.name: Min Segment Length
inputs.desc: Below this chord length, a segment is treated as noise and discarded.

inputs.name: Residual Threshold
inputs.desc: Maximum mean point-to-point ICP residual for an accepted match (mm).

inputs.name: Beam Width
inputs.desc: Number of concurrent beam states retained between iterations.

inputs.name: Max Iterations
inputs.desc: Maximum outer-loop iterations.

inputs.name: Run
inputs.desc: Execute the solver.

inputs.name: Non-Crossing
inputs.desc: Order-preserving rim correspondence. FALSE (default) = free  nearest-point ICP (unchanged behaviour). TRUE = monotone,  non-crossing point pairing between rims (OrderedBoundaryMatcher);  more robust on wiggly / noisy rims where free matching tangles.

inputs.name: Options
inputs.desc: Optional AssemblyOptions DTO from EdgeMatch Options. When wired,  its advanced flags (Mode, scale-relative gates, partial sub-segment  matching, overlap resolve, Soft-ICP refine, projection bootstrap)  override the defaults; the simple inputs above keep owning the basic  fields. Leave disconnected for unchanged behaviour.

outputs.name: Placed
outputs.desc: Shard contours transformed by their solved placements. Frame is included as Identity.

outputs.name: Transforms
outputs.desc: Per-panel rigid transform.

outputs.name: Ids
outputs.desc: Panel ids matching the Placed and Transforms order.

outputs.name: Modes
outputs.desc: Per-panel mode: Planar2D or Spatial3D.

outputs.name: Planarity RMS
outputs.desc: Per-panel best-fit plane RMS (mm).

outputs.name: Residuals
outputs.desc: Per-placement ICP residual. Length = placed shard count (frame excluded).

outputs.name: Total Residual
outputs.desc: Sum of per-placement residuals.

outputs.name: Report
outputs.desc: Human-readable summary of the solve.

name: Live Edge Classify
description: Classify a wood-offcut outline into LIVE (curvy, natural) edges and SAWN (straight, machine-cut) ends,  for live-edge flooring 2D edge matching. Robust to live-edge wiggles: the two longest straight runs are  taken as the sawn ends and the two arcs between them as the live edges.

algorithm.title: Live/sawn straight-run classifier
algorithm.citation: Frahan-original

inputs.name: Outline
inputs.desc: Closed offcut outline (one board).

outputs.name: Live edges
outputs.desc: The two LIVE (curvy) edges.

outputs.name: Sawn edges
outputs.desc: The two SAWN (straight) ends.

outputs.name: Corners
outputs.desc: The four detected corners.

outputs.name: Straightness
outputs.desc: Per-edge chord/arc-length (~1 = straight).

name: Live Edge Match
description: Assign a pool of offcuts to the staggered live-edge course slots and report the assignment plus per-board  scribe trim. Mode 0 = greedy, Mode 1 = Hungarian (global minimum total trim). No Outlines -> a demo pool.

algorithm.title: Scribe-trim cost matcher
algorithm.citation: Frahan-original

inputs.name: Outlines
inputs.desc: Offcut outline pool (closed curves). Empty -> demo pool.

inputs.name: Floor width
inputs.desc: Floor width along the courses.

inputs.name: Courses
inputs.desc: Number of courses (rows).

inputs.name: Course height
inputs.desc: Nominal course height.

inputs.name: Seed
inputs.desc: Deterministic seed.

inputs.name: Mode
inputs.desc: 0 = Greedy, 1 = Hungarian (global min-trim).

inputs.name: Run
inputs.desc: Set true to solve the assignment.

outputs.name: Pool index
outputs.desc: Offcut pool index assigned to each placed slot (placement order).

outputs.name: Course
outputs.desc: Course (row) of each placed slot.

outputs.name: Trim
outputs.desc: Mean scribe trim per placed board.

outputs.name: Mean trim
outputs.desc: Mean scribe trim over the floor.

outputs.name: Max trim
outputs.desc: Max scribe deviation over the floor.

outputs.name: Placed
outputs.desc: Number of boards placed.

outputs.name: Rivers
outputs.desc: The live-edge seams between courses.

name: Live Edge Stagger Layup
description: Lay a pool of wood offcuts into a staggered live-edge floor: live edges matched along the course as  continuous wavy seams, short sawn butt joints staggered brick-bond, each board scribe-trimmed to fit.  Mode 0 = greedy, Mode 1 = Hungarian (global min-trim). No Outlines -> a demo pool is synthesised.

algorithm.title: Brick-bond scribe layup
algorithm.citation: Frahan-original

inputs.name: Outlines
inputs.desc: Offcut outline pool (closed curves). Empty -> demo pool.

inputs.name: Floor width
inputs.desc: Floor width along the courses.

inputs.name: Courses
inputs.desc: Number of courses (rows).

inputs.name: Course height
inputs.desc: Nominal course height.

inputs.name: Seed
inputs.desc: Deterministic seed (river shapes + demo pool).

inputs.name: Mode
inputs.desc: 0 = Greedy, 1 = Hungarian (global min-trim).

inputs.name: Run
inputs.desc: Set true to lay the floor.

outputs.name: Boards
outputs.desc: Placed boards (vertex-coloured meshes).

outputs.name: Rivers
outputs.desc: The live-edge seams between courses.

outputs.name: Butt joints
outputs.desc: Staggered sawn butt joints.

outputs.name: Trim slivers
outputs.desc: Scribe-and-fill strips removed from each board.

outputs.name: Report
outputs.desc: Layup summary.

name: Live Edge Trim
description: Scribe a board's two live edges onto target seam curves (lower + upper rivers). Returns the trimmed  outline, the scribe-and-fill slivers, and the max trim depth. Leave a seam unconnected to keep that edge.

algorithm.title: Scribe-and-fill trim
algorithm.citation: Frahan-original; live-edge river gap-fill practice

inputs.name: Board
inputs.desc: Closed board outline (laid horizontally).

inputs.name: Lower seam
inputs.desc: Target seam for the bottom live edge.

inputs.name: Upper seam
inputs.desc: Target seam for the top live edge.

outputs.name: Trimmed
outputs.desc: The scribed (trimmed) board outline.

outputs.name: Slivers
outputs.desc: The scribe-and-fill strips removed (bottom + top).

outputs.name: Depth
outputs.desc: Max trim depth.

name: Mesh Template Match
description: Simple-component template matcher: given stock meshes + one  target template, find the stock whose OBB contains the template  with the lowest waste. Inspired by PolytopeSolutions'  MatchMeshTransformation but generalised for scanned-stone-vs- designed-template where the topology doesn't match. The simple  first-cut matcher for the cathedral / Vitruvian / fluidic  stone workflows -- reach for Template Block Match 3D when  production cost matters.

algorithm.title: OBB containment + best-fit alignment
algorithm.citation: PrincipalAxes3d via covariance / Jacobi eigendecomposition

inputs.name: Stock Meshes
inputs.desc: List of stock stone meshes to search (scanned quarry blocks, off-cuts, etc.).

inputs.name: Template Mesh
inputs.desc: The designed template mesh to fit (e.g. one voussoir, one column drum, one wall block).

inputs.name: Margin
inputs.desc: Safety margin (mm) the template's OBB must clear within the stock's OBB. Default 5 mm.

inputs.name: Min Yield
inputs.desc: Minimum yield ratio (template_vol / stock_vol) for a feasible match. Default 0.4 (40 %).

outputs.name: Matched Index
outputs.desc: Index of the picked stock mesh (-1 if none feasible).

outputs.name: Transformation
outputs.desc: Rigid transform that aligns the template into the picked stock's OBB  (Plane.PlaneToPlane from template OBB to stock OBB).

outputs.name: Yield Ratio
outputs.desc: Achieved yield ratio (template_vol / stock_vol) for the picked stock. 0 if no match.

outputs.name: Carving Volume
outputs.desc: Estimated material to carve away (stock_vol - template_vol) in mm^3.

outputs.name: Remarks
outputs.desc: Per-candidate diagnostic notes -- feasible / infeasible reasons.

name: Soft ICP 3D
description: Refine the poses of 3D fragment meshes so their rims come into  CONTACT while their solids do not interpenetrate. EM weighted- Kabsch over CPD soft correspondence + smooth penetration hinge.  The standalone primitive that EdgeMatch Solve / Kintsugi / Trencadis  / Cyclopean Recipe / Voussoir Match all invoke internally; surface  on the canvas to chain into any custom workflow. [Myronenko & Song 2010]

algorithm.title: Penetration hinge (smooth non-penetration)
algorithm.citation: Mesh.IsPointInside inside-test + smooth quadratic hinge per SettleContactComponent / OverlapResolver2D

inputs.name: Fragments
inputs.desc: Placed fragment meshes at their CURRENT pose. Rim samples are taken  from each mesh's naked-edge / boundary loops automatically.

inputs.name: Anchor Index
inputs.desc: Index of the fragment to PIN (its pose stays fixed; others move  relative to it). Default 0. Set -1 to anchor none (free-floating refine).

inputs.name: Tau0 Factor
inputs.desc: Initial CPD temperature factor (tau0 = T0 * (median rim spacing)^2).  Larger = softer / wider correspondence. Default 4.0.

inputs.name: Tau Anneal
inputs.desc: Geometric anneal factor (0,1) applied to tau each outer iter. Default 0.8.

inputs.name: Outlier Weight
inputs.desc: Uniform-outlier pseudo-weight in the softmax denominator.  Default 0.01.

inputs.name: Max Iterations
inputs.desc: Maximum EM outer iterations. Default 40.

inputs.name: Sample Spacing
inputs.desc: Arc-length sample spacing along naked edges (mm). Default 1.0 mm;  0 = auto from the assembly bbox.

outputs.name: Delta
outputs.desc: Per-fragment pose increment (left-composed onto the input pose).  Anchored fragments emit Transform.Identity.

outputs.name: Refined Fragments
outputs.desc: Per-fragment refined mesh (input mesh with Delta applied).

outputs.name: Mean Rim Gap
outputs.desc: Mean nearest-neighbour rim gap across all matched neighbour rims  (global Report metric).

outputs.name: Max Penetration
outputs.desc: Maximum penetration depth across all fragment pairs  (should be <= 0 or very small after a successful refine).

outputs.name: Contact Samples
outputs.desc: Count of rim samples on mating interfaces (drives the contact term).

outputs.name: Iterations
outputs.desc: Outer EM iterations actually run.

outputs.name: Remarks
outputs.desc: Per-fragment diagnostic notes + convergence flags.

name: Template Block Match 3D
description: 3D sibling of Component D. Designer supplies an N-cell 3D template  (voussoir layout). Inventory is a list of scanned stones.  Hungarian bipartite assignment solves the optimal one-to-one  mapping (stone -> cell) minimising total trim volume + post-trim  residual. Cost matrix per-cell evaluated via Component C3D in  dry-run mode. Same algorithm as Voussoir Stone Matcher (shared  HungarianAssigner.cs). [Kuhn 1955]

algorithm.title: Hungarian assignment
algorithm.citation: H.W. Kuhn 1955 Hungarian Method for the Assignment Problem; Jonker-Volgenant pivot

inputs.name: Template Cells
inputs.desc: List of designed cell meshes (voussoir layout). One mesh per cell.

inputs.name: Stone Inventory
inputs.desc: List of scanned-stone meshes.

inputs.name: Strategy
inputs.desc: 0=Greedy, 1=Hungarian (default; globally optimal), 2=Pareto (NSGA-II fallback for M*N > 40k).

inputs.name: Allow Trim
inputs.desc: If true, calls Component C3D to evaluate cost with minimal trim.  If false, only no-trim Block Pair Match 3D matches are considered.

inputs.name: Max Trim Volume Ratio
inputs.desc: Per-pair trim volume budget. Per-pair cost = infinity if exceeded.

inputs.name: Allow Empty
inputs.desc: If true, cells with no feasible inventory match remain unassigned (reported).  If false, fail loudly when any cell would remain empty.

outputs.name: Placed Stones
outputs.desc: Per-cell placed (and trimmed if applicable) stone meshes.

outputs.name: Cell Indices
outputs.desc: Per-cell index in the template (parallels Placed Stones).

outputs.name: Stone Indices
outputs.desc: Per-cell inventory index assigned (parallels Cell Indices).

outputs.name: Unassigned Cells
outputs.desc: Cell indices with no feasible inventory match (when Allow Empty=true).

outputs.name: Unused Stones
outputs.desc: Inventory indices not consumed in the assignment.

outputs.name: Total Cost
outputs.desc: Sum of per-cell costs across the assignment (Hungarian objective value).

outputs.name: Remarks
outputs.desc: Diagnostic notes -- strategy used, cost matrix size, rejected stones, etc.

name: Bench Monument Pack
description: Pack a MonumentInventory inside a fractured bench (BlockGraph)  using 24-rotation SO(3) sampling and greedy AABB placement  per cell. Monuments stay inside one cell — no fracture crossings. Frahan-original method.

algorithm.title: 24-orientation SO(3) sampling + greedy AABB packing
algorithm.citation: Frahan-original

inputs.name: Block Graph
inputs.desc: BlockGraph from Frahan Block Graph.

inputs.name: Inventory
inputs.desc: MonumentInventory.

inputs.name: Grid Stride (m)
inputs.desc: Candidate-origin sweep step.

outputs.name: Plan
outputs.desc: BenchMonumentPlan.

outputs.name: Placed Boxes
outputs.desc: Axis-aligned box per placement (visual).

outputs.name: Placed Ids
outputs.desc: Monument ids in placement order.

outputs.name: Orientation Index
outputs.desc: Rotation index (0..23) per placement.

outputs.name: Cell Ids
outputs.desc: Parent cell id per placement.

outputs.name: Unplaced Ids
outputs.desc: Monuments that did not fit.

outputs.name: Fill Ratio
outputs.desc: TotalPlacedVolume / BenchAabbVolume.

name: Fabrication Prep Report
description: Per-block weight (volume x density), centroid, and lift class  (hand <25 kg / two-person <50 / mechanical <2000 / crane) for the  crate + hoist plan. Assumes model units are metres. Default  density = 2700 kg/m3 (granite). Wire block meshes from Staggered  Block Decompose / Slab Cut.

inputs.name: Blocks
inputs.desc: Closed block meshes (e.g. from Staggered Block Decompose).

inputs.name: Density
inputs.desc: Stone density kg/m3 (default granite 2700).

inputs.name: Ids
inputs.desc: Optional per-block ids (parallel to Blocks).

outputs.name: Weights
outputs.desc: Per-block weight (kg).

outputs.name: Volumes
outputs.desc: Per-block volume (m^3).

outputs.name: Centroids
outputs.desc: Per-block centroid.

outputs.name: Lift Class
outputs.desc: Per-block lift class.

outputs.name: Total Weight
outputs.desc: Sum of block weights (kg).

outputs.name: Report
outputs.desc: Summary + per-class counts.

name: G-code Parser
description: Parse an ISO 6983-1-subset G-code file (.nc / .gcode / .cnc) into  a typed CutPath record. Phase B Stage 1 of the scan-to-mill  architecture per wiki/specs/scan_to_mill_architecture.md §1.6.  First production component bridging Stone-Aware Cut Export ->  KUKAprc / Robots / SprutCAM / RhinoCAM. Supports the RhinoCAM  3-axis dialect observed in the MRAC 2023 workshop.

algorithm.title: ISO 6983-1 G-code tokenizer + modal state machine
algorithm.citation: Frahan-original; ISO 6983-1:2009 standard for CNC numerical control

inputs.name: File Path
inputs.desc: Absolute or relative path to a G-code .nc / .gcode / .cnc file.

inputs.name: Initial Position
inputs.desc: Optional initial tool position before the first G-code line.  Default (0,0,0). Used as the Start of the first segment if the  file does not begin with an explicit G00 / G01 rapid.

inputs.name: Skip Rapids
inputs.desc: If true, G00 rapid-traverse segments are dropped from the output  (only G01-cut + G02/G03-arc segments remain). Default false --  preserves the full toolpath for visualisation.

outputs.name: Cut Path
outputs.desc: The typed CutPath record. Wire into GCodeToPlanesComponent or  WireSawToolpathAdapterComponent downstream.

outputs.name: Segment Count
outputs.desc: Total segments parsed (informational; matches CutPath.Segments.Count).

outputs.name: Total Length
outputs.desc: Sum of segment lengths (linear approximation for arcs) in the  file's units. Useful for time estimates: time ≈ length / feed.

outputs.name: Segment Endpoints
outputs.desc: Per-segment end points (one per CutSegment). For canvas preview.

outputs.name: Remarks
outputs.desc: Parser diagnostics: line count, modal-mode transitions, comment  count, file-level F + S defaults, encountered unknown G-codes.

name: G-code to Planes
description: Translate a parsed CutPath into a Plane[] consumable by KUKAprc  (via its Plane->Command components) or visose/Robots (via  CreateTarget). Phase B Stage 2 of the scan-to-mill architecture.  Arc segments are discretised at Arc Step intervals; linear  segments emit one Plane per segment endpoint. Tool axis defaults  to -Z (downward milling).

algorithm.title: CutPath -> Plane[] tool-axis frame construction
algorithm.citation: Frahan-original; standard milling-frame convention (tool axis = -Z by default)

inputs.name: Cut Path
inputs.desc: The typed CutPath from GCodeParserComponent (D5F10030).

inputs.name: Tool Axis
inputs.desc: Tool axis vector in WORLD coordinates. Default -Z (downward  milling, the dominant case). The emitted Plane's Z-axis aligns  with this vector; rotation about Z is determined by the segment  direction.

inputs.name: Arc Step
inputs.desc: Chord step (mm) for arc discretisation. Smaller = more Planes /  tighter chord. Default 2.0 mm (sub-mm-spec friendly). Set 0 to  emit only segment endpoints (chord-only mode).

outputs.name: Planes
outputs.desc: Per-segment + per-arc-sample Planes (KUKAprc + Robots consumable).

outputs.name: Feed Rates
outputs.desc: Per-Plane feed rate (mm/min); parallels Planes list.

outputs.name: Spindle Speeds
outputs.desc: Per-Plane spindle speed (RPM); parallels Planes list.

outputs.name: Segment Indices
outputs.desc: Per-Plane source segment index (so the user can trace each Plane  back to a CutPath.Segments entry).

outputs.name: Remarks
outputs.desc: Per-pipeline diagnostics: Plane count, arc-sample count, tool- axis quality flags.

name: Monument Inventory
description: Bundle Rhino meshes as a MonumentInventory consumable by  the Frahan Bench Monument Pack components. Each mesh becomes  one Monument; ids are optional and auto-generated when blank.

inputs.name: Meshes
inputs.desc: One mesh per monument.

inputs.name: Ids
inputs.desc: Optional ids; auto-generated when blank.

inputs.name: Density (kg/m^3)
inputs.desc: Material density.

outputs.name: Inventory
outputs.desc: MonumentInventory.

outputs.name: Count
outputs.desc: Number of monuments.

outputs.name: Total AABB Volume (m^3)
outputs.desc: Sum of monument AABB volumes.

name: Pack Monuments In Cell
description: Pack a MonumentInventory inside ONE BlockCell. Useful when  you want to assign specific monuments to specific cells  rather than letting the bench-wide packer order them. Frahan-original method.

algorithm.title: 24-orientation SO(3) sampling + greedy AABB packing
algorithm.citation: Frahan-original

inputs.name: Block Graph
inputs.desc: BlockGraph (the cell is selected by index).

inputs.name: Cell Index
inputs.desc: Index of the cell within Bg.Cells.

inputs.name: Inventory
inputs.desc: MonumentInventory.

inputs.name: Grid Stride (m)
inputs.desc: Candidate-origin sweep step.

outputs.name: Placed Boxes
outputs.desc: Axis-aligned box per placement.

outputs.name: Placed Ids
outputs.desc: Monument ids placed.

outputs.name: Orientation Index
outputs.desc: Rotation index per placement.

outputs.name: Placed Count
outputs.desc: Total placements in this cell.

name: Planes to KUKAprc Commands
description: Tag a Plane[] (from GCodeToPlanes or WireSawToolpath) with  KUKAprc-compatible motion-type (LIN / PTP) and feed metadata.  Thin wrapper -- Frahan stops here; KUKAprc Pro owns the final  Plane->LIN/PTP/CIRC command construction + KRL code generation.
