This wrapper IS the first FREE open-source G-code path into  KUKAprc (paid Generic NC Import is the only alternative).

algorithm.title: CutSegmentKind -> KUKAprc Motion mapping
algorithm.citation: Frahan-original; standard CAM-to-KRL motion-type convention (Rapid->PTP, Linear/Arc->LIN)

inputs.name: Planes
inputs.desc: Per-pose tool-axis frames (from GCodeToPlanes D5F10031 or  WireSawToolpath D5F10034). Each plane = one KUKAprc target.

inputs.name: Feed Rates
inputs.desc: Per-plane feed rate (mm/min); parallels Planes list. The Frahan  convention is mm/min; KUKAprc consumes this as the LIN/PTP velocity  argument after the user maps it to their unit-system in KUKAprc.

inputs.name: Spindle Speeds
inputs.desc: Per-plane spindle speed (RPM); parallels Planes list. KUKAprc has  no native spindle command; the user wires this into a KUKAprc  ENV-variable assignment or vendor-specific tool-trigger.

inputs.name: Cut Path (optional)
inputs.desc: Optional: the source CutPath typed record (from GCodeParser  D5F10030). If wired, the wrapper distinguishes Rapid (PTP) from  Linear/Arc (LIN) per-plane via the Segment Indices lookup.

inputs.name: Segment Indices (optional)
inputs.desc: Optional: per-plane source segment index (from GCodeToPlanes).  Required when Cut Path is wired so the wrapper can look up the  CutSegmentKind for motion-type mapping.

outputs.name: Planes
outputs.desc: Plane[] passthrough; wire into KUKAprc Pro's Plane->LIN/PTP  command components.

outputs.name: Motion Types
outputs.desc: Per-plane motion type: "LIN" (linear cut) or "PTP"  (point-to-point rapid). Wire into KUKAprc's command branch  selector.

outputs.name: Feed Rates
outputs.desc: Per-plane feed rate passthrough (mm/min).

outputs.name: Spindle Speeds
outputs.desc: Per-plane spindle RPM passthrough.

outputs.name: Remarks
outputs.desc: KUKAprc Pro version + paid-tier dependency note + motion-type  histogram.

name: Planes to Robot Targets
description: Tag a Plane[] (from GCodeToPlanes or WireSawToolpath) with  visose/Robots-compatible motion (Linear / Joint), speed (mm/s),  and zone (mm blending) metadata. Thin wrapper -- Frahan stops  here; visose/Robots owns the Plane->CreateTarget construction +  kinematic simulation. This wrapper + Frahan's GCodeParser is the  only path from G-code into visose/Robots (the plugin has zero  native G-code ingest).

algorithm.title: CutSegmentKind -> visose/Robots Motion mapping
algorithm.citation: Frahan-original; standard CAM-to-robot-target motion convention (Rapid->Joint, Linear/Arc->Linear)

inputs.name: Planes
inputs.desc: Per-pose tool-axis frames (from GCodeToPlanes D5F10031 or  WireSawToolpath D5F10034). Each Plane = one visose/Robots Target.

inputs.name: Feed Rates
inputs.desc: Per-plane feed rate (mm/min); parallels Planes. Converted to  mm/s (factor 1/60) on the way to visose/Robots' Speed convention.

inputs.name: Spindle Speeds
inputs.desc: Per-plane spindle RPM passthrough. visose/Robots does not consume  spindle directly; the user wires this into a Robots `Command`  tool-trigger if needed.

inputs.name: Default Zone
inputs.desc: Blending zone radius (mm) applied to all targets when CutPath  is not wired. Default 1.0 mm (visose/Robots fine zone).

inputs.name: Cut Path (optional)
inputs.desc: Optional CutPath typed record (from GCodeParser D5F10030). If  wired, the wrapper distinguishes Rapid (Joint motion) from  Linear / Arc (Linear motion).

inputs.name: Segment Indices (optional)
inputs.desc: Optional: per-plane source segment index (from GCodeToPlanes);  required when Cut Path is wired.

outputs.name: Planes
outputs.desc: Plane[] passthrough; wire into visose/Robots `Create Target`.Plane.

outputs.name: Motions
outputs.desc: Per-plane motion: "Linear" or "Joint". Wire into visose/Robots  `Create Target`.Motion.

outputs.name: Speeds
outputs.desc: Per-plane speed (mm/s); fed into visose/Robots' Speed parameter.

outputs.name: Zones
outputs.desc: Per-plane blending zone (mm); fed into visose/Robots' Zone parameter.

outputs.name: Remarks
outputs.desc: visose/Robots version + packaging note + motion-type histogram.

name: Staggered Block Decompose
description: Lay out staggered (running-bond) blocks over a sculpted form's  bounding box for wire-saw + robotic-mill fabrication. Emits the  staggered cells (boxes + box meshes) + per-cell course index  (ascending = build order). Pipe Cell Meshes into Quarry Decompose  By Mesh (CGAL) / Mesh CSG (CGAL) for form-fitted blocks — this  component does NOT fire many RhinoCommon booleans (the HITL  large-slab failure mode). Frahan-original method.

algorithm.title: Running-bond staggered cell layout
algorithm.citation: Frahan-original

inputs.name: Form
inputs.desc: Sculpted / freeform stone mesh to decompose.

inputs.name: Course Height
inputs.desc: Course (layer) height along the up axis.

inputs.name: Block Length
inputs.desc: Block length along the bond axis.

inputs.name: Stagger
inputs.desc: Odd-course shift as a fraction of block length (0..1). Default 0.5 = running bond.

inputs.name: Up Axis
inputs.desc: Course-stacking axis: 0=X, 1=Y, 2=Z (default).

outputs.name: Cells
outputs.desc: Staggered cell boxes.

outputs.name: Cell Meshes
outputs.desc: Cell boxes as meshes (feed into CGAL/geogram decompose).

outputs.name: Course
outputs.desc: Per-cell course index (ascending = build order).

outputs.name: Count
outputs.desc: Number of cells.

outputs.name: Report
outputs.desc: Layout summary + min/max cell size (wire-saw / mill feasibility).

name: Stone-Aware Cut Export
description: Write cut pieces (mesh / brep / curve / surface) to a .3dm with  stone metadata (bed direction, finish, weight, kerf, provenance)  attached per piece as namespaced user-strings + one layer per  piece, so CAM (EasySTONE, Alphacam, Breton, Lantek) keeps the  stone intelligence. Set Write = true to write the file.

inputs.name: Geometry
inputs.desc: Cut pieces: mesh / brep / curve / surface.

inputs.name: Piece Ids
inputs.desc: Per-piece id (parallel to Geometry). Auto S001.. if absent.

inputs.name: Stone
inputs.desc: Stone / source applied to all pieces (e.g. 'TN Black Granite').

inputs.name: Finish
inputs.desc: Finish applied to all (polished / honed / flamed / sandblasted).

inputs.name: Bed Direction
inputs.desc: Bed / grain direction (unit vector) applied to all.

inputs.name: Weight kg
inputs.desc: Per-piece weight in kg (parallel to Geometry).

inputs.name: Kerf mm
inputs.desc: Saw kerf in mm applied to all.

inputs.name: File Path
inputs.desc: Output .3dm path.

inputs.name: Write
inputs.desc: Set true to write the .3dm. False = dry run (reports only).

outputs.name: File Path
outputs.desc: Path written (empty on dry run / failure).

outputs.name: Piece Count
outputs.desc: Number of pieces exported.

outputs.name: Report
outputs.desc: Export summary.

name: Wire-Saw Toolpath
description: Generate a Plane[] toolpath for a robot-mounted diamond-wire saw  to cut stone along a designed curve. Frahan-original component  closing the toolchain gap left by Zhang 2024 + Moult 2018 (neither  has KUKAprc / Robots plugin integration). v1 supports planar cuts;  v1.x adds ruled-surface decomposition + variable wire tension.  Outputs feed directly into KUKAprc / Robots downstream.

algorithm.title: Moult2018PortableWireBandsaw
algorithm.citation: Moult, Weir, Fernando 2018 University of Sydney KUKA + portable diamond-wire bandsaw end-effector

inputs.name: Cut Curve
inputs.desc: The designed cut path the wire traces. Closed or open.  v1 supports planar curves; v1.x adds ruled-surface paths.

inputs.name: Wire Axis
inputs.desc: Wire-axis vector in WORLD coordinates -- the direction the  wire is tensioned. Perpendicular to the cut direction at  every sample. Default world-Y (cuts in XZ plane).

inputs.name: Kerf Width
inputs.desc: Diamond-wire kerf width (mm). Default 4.0 mm (mid-range for  brazed diamond wires; Zhang 2024 reports Δ = 1.75 mm half-kerf).

inputs.name: Sample Count
inputs.desc: Number of Planes to emit along the cut curve. Higher = smoother  robot motion + more program lines. Default 32.

inputs.name: Feed Rate
inputs.desc: Wire feed rate (mm/min) at each sample. Zhang 2024 reports  wire surface speeds in the 30-50 m/s range; conservative GH  feed default = 300 mm/min linear advance.

inputs.name: Apply Kerf Compensation
inputs.desc: If true, offsets the cut curve by Kerf Width / 2 so the FINISHED  cut surface matches the design. Default true.

outputs.name: Planes
outputs.desc: Per-sample Plane[]: origin = curve sample, X = tangent, Z = wire axis.  Wire into KUKAprc / Robots downstream.

outputs.name: Feed Rates
outputs.desc: Per-Plane feed rate (mm/min); parallels Planes list.

outputs.name: Compensated Curve
outputs.desc: The kerf-compensated cut curve (offset by Kerf Width / 2).  Returned even when Apply Kerf Compensation = false (= input curve).

outputs.name: Remarks
outputs.desc: Per-pipeline diagnostics + Zhang 2024 / Moult 2018 reference notes.

name: Brick-Pattern Fracture Planes
description: Orthogonal fracture set emulating a running-bond brick  layout. nX = vertical planes per course, nZ = horizontal  course separators. Frahan-original method.

algorithm.title: Running-bond brick-pattern fracture set
algorithm.citation: Frahan-original

inputs.name: Slab
inputs.desc: Slab DTO whose bounding box seeds the brick grid.

inputs.name: nX
inputs.desc: Vertical fracture planes per course. >= 0.

inputs.name: nZ
inputs.desc: Horizontal course separators. >= 0.

outputs.name: Planes
outputs.desc: FracturePlane DTOs in a running-bond pattern.

name: Fracture Plane Filter
description: Drops planes that miss the target slab's bounding box.  Pre-cutting optimisation; SlabCutter handles non-intersecting  planes gracefully on its own.

inputs.name: Planes
inputs.desc: Input FracturePlane DTOs (any source).

inputs.name: Slab
inputs.desc: Target Slab whose bounding box drives the filter.

outputs.name: Planes
outputs.desc: Filtered FracturePlane DTOs (only those intersecting the slab AABB).

name: Fracture Polygon From Curve
description: Wraps a closed planar polyline into a FracturePolygon DTO.  The curve must convert to a polyline with at least 4 points  (closing duplicate dropped). The polygon must be convex and  planar; the FracturePolygon constructor enforces both.

inputs.name: Curve
inputs.desc: Closed polyline (or PolylineCurve) describing a finite convex  fracture polygon. The curve must yield a polyline with at least  4 points and be closed. Planarity required unless ForceProject  is true.

inputs.name: ForceProject
inputs.desc: When true, near-planar curves are projected onto their best-fit  plane before constructing the polygon. Default false (strict).

outputs.name: FracturePolygon
outputs.desc: FracturePolygon DTO. Wire into Slab Cut By Fracture Polygons.

name: Grid Fracture Planes
description: Produces an orthogonal grid of FracturePlanes inside the  bounding box of the input Slab. nX/nY/nZ control how many  evenly-spaced planes are emitted along each axis. Frahan-original method.

algorithm.title: Orthogonal grid fracture set
algorithm.citation: Frahan-original

inputs.name: Slab
inputs.desc: Slab DTO whose bounding box seeds the grid.

inputs.name: nX
inputs.desc: Number of planes perpendicular to +X. Must be >= 0.

inputs.name: nY
inputs.desc: Number of planes perpendicular to +Y. Must be >= 0.

inputs.name: nZ
inputs.desc: Number of planes perpendicular to +Z. Must be >= 0.

outputs.name: Planes
outputs.desc: FracturePlane DTOs. Wire into Slab Cut By Fractures or Quarry Decompose.

name: Jittered Grid Fracture Planes
description: Orthogonal grid of FracturePlanes with each plane jittered  by up to (jitter * cellStep) along its normal. Deterministic  for a given Seed. Frahan-original method.

algorithm.title: Grid with per-plane offset jitter
algorithm.citation: Frahan-original

inputs.name: Slab
inputs.desc: Slab DTO whose bounding box seeds the grid.

inputs.name: nX
inputs.desc: Planes perpendicular to +X (>= 0).

inputs.name: nY
inputs.desc: Planes perpendicular to +Y (>= 0).

inputs.name: nZ
inputs.desc: Planes perpendicular to +Z (>= 0).

inputs.name: Jitter
inputs.desc: Per-plane offset jitter as a fraction of the cell step. In [0, 0.5).

inputs.name: Seed
inputs.desc: Random seed.

outputs.name: Planes
outputs.desc: FracturePlane DTOs.

name: Layered Fracture Planes
description: Parallel planes equally spaced along the chosen axis.  Use Axis = 0 (X), 1 (Y), or 2 (Z). Common pattern for  sedimentary rocks. Frahan-original method.

algorithm.title: Parallel layered fracture set
algorithm.citation: Frahan-original

inputs.name: Slab
inputs.desc: Slab DTO whose bounding box seeds the layers.

inputs.name: Axis
inputs.desc: Layer normal direction: 0 = X, 1 = Y, 2 = Z. Default 2 (Z).

inputs.name: Count
inputs.desc: Number of layers (interior cuts). Must be >= 0.

outputs.name: Planes
outputs.desc: FracturePlane DTOs (parallel, evenly spaced).

name: Radial Fracture Planes
description: N planes that share a common axis line, rotated by  180/N degrees per plane. Pie-wedge cut pattern; common  for log-like or cylindrical stones. Frahan-original method.

algorithm.title: Radial / fan fracture set
algorithm.citation: Frahan-original

inputs.name: Center
inputs.desc: Point on the rotation axis.

inputs.name: Axis
inputs.desc: Axis direction.

inputs.name: Count
inputs.desc: Number of radial planes. Must be >= 0.

outputs.name: Planes
outputs.desc: FracturePlane DTOs around the rotation axis.

name: Random Fracture Planes
description: Produces N FracturePlanes with points inside the slab's  bounding box and normals uniform on the sphere. Deterministic  for a given Seed. Frahan-original method.

algorithm.title: Random plane placement
algorithm.citation: Frahan-original

inputs.name: Slab
inputs.desc: Slab DTO whose bounding box seeds the random plane points.

inputs.name: Count
inputs.desc: Number of random planes. Must be >= 0.

inputs.name: Seed
inputs.desc: Random seed for reproducibility.

outputs.name: Planes
outputs.desc: FracturePlane DTOs.

name: Slab Cut By Fracture Polygons
description: Cuts a list of Slabs by a list of finite FracturePolygons.  A polygon that fully contains the slab cross-section produces  two pieces; a polygon that misses the slab is a passthrough;  a polygon that only partially overlaps the cross-section is a  passthrough unless ExtendPartial is set, in which case the  polygon's supporting plane is used as an infinite cut.

inputs.name: Mesh
inputs.desc: Convex meshes to cut. Standard Rhino mesh wires; the cutter  converts to its internal Slab DTO automatically.

inputs.name: FracturePolygon
inputs.desc: FracturePolygon DTOs (from Fracture Polygon From Curve).

inputs.name: ExtendPartial
inputs.desc: When true, a fracture polygon that only partially covers the  slab cross-section is treated as if it were an infinite plane.  Default: false (partial fractures pass through untouched).

inputs.name: Eps
inputs.desc: Vertex-classification epsilon for the underlying SlabCutter.  Default 1e-9.

outputs.name: Slab
outputs.desc: Output Slabs after cutting.

outputs.name: Count
outputs.desc: Number of resulting Slabs.

outputs.name: TotalVolume
outputs.desc: Sum of signed volumes of all output Slabs (sanity check).

outputs.name: Mesh
outputs.desc: Output Slabs as Rhino Meshes (parallel to the Slab list).

name: Voronoi Fracture Planes
description: Emits the perpendicular bisector plane between every pair of  input seed Points. Cutting a slab with these planes  approximates Voronoi-cell decomposition. Implements Voronoi partition (Aurenhammer 1991).

algorithm.title: Voronoi perpendicular-bisector cell construction
algorithm.citation: Aurenhammer, F. (1991). "Voronoi diagrams—a survey." ACM Computing Surveys 23(3):345-405

inputs.name: Seeds
inputs.desc: Voronoi seed points. At least 2 required.

outputs.name: Planes
outputs.desc: FracturePlane DTOs (one per distinct seed pair).

name: GPR File Loader
description: Load a ground-penetrating-radar file by extension: CSV / SEG-Y / MALA RD3 / pulseEKKO DT1.  Emits trace-start points + count + sample spacing. Sample amplitudes are not piped to the  canvas (too large for GH data trees); use the Core reader for per-sample access.  Workflows cross-checked against RGPR (the open R GPR-processing package) in the companion paper.

algorithm.title: Multi-format GPR ingest dispatcher
algorithm.citation: Frahan-original; routes .csv / .sgy / .segy / .rd3 / .dt1 to matching reader

inputs.name: File Path
inputs.desc: Absolute path to a .csv / .sgy / .segy / .rd3 / .dt1 file.  .rd3 expects a companion .rad alongside; .dt1 expects a companion .HD.

inputs.name: Id
inputs.desc: Optional radargram identifier label. Defaults to the file's basename.

outputs.name: Trace Count
outputs.desc: Number of traces in the radargram.

outputs.name: Trace Origins
outputs.desc: One Point3d per trace, at (sourceX, sourceY, 0) in source CRS units.

outputs.name: Sample Spacing m
outputs.desc: Sample spacing (metres) of the first trace; uniform within one radargram.

outputs.name: Sample Count
outputs.desc: Number of samples per trace (first trace).

outputs.name: Source File
outputs.desc: The source file path echoed verbatim.

name: GPR Picks From Points
description: Turn points picked on a GPR Radargram Mesh section into reflector  picks for GPR Fractures on Mesh. Recovers true depth by undoing the  Depth Scale, tags label + confidence, and optionally writes a picks  CSV (x_m,y_m,depth_m,confidence_01,label) that reloads via GPR  Radargram Mesh's Picks CSV input.

algorithm.title: Interactive reflector picking
algorithm.citation: Frahan-original: viewport points on the radargram section -> reflector picks (depth = -z / DepthScale)

inputs.name: Points
inputs.desc: Points picked on the radargram section (snap to the section mesh).

inputs.name: Depth Scale
inputs.desc: The Depth Scale used on GPR Radargram Mesh (to recover true depth = -z / Z).  Match it. Default 1.

inputs.name: Label
inputs.desc: Fracture label(s). One value = applied to all picks; a list of the same  length = per-pick (group picks into distinct fractures). Default 'pick'.

inputs.name: Confidence
inputs.desc: Confidence 0..1 for the picks. Default 1.

inputs.name: CSV Out
inputs.desc: OPTIONAL path to write a picks CSV (reloadable via GPR Radargram Mesh Picks CSV).

outputs.name: Pick Points
outputs.desc: The picks (section frame) — wire into GPR Fractures on Mesh 'Picks'.

outputs.name: Labels
outputs.desc: Resolved label per pick.

outputs.name: Confidence
outputs.desc: Confidence per pick.

outputs.name: Picks CSV
outputs.desc: The picks CSV content (also written if CSV Out is set).

name: GPR Radargram Mesh
description: Read a GPR file and draw the radargram as a vertex-coloured vertical  section mesh: the curtain follows the survey line (trace X,Y) and  goes down by sample depth; vertex colour = reflection amplitude  (blue low, white mid, red high). Reflector picks come out as points.  Use instead of GPR File Loader when you want to SEE the radargram.

algorithm.title: GPR amplitude section visualisation
algorithm.citation: Frahan-original: traces x samples amplitude grid -> vertex-coloured vertical-section mesh

inputs.name: File Path
inputs.desc: Path to a GPR file (CSV radargram; see GPR File Loader for formats).

inputs.name: Id
inputs.desc: Optional radargram id (defaults to file name).

inputs.name: Depth Scale
inputs.desc: Vertical exaggeration of the depth axis. 1 = true scale.

inputs.name: Trace Spacing
inputs.desc: Fallback in-plane spacing between traces when traces share the same  X,Y (no survey geometry). 0 = use the trace X,Y as-is.

inputs.name: Contrast
inputs.desc: Amplitude contrast (gamma on the normalized amplitude). 1 = linear;  >1 boosts faint reflectors.

inputs.name: Picks CSV
inputs.desc: OPTIONAL path to an interpreted-reflector picks CSV  (x_m,y_m,depth_m,confidence_01,label). Most GPR files carry NO picks,  so without this the Pick Points output is empty. Supply picks here to  drive GPR Fractures on Mesh.

outputs.name: Radargram
outputs.desc: Vertex-coloured radargram section mesh.

outputs.name: Pick Points
outputs.desc: Interpreted reflector picks at depth.

outputs.name: Pick Labels
outputs.desc: Label per pick.

outputs.name: Pick Confidence
outputs.desc: 0..1 confidence per pick.

outputs.name: Amplitude Range
outputs.desc: Min/max amplitude used for the colour map.

outputs.name: Report
outputs.desc: Summary.

name: Import Photo Markers
description: Read photogrammetry markers / GCPs from a CSV (Metashape / COLMAP /  RealityCapture export or a plain GCP file):  'label, worldX,Y,Z [, modelX,Y,Z]'. Outputs World points (base  frame) + Model points (scan frame, if present) + labels. Feed  World -> Target and Model -> Source of Georeference (Align by  Points), Scale = true, to position the scan on its base.

inputs.name: File
inputs.desc: Path to a marker / GCP CSV.

outputs.name: Labels
outputs.desc: Per-marker label.

outputs.name: World
outputs.desc: Marker positions in the base / world frame (-> Georeference Target).

outputs.name: Model
outputs.desc: Marker positions in the scan / model frame, if present (-> Georeference Source).

outputs.name: Has Model
outputs.desc: True if the file carried model-frame positions.

outputs.name: Count
outputs.desc: Number of markers read.

name: Vector Fractures Loader
description: Load fracture traces from a Shapefile (.shp) or GeoJSON (.geojson) into Rhino  as open PolylineCurves, plus their attributes and source CRS WKT. Uses  NetTopologySuite under the hood; format dispatched by file extension.  Reads ESRI Shapefile / OGC Simple Features (industry standard, not a published algorithm).

algorithm.title: Vector fracture import
algorithm.citation: ESRI Shapefile / OGC Simple Features (standard)

inputs.name: File Path
inputs.desc: Absolute path to a .shp or .geojson file. For Shapefiles,  the companion .dbf + .shx + .prj must sit alongside.

outputs.name: Traces
outputs.desc: One open PolylineCurve per fracture trace, in the source CRS units.

outputs.name: Count
outputs.desc: Number of traces returned.

outputs.name: CRS WKT
outputs.desc: Coordinate reference system as WKT (Shapefile .prj). Empty for GeoJSON.

outputs.name: Attribute Keys
outputs.desc: Per-trace attribute keys as a {trace_index;0} data tree.

outputs.name: Attribute Values
outputs.desc: Per-trace attribute values parallel to Attribute Keys.

outputs.name: Source File
outputs.desc: The source file path echoed verbatim.

name: Contact Settle
description: Push placed fragment meshes apart with rigid translations until  they touch at fracture faces but do not interpenetrate. Closes  open meshes for the solid inside test. Run after Frahan Kintsugi.

algorithm.title: Rigid depenetration (contact settle)
algorithm.citation: Iterative rigid relaxation that pushes placed fragments apart until solids

inputs.name: Fragments
inputs.desc: Placed fragment meshes to de-penetrate (e.g. Frahan Kintsugi output).

inputs.name: Iterations
inputs.desc: Max relaxation iterations. Stops early once max penetration <=  Penetration Tol. Default 25.

inputs.name: Penetration Tol
inputs.desc: Target max penetration depth (model units). 0 = settle to just  touching; a small positive value tolerates slight overlap.  Default 0.0.

inputs.name: Relaxation
inputs.desc: Per-iteration step factor 0..1. Lower = stabler but slower; higher  = faster but can oscillate. Default 0.5.

inputs.name: Close Open Meshes
inputs.desc: FillHoles each fragment to a watertight solid for the inside test  (output keeps the original open mesh, translated). Default true.

inputs.name: Lock First
inputs.desc: Keep fragment 0 fixed as the anchor so the whole assembly does not  drift; all corrections go to the other piece. Default true.

inputs.name: Run
inputs.desc: Execute the settle.

outputs.name: Settled Fragments
outputs.desc: Fragments translated so solids no longer interpenetrate.

outputs.name: Transforms
outputs.desc: Net rigid translation applied to each fragment (input order).

outputs.name: Max Penetration
outputs.desc: Final maximum pairwise penetration depth (should be <= Pt).

outputs.name: Report
outputs.desc: Per-iteration max penetration + close/fallback diagnostics.

name: Fracture Roughen
description: Give Voronoi shatter fragments worn, irregular fracture surfaces  using a shared world-position fractal field, so the pieces still  fit together. Wire between Frahan Fragment Shatter and Frahan  Kintsugi.

algorithm.title: Fracture surface roughen (shared fractal field)
algorithm.citation: Displaces cut-region vertices by a single world-position fractal noise

inputs.name: Fragments
inputs.desc: List of fragments to roughen (typically from Frahan Fragment Shatter).

inputs.name: Amplitude
inputs.desc: Displacement amplitude as a FRACTION of the bounding box diagonal.  Default 0.02 (2% of bbox). Larger = deeper worn relief.

inputs.name: Roughness
inputs.desc: Per-octave amplitude falloff (persistence). 0.5 = balanced.  Higher = rougher/grittier; lower = smoother. Default 0.5.

inputs.name: Seed
inputs.desc: RNG seed for the shared noise field. SAME seed = SAME field for  every fragment (required for mating). Default 42.

inputs.name: Run
inputs.desc: Apply.

inputs.name: Frequency
inputs.desc: Noise frequency as cycles across the bounding box diagonal.  Lower = broad gentle waves; higher = fine pitting. Default 3.0.

inputs.name: Octaves
inputs.desc: Fractal octaves summed (each doubles frequency, halves amplitude).  1 = smooth, 4-5 = rich worn detail. Default 4.

inputs.name: Cap Cuts
inputs.desc: TRUE = FillHoles first so the cut becomes a worn SURFACE (closed  fragment). FALSE = displace only the open rim. Default TRUE.

outputs.name: Roughened Fragments
outputs.desc: Fragments with worn, irregular fracture surfaces (still mating).

outputs.name: Displaced Count
outputs.desc: Total cut-region vertices displaced (across all fragments).

outputs.name: Report
outputs.desc: Per-fragment displacement count.

name: Fragment Shatter
description: Voronoi-shatter a solid input mesh into N fragments suitable  for round-trip testing of Frahan Kintsugi.  Outputs each Voronoi cell as a separate mesh with the original  outer surface plus fresh fracture rims on the cut surfaces.

algorithm.title: Voronoi shatter for fracture test-beds
algorithm.citation: Frahan-original: deterministic seed -> pairwise bisector planes ->

inputs.name: Solid
inputs.desc: Input mesh to shatter. Pot, sphere, sculpture, etc. Should be  closed-ish; tiny gaps are tolerated.

inputs.name: Fragment Count
inputs.desc: Number of Voronoi cells (= output fragments). Practical range 2..30.  Higher = slower (O(N^2) plane clips).

inputs.name: Seed
inputs.desc: Deterministic random seed. Re-running with the same value  produces the same shatter pattern. Default 42.

inputs.name: Jitter
inputs.desc: Voronoi seed positional noise relative to the bbox diagonal.  0 = grid layout, 1 = full random in the bbox. Default 0.6  (mostly random with a touch of regularity for predictable demos).

inputs.name: Min Fragment Volume
inputs.desc: Drop any cell whose volume is below this fraction of the  input volume (0 to disable). Default 0.005 = 0.5% drops  slivers from edge cells.

inputs.name: Run
inputs.desc: Execute the shatter.

outputs.name: Fragments
outputs.desc: Voronoi-shattered fragments. Wire directly into Kintsugi.

outputs.name: Seed Points
outputs.desc: Voronoi seed points used (one per fragment). Diagnostic.

outputs.name: Drop Count
outputs.desc: Number of cells dropped under Min Fragment Volume.

outputs.name: Report
outputs.desc: Per-cell volume / face / vertex / naked-edge counts.

name: Kintsugi
description: 3D mesh fracture-assembly via naked-edge rim matching.  Each fragment's open-boundary loops are treated as 3D  panels and joined by the same deterministic 5-stage  edge-matching pipeline used by Frahan Trencadís EdgeMatch.  Inspired by PuzzleFusion++ but no learned model; runs  entirely in-process. Best when fracture rims are clean  and well-defined. [Wang et al. 2025]

algorithm.title: Auto-agglomerative outer loop
algorithm.citation: Clean-room translation of PuzzleFusion++ Section 3 outer schedule

inputs.name: Fragments
inputs.desc: List of mesh fragments to reassemble. First fragment is  anchored at the identity transform; all others are placed  relative to it. Each mesh must have at least one open  boundary loop (naked edges) for the GEOMETRIC path.  For Mode=Port: OPTIONAL when Point Clouds (PC) is wired -- the  port path derives output meshes from the point clouds directly  if Fragments is unwired.

inputs.name: Joint Width
inputs.desc: Edge-match residual tolerance (document units). Larger =  more forgiving rim alignment. Default 1.0.

inputs.name: Sample Spacing
inputs.desc: Arc-length spacing along each naked-edge loop. Match the  mesh's edge length. Default 1.0.

inputs.name: Break Angle Deg
inputs.desc: Curvature peak threshold for segment break (degrees).  Lower = more sensitive (more segments per rim). Default 8  (was 25 -- pottery rims are usually smoother than wood,  needs lower threshold to find the curvature peaks).

inputs.name: Min Segment Length
inputs.desc: Below this chord length a rim segment is treated as noise.  Lower = preserves shorter notch features. Default 1.0  (was 5 -- too aggressive for dense rim meshes; lots of  real notches got filtered out as noise).

inputs.name: Beam Width
inputs.desc: Beam-search concurrent states. Pottery fragments have more  ambiguous matches than wood; 32 recommended.

inputs.name: Max Iterations
inputs.desc: AssemblySolver inner-loop iteration cap (per round).

inputs.name: Min Loop Length
inputs.desc: Naked-edge loops shorter than this are ignored as noise  (e.g. tiny holes from mesh artifacts). Default 10.

inputs.name: Max Rounds
inputs.desc: Auto-agglomerative outer-loop cap. After each round,  successfully-placed fragments merge into the anchor cluster  and unplaced fragments retry against the larger cluster.  Mirrors PuzzleFusion++'s up-to-6-iteration outer schedule.

inputs.name: Verifier Penetration Tol
inputs.desc: Geometric verifier: reject placements whose transformed  mesh penetrates an already-placed mesh by more than this  distance (document units). 0 = disable the verifier.  Replaces PuzzleFusion++'s learned binary verifier. Default 0.5.

inputs.name: Diffusion Steps
inputs.desc: Mode=Port only. Number of diffusion sampling steps. Higher =  better assembly quality, slower. Paper default is 20; for  interactive prototyping use 5-10. Cost scales linearly  (re-encoder runs at each step).

inputs.name: Use Port Mode
inputs.desc: FALSE (default) = geometric path via Frahan.EdgeMatching.Core;  no GPL code linked at runtime; in-process; deterministic.  TRUE = GPL-3.0 PuzzleFusion++ learned path via Frahan.Kintsugi.Port;  requires kintsugi.bin weight file in the .gha deploy folder.

inputs.name: Run
inputs.desc: Execute the solver.

inputs.name: Use TorchSharp
inputs.desc: Mode=Port only. FALSE (default) = manual C# port denoiser  (~3-5% per-layer drift vs paper). TRUE = TorchSharp/libtorch  denoiser using PyTorch's exact kernels for paper-quality  inference. Requires libtorch DLLs in the .gha deploy folder.  Falls back to manual port if TorchSharp init fails.

inputs.name: Point Clouds
inputs.desc: OPTIONAL Mode=Port override. Per-fragment point cloud as a  Grasshopper tree: one BRANCH per fragment, N=1000 points per  branch. When wired, the Port-mode pipeline uses these points  DIRECTLY for the encoder instead of sampling N=1000 points from  the Fragments meshes. Useful when you have authoritative point  data (e.g. from Load BB Sample) and don't want sampling noise.\n If wired branch-count mismatches Fragments count, this input is  ignored and the mesh sampler runs. Leave unwired for the default  mesh-sampling path.

inputs.name: Verifier Accept Threshold
inputs.desc: Mode=Port only. Minimum verifier pair-score for a fragment to be  PLACED via the network pose. Fragments whose best pair-score is  below this stay at their INPUT world position and are listed as  Unplaced. Default 0.5 (matches the 'STRONG' tag in the report).  Lower it (e.g. 0.45) to accept the network's near-miss pairs on  hard multi-fragment samples; raise it to demand higher confidence.

inputs.name: Non-Crossing
inputs.desc: Geometric path only. Order-preserving rim correspondence.  FALSE (default) = free nearest-point ICP (unchanged behaviour).  TRUE = monotone, non-crossing point pairing between fracture rims  (OrderedBoundaryMatcher); more robust on wiggly / noisy rims where  free matching tangles. Ignored in Mode=Port.

outputs.name: Assembled Fragments
outputs.desc: Input fragments transformed into their joined placement.  First fragment is at identity; others composed.

outputs.name: Transforms
outputs.desc: Per-fragment rigid SE(3) Transform. Parallel to Fragments.

outputs.name: Placed Indices
outputs.desc: Source-list indices of fragments that the solver placed.

outputs.name: Unplaced Indices
outputs.desc: Source-list indices of fragments left unjoined (no rim match found).

outputs.name: Residuals
outputs.desc: Per-rim-match ICP residual for diagnostics.

outputs.name: Total Residual
outputs.desc: Sum of per-rim residuals.

outputs.name: Rim Polylines
outputs.desc: Extracted naked-edge rim polylines per fragment (placed  frame). Diagnostic for tuning Sample Spacing / Break Angle.

outputs.name: Report
outputs.desc: Human-readable assembly summary.

name: Load BB Sample
description: Load a Breaking Bad sample (.bin from extract_breaking_bad_sample.py)  and output per-fragment point clouds + convex-hull meshes ready for  Frahan Kintsugi.

algorithm.title: Breaking Bad fragment loader
algorithm.citation: Loads upstream PuzzleFusion++ training-distribution fragments from

inputs.name: Sample File
inputs.desc: Path to a FRKINTSU .bin produced by extract_breaking_bad_sample.py.  Defaults to the bb_sample_00697.bin generated during the parity work. D:\code_ws\Template-General\outputs\2026-05-22\reference\bb_sample_00697.bin

inputs.name: Mesh Style
inputs.desc:
How to convert the loaded point cloud into a Mesh for  downstream Frahan Kintsugi consumption:
   0 = point-cloud vertices (DEFAULT, accuracy-preferred).  Builds a 12^3 bbox-subdivided mesh and pulls each vertex  to its nearest input point. Rhino may flag the mesh as  invalid (overlapping verts), but Kintsugi's surface sampler  lands closer to the ORIGINAL point cloud, giving the encoder  more accurate features (better verifier scores).
   1 = convex hull (display-preferred). Clean manifold via  FPS-subsample + QuickHull; valid mesh but loses interior  fragment shape and fragments at curved fracture interfaces  will visibly interpenetrate when assembled.
   2 = bbox cube. Always valid, simplest, but Kintsugi sees  a cube and the encoder produces ~unrelated features.
   3 = high-resolution bbox-pulled (24^3). Use when style 0  still leaves visible interpenetration between assembled  fragments at curved fracture surfaces. ~3750 surface  vertices per fragment; slower to display but tracks the  actual cloud surface closely.

inputs.name: Run
inputs.desc: Load.

outputs.name: Fragment Points
outputs.desc: Per-fragment 3D points (1000 per fragment, flattened across fragments).  Use the Branches output for per-fragment grouping.

outputs.name: Fragments
outputs.desc: Per-fragment convex-hull Mesh (coarse approximation suitable for  Frahan Kintsugi -- it re-samples points from the mesh surface anyway).  Wire this directly into Frahan Kintsugi's Fragments input.

outputs.name: Fragment Count
outputs.desc: Number of fragments in the sample.

outputs.name: Report
outputs.desc: Sample loader diagnostic.

name: Load Scan Fragments
description: Load scanned fragment .ply files (mesh or point cloud) into  per-fragment point clouds + meshes for Frahan Kintsugi Port mode.  Wire Points -> Point Clouds and Fragments -> Fragments.

algorithm.title: Load scanned PLY fragments for Kintsugi inference
algorithm.citation: Reads scattered scanned fragment .ply files into per-fragment point

inputs.name: Folder
inputs.desc: Optional folder to scan for *.ply (each file = one fragment).  Used when File Paths is empty.

inputs.name: File Paths
inputs.desc: Optional explicit list of .ply file paths (one fragment each).  Takes priority over Folder.

inputs.name: Sample Count
inputs.desc: Points emitted per fragment for the Point Clouds tree.  Upstream convention is 1000. Dense scans are subsampled; sparse  ones are repeated up to N.

inputs.name: Split Disjoint
inputs.desc: TRUE = a single .ply containing many shards is SPLIT into separate  fragments (disjoint mesh pieces, or proximity-clustered points).  Use this for one-file-many-shards scans. FALSE = each .ply is one  fragment. Default TRUE.

inputs.name: Cluster Tol
inputs.desc: Point-cloud clustering distance (document units) when Split  Disjoint is on and the .ply is a raw cloud (no faces). 0 = auto  (bbox diagonal / 80). Ignored for mesh PLYs (uses disjoint faces).

inputs.name: Remove Floor
inputs.desc: TRUE = RANSAC-detect the dominant plane (the scan floor/ground)  and strip points near it BEFORE splitting, so resting shards  separate into individual fragments. Works at the point level  (shards become point clusters). Default FALSE.

inputs.name: Floor Tol
inputs.desc: Distance band (document units) around the detected floor plane to  remove. 0 = auto (bbox diagonal / 150). Raise if the floor isn't  fully removed; lower if shard bottoms get clipped.

inputs.name: Run
inputs.desc: Load.

outputs.name: Points
outputs.desc: Per-fragment points as a tree: one BRANCH per fragment, N points  per branch. Wire into Frahan Kintsugi -> Point Clouds.

outputs.name: Fragments
outputs.desc: Per-fragment mesh (the PLY mesh if present, else a coarse  point-pulled mesh). Wire into Frahan Kintsugi -> Fragments.

outputs.name: Fragment Count
outputs.desc: Number of fragments loaded.

outputs.name: Report
outputs.desc: Per-file load diagnostic.

name: Synthetic Block
description: Generate parametric closed stone-block meshes (10 shapes) as  targets for synthetic fracture-assembly training. Wire into  Frahan Fragment Shatter or bake to .3dm for the PotNet-stone  exporter. Distinctive shapes train better than featureless ones.

inputs.name: Shape
inputs.desc: Block shape index 0..9: 0 Irregular Boulder, 1 Slab, 2 Tapered  Wedge, 3 Pyramidal Frustum, 4 Fluted Drum, 5 Faceted Gem,  6 Bossed Block, 7 Ridged Block, 8 Sculpted Relief, 9 Stepped  Block. For per-object 6-DoF training prefer 6/8 (asymmetric  features); 4/7/9 are periodic/symmetric and 0/1/5 featureless ->  ambiguous. All are fine for shatter / round-trip testing.

inputs.name: Size
inputs.desc: Longest block dimension in model units. Default 100.

inputs.name: Seed
inputs.desc: Deterministic seed (jitter / sculpt / boss placement). Default 42.

inputs.name: Feature
inputs.desc: Feature strength 0..1: flute depth, boss height, ridge depth,  sculpt amplitude, taper, step height. 0 = near-flat, 1 = bold.  Default 0.5.

inputs.name: Resolution
inputs.desc: Tessellation density for featured shapes (grid / around count).  Higher = finer surface detail, more faces. Default 24.

inputs.name: Variations
inputs.desc: Number of seed-varied copies, laid out along +X (1.6*Size apart).  Use to compare shapes by eye or build a multi-object set. Default 1.

outputs.name: Blocks
outputs.desc: Closed block meshes. Wire into Frahan Fragment Shatter or bake  to .3dm for the PotNet-stone exporter.

outputs.name: Shape Name
outputs.desc: Name of the selected shape.

outputs.name: Report
outputs.desc: Per-block vertex / face / closed / volume diagnostics.

name: CVT Seeds (Geogram)
description: Compute optimized seed positions on a surface via  centroidal Voronoi tessellation (Lloyd + Newton-Lloyd).  Output feeds directly into Voronoi Block Partition.  Implements CVT (Lloyd 1982) via Geogram.

algorithm.title: Centroidal Voronoi tessellation (Lloyd relaxation)
algorithm.citation: Lloyd, S. (1982). Least squares quantization in PCM. IEEE Trans. Inf. Theory IT-28:129-137

inputs.name: Mesh
inputs.desc: Input surface.

inputs.name: Points
inputs.desc: Seed count (50..500 typical for masonry blocks).

inputs.name: Lloyd Iters
inputs.desc: Lloyd relaxation iterations.

inputs.name: Newton Iters
inputs.desc: Newton iterations after Lloyd.

inputs.name: Run
inputs.desc: Set true to compute.

outputs.name: Seeds
outputs.desc: CVT seed positions.

outputs.name: Available
outputs.desc: True iff Geogram shim loaded.

outputs.name: Report
outputs.desc: Diagnostic report.

name: Density-Watershed Zones
description: Adaptive sub-division of the tested area by 2D fracture- density watershed (synthesis I5). Each zone boundary snaps  to high-density ridges so the unavoidable boundary penalty  lands on already-broken rock. Feed FracturePlanes from  Mesh2FxPl or any other planes-producing component.  Frahan-original method.

algorithm.title: Density-watershed partition (BlockCutOpt I5)
algorithm.citation: Frahan-original

inputs.name: Tested Area
inputs.desc: Bench bounding box (m).

inputs.name: Fracture Planes
inputs.desc: List<FracturePlane> (e.g. from Mesh2FxPl).

inputs.name: Bandwidth (m)
inputs.desc: Gaussian KDE bandwidth.

inputs.name: Raster Cell (m)
inputs.desc: Density-raster cell size; 0 = bandwidth/4.

outputs.name: Zone Boxes
outputs.desc: One axis-aligned Box per watershed basin.

outputs.name: Zone Ids
outputs.desc: Synthetic id per zone.

outputs.name: Zone Count
outputs.desc: Total number of zones.

name: Download Frahan Data
description: Download the optional large plugin data (Kintsugi Mode=Port weights +  torch/CUDA runtime, and/or examples) from a release manifest into the  folder beside the .gha, with SHA-256 verification. Runs on a background  thread; the canvas stays responsive. Files already present and verified  are skipped.

algorithm.title: On-demand data fetch + SHA-256 verify
algorithm.citation: Frahan-original distribution helper

inputs.name: Manifest URL
inputs.desc: URL of the data manifest (plain text; see component help). Usually a  manifest.txt attached to the GitHub Release.

inputs.name: What
inputs.desc: 0 = Port (kintsugi.bin + torch/CUDA runtime), 1 = Examples, 2 = All. Default 0.

inputs.name: Run
inputs.desc: Set true to download.

outputs.name: Port Ready
outputs.desc: True if kintsugi.bin is present beside the .gha (Mode=Port can run).

outputs.name: Installed
outputs.desc: Files installed / skipped this run.

outputs.name: Report
outputs.desc: Status summary.

name: Fisher-Robust BCO
description: Run BlockCutOpt M times against M Fisher-perturbed DFN  realisations of the same joint sets; return p10 / p50 / p90  recovery percent and the median psi. The robust optimum  direction is the median psi, not the single deterministic  best (Azarafza 2016 / synthesis I8). Parallel joint-set  lists must all be the same length.  Implements Fisher-scatter robustness sampling (Azarafza 2016).

algorithm.title: Fisher-distribution joint-scatter robustness sampling
algorithm.citation: Azarafza et al. (2016) granite block-cut + Fisher-distribution joint scatter

inputs.name: Tested Area
inputs.desc: Bench bounding box (m).

inputs.name: Dip Directions (deg)
inputs.desc: Per joint set, in [0,360).

inputs.name: Dips (deg)
inputs.desc: Per joint set, in [0,90].

inputs.name: Mean Spacings (m)
inputs.desc: Per joint set.

inputs.name: Scatters (deg)
inputs.desc: Fisher scatter per joint set.

inputs.name: Block X
inputs.desc: Block length (m).

inputs.name: Block Y
inputs.desc: Block width (m).

inputs.name: Block Z
inputs.desc: Block height (m).

inputs.name: Kerf
inputs.desc: Material-lost-by-quarrying (m).

inputs.name: Psi Step (deg)
inputs.desc: Angular search step.

inputs.name: MC Samples
inputs.desc: Monte Carlo sample count.

inputs.name: Base Seed
inputs.desc: Reproducibility seed.

outputs.name: Recovery p10 %
outputs.desc: 10th-percentile recovery (robust score).

outputs.name: Recovery p50 %
outputs.desc: Median recovery.

outputs.name: Recovery p90 %
outputs.desc: 90th-percentile recovery.

outputs.name: Recovery Mean %
outputs.desc: Mean recovery.

outputs.name: Recovery StdDev %
outputs.desc: Sample standard deviation.

outputs.name: Median Psi (deg)
outputs.desc: Median psi across MC samples.

outputs.name: Per-Sample Recovery %
outputs.desc: All M recovery values.

outputs.name: Per-Sample Psi (deg)
outputs.desc: All M psi values.

name: Geodesic Voronoi (CGAL)
description: Split a mesh surface into Voronoi cells driven by  geodesic distance from user-supplied seed points (Crane  et al. Heat Method 2013). Each seed snaps to its nearest  vertex; each face joins the cell of the seed with the  shortest on-surface distance. Cuts follow surface  curvature - neat boundaries on curved meshes where  Euclidean Voronoi would slice through the form.  Wraps CGAL Heat_method_3.

algorithm.title: Heat-method geodesic distance
algorithm.citation: CGAL Heat_method_3 (Crane-Weischedel-Wardetzky heat method)

inputs.name: Mesh
inputs.desc: Input surface (2-manifold gives a stable cotangent Laplacian).

inputs.name: Seeds
inputs.desc: Seed points - each is snapped to the nearest mesh vertex.  Place 5-50 for a useful tessellation.

inputs.name: Run
inputs.desc: Set true to compute.

outputs.name: Cells
outputs.desc: One mesh per geodesic Voronoi cell.

outputs.name: Cell Count
outputs.desc: Number of non-empty cells (== seed count when input is a single connected component).

outputs.name: Available
outputs.desc: True iff CGAL shim loaded.

outputs.name: Report
outputs.desc: Diagnostic report.

name: Mesh CSG (CGAL)
description: Boolean operation between two meshes via the CGAL native  shim. Falls back transparently to in-tree BSP CSG when the  shim is absent. Reports which back-end actually ran.  Wraps CGAL corefine_and_compute_boolean_operations.

algorithm.title: Mesh corefinement boolean
algorithm.citation: CGAL Polygon Mesh Processing (corefine_and_compute_boolean_operations)

inputs.name: Mesh A
inputs.desc: First operand.

inputs.name: Mesh B
inputs.desc: Second operand.

inputs.name: Operation
inputs.desc: 0 = Union, 1 = Intersection, 2 = Difference (A − B).

inputs.name: Use Hybrid Kernel
inputs.desc: False = EPICK only (default, fast). True = HYBRID — EPICK  storage + EPECK intersection construction. Use Hybrid when  inputs may be numerically fragile (multi-cut chains,  near-tangent contacts).

inputs.name: Run
inputs.desc: Set true to compute.

outputs.name: Result
outputs.desc: Result mesh.

outputs.name: Backend
outputs.desc: Which kernel ran: 'CGAL' or 'ManagedBsp'.

outputs.name: Available
outputs.desc: True iff the CGAL native shim is loadable.

outputs.name: Version
outputs.desc: Reported version string from the shim.

outputs.name: Report
outputs.desc: Diagnostic report (timing, kernel, fallback note).

name: Mesh Decimate (Auto)
description: Mesh decimation via the best available backend (Geogram  vertex-clustering preferred; CGAL edge-collapse fallback).  Single ratio in (0,1) is mapped to backend-specific params.

inputs.name: Mesh
inputs.desc: Input mesh.

inputs.name: Target Ratio
inputs.desc: In (0, 1). Higher = more detail kept. Mapped to bin count  (Geogram) or edge-count ratio (CGAL).

inputs.name: Run
inputs.desc: Set true to compute.

outputs.name: Decimated
outputs.desc: Decimated mesh.

outputs.name: Backend
outputs.desc: Which backend ran.

outputs.name: Diagnostics
outputs.desc: Loaded shim versions.

outputs.name: Report
outputs.desc: Diagnostic report.

name: Mesh Decimate (CGAL)
description: Mesh simplification via CGAL Surface_mesh_simplification  (quadric-error edge collapse, Lindstrom-Turk policies).  Three stop modes: count ratio, target edge count, edge  length. Run before CoACD to speed up decomposition on  scanned statue input.  Wraps CGAL Surface_mesh_simplification (Lindstrom-Turk policies).

algorithm.title: Quadric edge-collapse simplification
algorithm.citation: CGAL Surface_mesh_simplification (Lindstrom-Turk edge-collapse policies)

inputs.name: Mesh
inputs.desc: Input mesh. Should be a valid 2-manifold for stable results.

inputs.name: Stop Kind
inputs.desc: 0 = count ratio (remaining/initial, value in (0, 1)). Most common.\n 1 = target edge count (>= 1).\n 2 = minimum edge length (> 0); preserves edges shorter than the  threshold (good for keeping sharp features).

inputs.name: Stop Value
inputs.desc:
Threshold meaning depends on Stop Kind:
   Kind 0: 0.5 = halve edge count.
   Kind 1: 5000 = stop at 5000 edges.
   Kind 2: 0.05 = stop when next edge to collapse is >= 0.05.

inputs.name: Run
inputs.desc: Set true to compute.

outputs.name: Decimated
outputs.desc: Decimated mesh.

outputs.name: Available
outputs.desc: True iff the CGAL native shim is loadable.

outputs.name: Report
outputs.desc: Diagnostic report (V/F counts in/out, runtime).

name: Mesh Decimate (Geogram)
description: Vertex-clustering decimation via Geogram  (GEO::mesh_decimate_vertex_clustering). Voxel-bin algorithm:  higher Bins = more detail. Different from CGAL's edge-collapse  decimation - use this for very high-poly scans where you want  controlled spatial sampling, and CGAL's for precise count  targeting.  Wraps Geogram mesh_decimate_vertex_clustering.

algorithm.title: Vertex-clustering decimation
algorithm.citation: Levy, B., INRIA/ALICE. Geogram v1.9.9. BSD-3. https://github.com/BrunoLevy/geogram

inputs.name: Mesh
inputs.desc: Input mesh.

inputs.name: Bins
inputs.desc: Voxel grid resolution per bbox dimension. Higher = more  detail (less aggressive decimation). Typical 50..300;  default 100. Minimum 2.

inputs.name: Mode
inputs.desc:
Bitwise OR of mode flags:
   0 = FAST (no extra cleanup)
   1 = REMOVE_DUPLICATES
   2 = REMOVE_DEGREE_3
   4 = KEEP_BORDERS
   7 = DEFAULT (1|2|4)

inputs.name: Run
inputs.desc: Set true to compute.

outputs.name: Decimated
outputs.desc: Decimated mesh.

outputs.name: Backend
outputs.desc: Reported version from the loaded shim.

outputs.name: Available
outputs.desc: True iff the Geogram native shim is loadable.

outputs.name: Report
outputs.desc: Diagnostic report (V/F counts in/out, runtime).

name: Mesh Decompose (CoACD)
description: Approximate convex decomposition via the CoACD native shim.  Input must be 2-manifold for the lightweight build (no  manifold preprocess); pre-clean with Mesh Repair (CGAL) if  input is non-manifold and the OpenVDB-equipped build is not  loaded.  Wraps CoACD (Wei et al. 2022).

algorithm.title: Collision-aware approximate convex decomposition
algorithm.citation: Wei, J., Liu, M., Wang, J. et al. (2022). Approximate Convex Decomposition for 3D Meshes with Collision-Aware Concavity and Tree Search. SIGGRAPH 2022

inputs.name: Mesh
inputs.desc: Input mesh. Must be 2-manifold when running against the  lightweight (WITH_3RD_PARTY_LIBS=OFF) shim build. The full  build accepts non-manifold input via OpenVDB preprocessing.

inputs.name: Threshold
inputs.desc: Concavity threshold. Lower = more pieces, finer fit.  Default 0.05 (normalized [0..1]) or 0.05 metres if Real  Metric is true.

inputs.name: Max Hulls
inputs.desc: Cap on output piece count. -1 = unlimited.

inputs.name: Preprocess
inputs.desc: 0 = auto, 1 = on, 2 = off. Auto runs OpenVDB-based  manifold-isation only when input is non-manifold (requires  WITH_3RD_PARTY_LIBS=ON build).

inputs.name: Real Metric
inputs.desc: When true, Threshold is interpreted as metres rather than  CoACD's normalized [0..1] units. Use for statue-scale input.

inputs.name: Run
inputs.desc: Set true to compute.

inputs.name: MCTS Iters
inputs.desc: MCTS iterations per cut (default 150).

inputs.name: MCTS Depth
inputs.desc: MCTS tree depth (default 3).

inputs.name: MCTS Nodes
inputs.desc: MCTS nodes per cut (default 20).

inputs.name: Seed
inputs.desc: RNG seed for reproducibility (default 0).

inputs.name: PCA
inputs.desc: Align cuts to PCA frame (default false). World-axis cuts  are usually better for architectural input.

outputs.name: Convex Hulls
outputs.desc: List of convex pieces approximating the input.

outputs.name: Count
outputs.desc: Number of hulls produced.

outputs.name: Runtime
outputs.desc: Runtime in milliseconds.

outputs.name: Backend
outputs.desc: Reported version + build-flag status from the loaded shim.  Use this to confirm whether OpenVDB-based manifold  preprocessing is available.

outputs.name: Available
outputs.desc: True iff the CoACD native shim is loadable.

outputs.name: Report
outputs.desc: Diagnostic report (input/output sizes, runtime, parameters).

name: Mesh Fill Holes (Geogram)
description: Triangulate open boundary loops smaller than a size threshold.  Use it to close sliver-holes in a Voronoi cell sub-mesh while  keeping the main outer boundary open - exactly what BFF needs  to flatten without self-overlap. BSD-3 (GEO::fill_holes).  Wraps Geogram fill_holes.

algorithm.title: Boundary-loop hole filling
algorithm.citation: Levy, B., INRIA/ALICE. Geogram v1.9.9. BSD-3. https://github.com/BrunoLevy/geogram

inputs.name: Mesh
inputs.desc: Input mesh (open patch).

inputs.name: Max Area
inputs.desc: Maximum hole AREA (input units squared) to fill. 0 = fill  nothing. A very large value (1e30) fills every hole.

inputs.name: Max Edges
inputs.desc: Maximum boundary edges per hole. 0 = no edge limit (size  governed by area alone). Set to ~30 to target only  sliver-style holes that have few edges.

inputs.name: Repair After
inputs.desc: Run mesh_repair (DEFAULT mode) after filling to clean up  duplicate vertices / facets the hole triangulator may  leave behind.

inputs.name: Run
inputs.desc: Set true to compute.

outputs.name: Filled
outputs.desc: Mesh with small holes triangulated.

outputs.name: Available
outputs.desc: True iff Geogram shim loaded.

outputs.name: Report
outputs.desc: Diagnostic report.

name: Mesh Remesh (Geogram)
description: Uniform surface remeshing via centroidal-Voronoi-driven  Lloyd + Newton optimization (GEO::remesh_smooth). Accepts a direct  Mesh OR a File Path (.ply / .obj / .stl / .wrl; takes precedence).  Runs on a background thread (Run gate) so the canvas never freezes.  Wraps Geogram remesh_smooth.

algorithm.title: Centroidal-Voronoi remeshing
algorithm.citation: Levy, B., INRIA/ALICE. Geogram v1.9.9. BSD-3. https://github.com/BrunoLevy/geogram

inputs.name: Mesh
inputs.desc: Input mesh (optional if File Path is given).

inputs.name: Points
inputs.desc: Desired vertex count in output (5000..50000 typical).

inputs.name: Lloyd Iters
inputs.desc: Lloyd relaxation iterations (default 5).

inputs.name: Newton Iters
inputs.desc: Newton iterations after Lloyd (default 30).

inputs.name: Run
inputs.desc: Set true to compute.

inputs.name: File Path
inputs.desc: Optional mesh file to remesh directly (.ply / .obj / .stl / .wrl).  Takes precedence over the Mesh input. Empty = use the Mesh input.

outputs.name: Remeshed
outputs.desc: Remeshed surface.

outputs.name: Available
outputs.desc: True iff Geogram shim loaded.

outputs.name: Report
outputs.desc: Diagnostic report.

name: Mesh Repair (Auto)

inputs.name: Mesh

inputs.name: Run

outputs.name: Repaired
outputs.desc: Repaired mesh.

outputs.name: Backend
outputs.desc: Which backend ran: Geogram, Cgal, or None.

outputs.name: Diagnostics

description: Robust mesh repair via CGAL Polygon Mesh Processing.  Triangulates non-triangle faces, stitches coincident  borders, removes degenerate triangles, and orients faces  outward when the mesh is closed.  Wraps CGAL PMP repair routines.

algorithm.citation: CGAL Polygon Mesh Processing (repair package)

name: Mesh Repair (Geogram)
description: Topology-aware mesh repair via GEO::mesh_repair  (colocate + remove duplicate facets + triangulate). BSD-3.  Wraps Geogram mesh_repair.

algorithm.title: Geogram mesh repair
algorithm.citation: Levy, B., INRIA/ALICE. Geogram v1.9.9. BSD-3. https://github.com/BrunoLevy/geogram

inputs.desc: Input mesh.

inputs.desc:
Bitwise OR of GeogramRepairMode flags:
   0 = TopologyOnly   (always done; dissociate non-manifold)

   7 = Default = 1|2|4

inputs.desc: Tolerance for COLOCATE merge (0 = exact only). Match scene units.

name: Mesh Segmentation (CGAL SDF)
description: Surface mesh segmentation via Shape Diameter Function.  Cuts at concave features (deep folds, narrow necks); the  tried-and-tested CGAL Surface_mesh_segmentation pipeline.  Returns one mesh per segment. NOT a Voronoi-style spatial  split: convex inputs collapse to one segment.  Wraps CGAL Surface_mesh_segmentation (SDF).

algorithm.title: SDF-based mesh segmentation
algorithm.citation: CGAL Surface_mesh_segmentation (Shape Diameter Function graph-cut)

inputs.name: Mesh
inputs.desc: Input surface (2-manifold gives best results).

inputs.name: Clusters
inputs.desc: Target number of segments (>= 2). CGAL's example uses 5.

inputs.name: Smoothing
inputs.desc: Graph-cut smoothness penalty in [0, 1]. Higher = more  spatially coherent / fewer islands. CGAL default 0.26.

inputs.name: Cone Angle
inputs.desc: SDF inward cone half-angle (radians). 0 = CGAL default  (2/3 * pi, ~120 degrees).

inputs.name: Rays
inputs.desc: Rays per facet for SDF estimation. 0 = CGAL default (25).  More rays = smoother SDF, slower compute.

inputs.name: Postprocess
inputs.desc: Run CGAL's SDF postprocess (smoothing + connected-component  cleanup). Recommended.

inputs.name: Run
inputs.desc: Set true to compute.

outputs.name: Segments

outputs.name: Available
outputs.desc: True iff CGAL shim loaded.

outputs.name: Report
outputs.desc: Diagnostic report.

name: Mesh Segmentation by Angle (CGAL)
description: Cluster mesh faces by dihedral-angle change. Detects  sharp edges (where adjacent face normals deviate by more  than the threshold) and flood-fills the rest into smooth  regions. Returns one mesh per region.  Tuning: 5-15 deg = strict planarity, 30-60 deg = smooth- band detection, 90+ = only orthogonal-ish creases.  Wraps CGAL detect_sharp_edges.

inputs.desc: Input surface (2-manifold gives stable dihedral computation).

inputs.desc: Dihedral angle threshold in DEGREES, in (0, 180). Edges  whose dihedral angle exceeds this become segment boundaries.  Try 30-45 for smooth bands on curved forms.

inputs.name: Run
inputs.desc: Set true to compute.

outputs.name: Segments
outputs.desc: One mesh per smoothly-connected region.

outputs.name: Segment Count

outputs.name: Available

outputs.name: Kerf Time Min -- Psi (deg)
outputs.desc: Min-kerf-time psi per zone.

outputs.name: BCSdbBV Min
outputs.desc: Min BCSdbBV cost (Jalalian) per zone.

outputs.name: BCSdbBV Min -- Psi (deg)
outputs.desc: Min-BCSdbBV psi per zone.

outputs.name: Pareto Front Size
outputs.desc: Number of non-dominated points per zone.

outputs.name: Total Evaluations
outputs.desc: Sum of (psi, dx, dy) samples evaluated.

outputs.name: Elapsed (ms)
outputs.desc: Wall-clock duration.

name: Polygon Partition (CGAL)
description: Decompose a 2D simple polygon into convex sub-polygons or  y-monotone pieces via CGAL Partition_2.  Wraps CGAL Partition_2 (Hertel-Mehlhorn and Greene).

algorithm.title: Convex polygon partition
algorithm.citation: CGAL Partition_2 (Hertel-Mehlhorn approximate + Greene optimal convex decomposition)

inputs.name: Polygon
inputs.desc: Closed planar simple polygon (no holes).

inputs.name: Kind
inputs.desc: 0 = approximate convex (Hertel-Mehlhorn, fast).  1 = optimal convex (Greene, O(n^4) but minimal pieces).  2 = y-monotone partition.

inputs.name: Tolerance
inputs.desc: Curve-to-polyline tolerance.

inputs.name: Run
inputs.desc: Set true to compute.

outputs.name: Pieces
outputs.desc: Sub-polygons as closed polylines.

outputs.name: Piece Count
outputs.desc: Number of pieces.

outputs.name: Available
outputs.desc: True iff the CGAL native shim is loadable.

outputs.name: Report
outputs.desc: Diagnostic report.

name: Straight Skeleton (CGAL)
description: Interior straight skeleton of a 2D polygon (with optional  holes) via CGAL Straight_skeleton_2. Outer ring CCW, holes  CW; the shim auto-reverses if winding is wrong.  Wraps CGAL Straight_skeleton_2.

algorithm.title: Straight skeleton
algorithm.citation: CGAL Straight_skeleton_2 (Aichholzer-Aurenhammer straight skeleton)

inputs.name: Outer
inputs.desc: Closed planar polyline / curve. The outer ring of the polygon.

inputs.name: Holes
inputs.desc: Optional closed planar curves treated as holes.

inputs.name: Tolerance
inputs.desc: Curve-to-polyline tolerance.

inputs.name: Run
inputs.desc: Set true to compute.

outputs.name: Edges
outputs.desc: Skeleton edges as 2D lines (Z = 0).

outputs.name: Vertices
outputs.desc: Skeleton + boundary vertex positions.

outputs.name: Times
outputs.desc: Time-of-arrival per vertex (boundary = 0; interior > 0).

outputs.name: Available
outputs.desc: True iff the CGAL native shim is loadable.

outputs.name: Report
outputs.desc: Diagnostic report.

name: Tetrahedralize (Geogram)
description: Volumetric tetrahedral mesh of a closed surface via  GEO::mesh_tetrahedralize. NOTE: requires the shim to be  built with GEOGRAM_WITH_TETGEN=ON. Default build has it  OFF for BSD-3 license cleanliness; in that mode this  component returns a clear error pointing at the rebuild.  Wraps Geogram mesh_tetrahedralize (TetGen).

algorithm.title: Constrained Delaunay tetrahedralisation
algorithm.citation: Levy, B., INRIA/ALICE. Geogram v1.9.9. BSD-3. https://github.com/BrunoLevy/geogram

inputs.name: Mesh
inputs.desc: Closed input surface.

inputs.name: Preprocess
inputs.desc: Clean input first.

inputs.name: Refine
inputs.desc: Insert Steiner points to improve quality.

inputs.name: Quality
inputs.desc: Element quality target [1.0..2.0]; 1.0 = max.

inputs.name: Keep Regions
inputs.desc: Keep all internal regions (else outermost only).

inputs.name: Run
inputs.desc: Set true to compute.

outputs.name: Tet Cells
outputs.desc: One mesh per tet (4 boundary triangles each).

outputs.name: Tet Count
outputs.desc: Number of tetrahedra.

outputs.name: Available
outputs.desc: True iff Geogram shim loaded.

outputs.name: Report
outputs.desc: Diagnostic report.

name: VTU Export
description: Run BlockCutOpt then dump the optimal cutting grid to a  ParaView .vtu file. Two cell sets: cell_status=1 (non- intersected, ready-to-quarry), cell_status=0 (intersected,  discarded). Matches BlockCutOpt 2020 Figures 3 and 6.

inputs.name: Tested Area
inputs.desc: Bench bounding box (m).

inputs.name: Fractures
inputs.desc: Fracture mesh.

inputs.name: VTU Path
inputs.desc: Output .vtu file path.

inputs.name: Block X
inputs.desc: Block length (m).

inputs.name: Block Y
inputs.desc: Block width (m).

inputs.name: Block Z
inputs.desc: Block height (m).

inputs.name: Kerf
inputs.desc: Material-lost-by-quarrying (m).

inputs.name: Psi Step (deg)
inputs.desc: Angular search step.

inputs.name: Write
inputs.desc: Trigger; set true to write the file.

outputs.name: Non-Intersected
outputs.desc: Cell count tagged status=1.

outputs.name: Intersected
outputs.desc: Cell count tagged status=0.

outputs.name: Recovery %
outputs.desc: Recovery percent at the winning (psi, dx, dy).

outputs.name: Written Path
outputs.desc: Path written, or empty when Write=false.

name: Voronoi Block Partition (Geogram)
description: Partition a surface mesh into N Voronoi cells given seed  points (use CVT Seeds upstream for uniform-area cells).  Output is one Mesh per cell. Quarry-pipeline use:  Statue → Decimate → Repair → Remesh → CVT → this  → BlockGraph → GeoCut → QuarryCutOpt.  Wraps Geogram restricted Voronoi diagram.

algorithm.title: Restricted Voronoi diagram partition
algorithm.citation: Levy, B., INRIA/ALICE. Geogram v1.9.9. BSD-3. https://github.com/BrunoLevy/geogram

inputs.name: Mesh
inputs.desc: Input surface.

inputs.name: Seeds
inputs.desc: Seed points (use CVT Seeds for optimized seeds).

inputs.name: Run
inputs.desc: Set true to compute.

inputs.name: Smooth Pts
inputs.desc: Pre-RVD uniform remesh target vertex count (0 = off,  5_000..50_000 typical). Smooths cell-boundary sawtooth.

inputs.name: Closed
inputs.desc: If true, return CLOSED Voronoi blocks (volumetric mode,  input must be a closed solid). If false, surface partition.

outputs.name: Cells
outputs.desc: One mesh per Voronoi cell.

outputs.name: Cell Count
outputs.desc: Number of non-empty cells produced.

outputs.name: Available
outputs.desc: True iff Geogram shim loaded.

outputs.name: Report
outputs.desc: Diagnostic report.

name: Ashlar Pack
description: Lays convex Slabs into a coursed-ashlar wall (running bond).  Emits a MasonryAssembly with bottom-row blocks fixed and an  AshlarPackResult carrying coverage / leftovers / notes.

algorithm.title: Ashlar coursed wall layout
algorithm.citation: Frahan-original 3D grid stacking with running bond

inputs.name: Wall Width
inputs.desc: Wall length along +X (units of the active Rhino document,  typically meters). Must be > 0. Recommended: wire a Wall  Frame instead and leave this at default.

inputs.name: Wall Height
inputs.desc: Wall height along +Z. Must be > 0. Default 1.0.

inputs.name: Wall Thickness
inputs.desc: Wall thickness along +Y. Must be > 0. Default 0.20 (typical  single-leaf masonry).

inputs.name: Meshes
inputs.desc: Block inventory as Rhino meshes (e.g. from Quarry DFN, Slab  Cut By Fractures, or hand-authored). Each mesh must be convex  with at least 4 vertices and 4 faces. The packer auto-converts  to its internal Slab DTO.

inputs.name: Course Mode
inputs.desc: Layout strategy. 0 = CoursedAshlar (uniform course height;  one block size). 1 = CoursedRubble (multi-bin: each course  uses one block-height bin; mixes block sizes across courses).

inputs.name: Target Course Height
inputs.desc: Course height for CoursedAshlar. For CoursedRubble it seeds  the first bin height. Should match the Z-extent of your  blocks within Height Tolerance. Default 0.15.

inputs.name: Bed Joint
inputs.desc: Vertical mortar gap between courses (units of the document).  >= 0. Default 0.001 (1 mm typical lime-mortar gap).

inputs.name: Head Joint
inputs.desc: Horizontal mortar gap between adjacent blocks in a course.  >= 0. Default 0.001 (1 mm).

inputs.name: Stagger Offset
inputs.desc: Running-bond shift on odd courses, as a fraction of the  average block width. In [0, 1]. Default 0.5 (half-bond).

inputs.name: Density
inputs.desc: Material density in kg/m³ (or any consistent mass-per-volume  unit). > 0. Default 2400 (typical limestone). Used by the  downstream stability solver to compute self-weight.

inputs.name: Height Tolerance
inputs.desc: Block-height tolerance for inventory filtering. >= 0. Default  0.05 (5 cm — accommodates rough-cut quarry blocks).

inputs.name: Wall Frame
inputs.desc: Optional WallFrame DTO from Wall Frame component. When wired,  this overrides the primitive Wall Width / Height / Thickness  inputs above. Recommended for clean canvas wiring.

inputs.name: Options
inputs.desc: Optional AshlarPackOptions DTO from Ashlar Pack Options  component. When wired, overrides Course Mode / Course Height  / joints / stagger / density / tolerance. Recommended for  clean canvas wiring.

inputs.name: Start Plane
inputs.desc: Optional start plane. Engine stays in world XY (correct for  the stability solver, which assumes gravity = -Z). The  component emits a Display Transform that maps world XY into  this plane; wire it into AssemblyPreview / mesh Transform  to re-orient the wall visually.

outputs.name: Assembly
outputs.desc: MasonryAssembly with bottom-course blocks fixed. Wire into  Masonry Stability (RBE).

outputs.name: Result
outputs.desc: AshlarPackResult carrying coverage / leftovers / notes / placed  blocks. Wire into Pack Diagnostics (Stage 3).

outputs.name: Display Transform
outputs.desc: World-XY → Start Plane transform. Identity when Start Plane  is not wired. Wire into mesh / preview transforms.

name: Ashlar Pack Options
description: Bundles ashlar packer knobs (course mode, joints, stagger,  density, height tolerance) into an AshlarPackOptions DTO.  Wire into Ashlar Pack's optional Options input.

inputs.name: Course Mode
inputs.desc: Layout strategy. 0 = CoursedAshlar (single block-height bin,  uniform courses). 1 = CoursedRubble (multi-bin, mixes block  sizes across courses). Default 0.

inputs.name: Course Height
inputs.desc: Target course height in document units (typically meters).  Must match the Z-extent of your blocks within Height Tolerance.  Default 0.15.

inputs.name: Bed Joint
inputs.desc: Vertical mortar gap between courses, in document units. >= 0.  Default 0.001 (1 mm typical).

inputs.name: Head Joint
inputs.desc: Horizontal mortar gap between adjacent blocks, in document  units. >= 0. Default 0.001.

inputs.name: Stagger Offset
inputs.desc: Running-bond shift on odd courses, as a fraction of the  average block width. In [0, 1]. Default 0.5 (half-bond,  the standard).

inputs.name: Density
inputs.desc: Material density in kg/m³ (or consistent mass-per-volume  unit). > 0. Default 2400 (typical limestone). Used by the  downstream stability solver.

inputs.name: Height Tolerance
inputs.desc: Block-height tolerance for inventory filtering and rubble  binning, in document units. >= 0. Default 0.05 (5 cm —  accommodates rough-cut quarry blocks).

outputs.name: Options
outputs.desc: AshlarPackOptions DTO bundling all algorithmic knobs. Wire  into Ashlar Pack's Options input — when wired, it overrides  the equivalent primitive inputs on the packer.

name: Assembly Preview
description: Visualizes a MasonryAssembly. Outputs one Mesh per block  (separated into free + fixed lists), the IDs of each, and  per-interface contact polylines + centroids + normals for  debugging.

inputs.name: Assembly
inputs.desc: MasonryAssembly DTO (from Masonry Assembly or Ashlar Pack).

outputs.name: Free Meshes
outputs.desc: Block meshes for blocks that are NOT fixed by boundary conditions.

outputs.name: Fixed Meshes
outputs.desc: Block meshes for blocks that ARE fixed (grounded).

outputs.name: Free Block Ids
outputs.desc: IDs of the free blocks (parallel to Free Meshes).

outputs.name: Fixed Block Ids
outputs.desc: IDs of the fixed blocks (parallel to Fixed Meshes).

outputs.name: Contact Polylines
outputs.desc: One closed polyline per MasonryInterface — the contact  polygon as drawn on the canvas.

outputs.name: Contact Centroids
outputs.desc: Centroid of each contact polygon. Use to anchor labels  or to draw normals.

outputs.name: Contact Normals
outputs.desc: Surface normal at each contact, pointing from block A to  block B (Frahan convention).

outputs.name: Contact Pairs
outputs.desc: Per-interface 'aId -> bId' string for hover-debugging.

name: Auto Interfaces
description: Detects face-face contacts between a list of placed Slabs  and emits the corresponding MasonryInterfaces. Wire output  into Masonry Assembly's Interfaces input. NOTE: generated  walls carry exact joints via the Assembly output — detection  is only needed for imported geometry; for scan-derived meshes  use Robust Auto Interfaces.

algorithm.title: Interface auto-detector
algorithm.citation: Frahan-original proximity-based pairwise contact detection

inputs.name: Meshes
inputs.desc: Block meshes in their final placed positions. Standard Rhino  mesh wires; this component finds face-face contacts between them.

inputs.name: Block Ids
inputs.desc: One ID per slab in the same order. Must match the IDs used  to construct MasonryBlocks.

inputs.name: Distance Tolerance
inputs.desc: Max distance between coplanar faces (>= 0).

inputs.name: Angle Tolerance Deg
inputs.desc: Max angle between antiparallel face normals, in degrees (>= 0, < 90).

outputs.name: Interfaces
outputs.desc: Detected MasonryInterfaces. Wire into Masonry Assembly.

name: Best Fit Pack
description: Inventory-aware ashlar packing. For each placement slot,  scores every remaining stone by width / depth / height /  aspect-ratio fit and picks the highest-scoring candidate.  Companion to Ashlar Pack (which uses first-fit). Recommended  for heterogeneous quarry inventories where stone sizes vary. [Gramazio et al. 2017]

algorithm.title: Best-fit rubble inventory placement
algorithm.citation: Gramazio Kohler Eichenhofer 2017 NCCR Digital Fabrication

inputs.name: Wall Width
inputs.desc: Wall length along +X. Must be > 0. Recommended: wire a  Wall Frame instead.

inputs.name: Wall Height
inputs.desc: Wall height along +Z. Must be > 0.

inputs.name: Wall Thickness
inputs.desc: Wall thickness along +Y. Must be > 0.

inputs.name: Inventory
inputs.desc: Block inventory as Rhino meshes. Each mesh becomes a  candidate stone for best-fit selection.

inputs.name: Course Height
inputs.desc: Target course height. Must match the Z-extent of your  blocks within Height Tolerance. Default 0.15.

inputs.name: Bed Joint
inputs.desc: Vertical mortar gap between courses. Default 0.001.

inputs.name: Head Joint
inputs.desc: Horizontal mortar gap between blocks. Default 0.001.

inputs.name: Stagger Offset
inputs.desc: Running-bond shift on odd courses, fraction of average  block width. Default 0.5.

inputs.name: Density
inputs.desc: Material density (kg/m³). Default 2400.

inputs.name: Height Tolerance
inputs.desc: Block-height tolerance for inventory filtering. Default 0.05.

inputs.name: Wall Frame
inputs.desc: Optional WallFrame DTO. Overrides primitive Wall W/H/T.

inputs.name: Options
inputs.desc: Optional AshlarPackOptions DTO. Overrides primitive  algorithmic inputs.

inputs.name: Start Plane
inputs.desc: Optional start plane. Engine stays in world XY (correct for  the stability solver). Component emits a Display Transform  that maps world XY into this plane for visual re-orientation.

outputs.name: Assembly
outputs.desc: MasonryAssembly with bottom-course blocks fixed.

outputs.name: Result
outputs.desc: AshlarPackResult — coverage / leftovers / notes / placed blocks.

outputs.name: Display Transform
outputs.desc: World-XY → Start Plane transform. Identity when unset.

name: Block Build Order
description: Computes a physically valid build order for a masonry  assembly. A block is placed only after every block it  rests on is already placed. Layer = course number  (longest support path from ground). [Kim et al. 2024]

algorithm.title: Support-DAG topological install order
algorithm.citation: Kim et al. 2024, ASME IDETC/CIE DETC2024-142563 Polygonal masonry install-order DAG

inputs.name: Assembly
inputs.desc: MasonryAssembly DTO.

inputs.name: Up Vector
inputs.desc: Direction the courses stack along. Default world Z.

inputs.name: Up Tolerance Deg
inputs.desc: An interface counts as a bed joint when its normal is  within this many degrees of the up axis. Head joints /  vertical contacts beyond this tolerance contribute no  support constraint. Default 30°.

outputs.name: Ordered Block Ids
outputs.desc: Block ids in build order (lowest course first).

outputs.name: Ordered Meshes
outputs.desc: Block meshes in build order, parallel to Ordered Block Ids.

outputs.name: Order Index
outputs.desc: 0-based placement index per block, parallel to Ordered  Block Ids. Equals the list index — exposed for downstream  components that rebuild the order.

outputs.name: Layer
outputs.desc: Course number (longest support path). 0 = ground course.  Useful for colour-by-course visualisation.

name: Block Graph Coloring
description: 4-colours the contact graph of a MasonryAssembly: no two  blocks sharing an interface get the same colour. Output  is one integer per block (0-3 typically; up to 7 for  non-planar topologies). Wire into native colour-mapping  to drive visualization or material assignment. [Welsh & Powell 1967]

algorithm.title: Welsh-Powell graph coloring
algorithm.citation: Welsh and Powell 1967, Computer Journal 10(1):85-86

inputs.name: Assembly
inputs.desc: MasonryAssembly with blocks + interfaces.

outputs.name: Block Ids
outputs.desc: Block identifiers, in iteration order.

outputs.name: Colour
outputs.desc: Per-block colour index (0-based). Same length and order as  the Block Ids output.

outputs.name: Colours Used
outputs.desc: Total number of distinct colours used. Should be <= 4 for  planar contact graphs (4-Colour Theorem).

name: Block Ground Transforms
description: Recovers the rigid transform per placed block. Wire  Source Meshes (canonical) and Placed Meshes (post-  assembly) for vertex-paired Horn QAO recovery, OR wire  Existing Transforms for direct pass-through. Output  transforms are expressed in the Ground Plane's frame  (default: world XY). Implements Horn QAO (Horn 1987).

algorithm.title: Closed-form absolute orientation (Horn QAO)
algorithm.citation: Horn 1987, Closed-form solution of absolute orientation using unit quaternions, JOSA A 4(4):629-642

inputs.name: Placed Meshes
inputs.desc: Meshes after assembly (one per block). The recovered  transform takes the matching Source Mesh to this pose.

inputs.name: Source Meshes
inputs.desc: Canonical / pre-placement meshes (one per block, parallel  to Placed). Required when Existing Transforms is empty.  Vertex count and order must match the placed mesh.

inputs.name: Existing Transforms
inputs.desc: Optional pre-known transforms (parallel to Placed). When  supplied at index i, this transform is passed through  verbatim and Horn QAO is skipped for that block.

inputs.name: Ground Plane
inputs.desc: Reference frame. Output transforms are re-expressed in  this plane's local basis. Default: world XY.

outputs.name: Transforms
outputs.desc: Per-block rigid transform (canonical → placed) in the  ground-plane frame.

outputs.name: Origins
outputs.desc: Where each block's local origin lands. Useful for  anchoring labels on the canvas.

outputs.name: RMS
outputs.desc: Per-block Horn QAO residual (root-mean-square of  vertex-pair distances after fit). 0.0 for pass-through.

outputs.name: Status
outputs.desc: 'passthrough' / 'kabsch (V=…)' / 'failed: …' per block.

name: Block Pack (Tree)
description: Pack sculpture/element cuboids into stone-block containers  with axis-aligned guillotine cuts. Frahan port of Kim 2025  (Computation 13:211, CC BY 4.0). Picks the cheapest subset  of containers that fits all elements; falls back to highest  packed-value when full packing is infeasible. Three  extensions beyond the paper: deterministic seed, saw kerf  width, and Forbidden Boxes per container.

algorithm.title: Tree-forest guillotine pack
algorithm.citation: Kim 2025 Computation 13:211

inputs.name: Elements
inputs.desc: Element AABBs (sculpture / final-piece bounding boxes).  Only the box dimensions are used for the fit test; the  Box.Plane defines the element's source pose.

inputs.name: Element Values
inputs.desc: Per-element value (e.g. piece price). Must match the  element count.

inputs.name: Containers
inputs.desc: Container AABBs (stone-block bounding boxes).

inputs.name: Container Prices
inputs.desc: Per-container price (e.g. stone-block material cost).  Must match the container count.

inputs.name: Rotation Mode
inputs.desc: 0 = None (identity only), 1 = OneAxis (vein-aligned),  2 = ThreeAxis (six 90° rotations).

inputs.name: Forests
inputs.desc: Number of independent randomised forests to grow. Score  plateaus by f ≈ 50–1000 on small instances; large jobs  may need 10⁴–10⁶ forests (see paper §4).

inputs.name: Seed
inputs.desc: Master seed (Frahan extension beyond Kim 2025). Forest k  uses (seed + k) internally; setting the same seed gives  the same result. Default 0 is deterministic.

inputs.name: Kerf Width
inputs.desc: Saw kerf width in model units (Frahan extension). Each  axis-aligned cut consumes this much material along its  direction. Real values: 5–10 mm for diamond wire saws,  1–3 mm for thin blades. Default 0.

inputs.name: Forbidden Boxes
