inputs.desc: Optional flat list of forbidden Box regions inside any  container (Frahan extension; closes Kim §8.2 gap on  fracture-aware containers). Elements that overlap a  forbidden region in their target container are rejected.  A forbidden box outside all containers has no effect.

inputs.name: Cut Surface Weight
inputs.desc: K2 / Jalalian I11 (BCSdbBV) extension. Score subtracts  weight × Σ(internal-face area) across placements. Default 0  preserves the original Kim 2025 score.

inputs.name: Max Parallelism
inputs.desc: K2 parallel-forest extension. 0 = auto (Environment. ProcessorCount). 1 forces serial. Parallel results are  bitwise identical to serial because each forest's RNG is  seeded independently.

inputs.name: Memory Budget MB
inputs.desc: K2 memory-cap extension. When > 0, Forests is  automatically reduced so f × ~1.4 KB × element-count ≤ budget.  0 = unlimited.

outputs.name: Placed Boxes
outputs.desc: Placed element AABBs in world-frame coordinates.

outputs.name: Transforms
outputs.desc: World-frame transform per placed element (apply to the  source element Box to recover the placed pose, including  any rotation).

outputs.name: Placed Element Ids
outputs.desc: Index into the input element list for each placement, in  placement order. Compare against the input element count  to find unpacked elements.

outputs.name: Placed Container Ids
outputs.desc: Index into the input container list for each placement.

outputs.name: Used Containers
outputs.desc: Sorted unique indices of containers that hold at least one  placed element.

outputs.name: Score
outputs.desc: Score of the winning forest (Kim 2025 §2.4): sum of packed  element values, plus 1/(1+containerPrice) bonus when all  elements fit.

outputs.name: All Packed
outputs.desc: True iff every input element landed in a container.

outputs.name: Best Forest
outputs.desc: Index of the winning forest (0 ≤ index < Forests).

outputs.name: Report
outputs.desc: Human-readable summary.

name: Block Size Distribution
description: Diagnostic stats over a list of slab volumes. Use to QA  quarry decomposition: high CV (> 1) signals the joint-set  parameters need retuning. Outlier fence per Tukey 1977 (EDA).

algorithm.title: Descriptive statistics + Tukey-fence outlier rule
algorithm.citation: Tukey 1977, Exploratory Data Analysis, Addison-Wesley

inputs.name: Slabs
inputs.desc: Slab DTOs.

inputs.name: Bins
inputs.desc: Histogram bin count. 0 = ceil(sqrt(N)). Default 0.

outputs.name: Count
outputs.desc: Total piece count.

outputs.name: Total Volume
outputs.desc: Sum of all volumes.

outputs.name: CV
outputs.desc: Coefficient of variation (StdDev/Mean).

outputs.name: Outlier Indices
outputs.desc: Indices outside the Tukey fence (Q1−1.5·IQR, Q3+1.5·IQR).

outputs.name: Bin Counts
outputs.desc: Histogram counts (length = Bins).

outputs.name: Bin Width
outputs.desc: Width of each histogram bin.

outputs.name: Report
outputs.desc: One-line summary.

name: Build Sequence JSON
description: Encodes the masonry build sequence as a JSON string.  Wire Block Ids, Place Planes (or Place Transforms via  Pick Place Frames), and Layers in matching list order.  Output is plain text — pipe to a file-writing component  if you need disk persistence.

inputs.name: Block Ids
inputs.desc: Per-block identifier. Required.

inputs.name: Place Planes
inputs.desc: Per-block placement plane (parallel to Block Ids).  Required.

inputs.name: Layers
inputs.desc: Per-block course number. Optional; if absent, all  layers are reported as 0.

inputs.name: Pretty
inputs.desc: Indent the JSON for human reading. Default true. Set  false for compact single-line output.

outputs.name: Json
outputs.desc: JSON text encoding the build sequence (schema 1.0).

name: Build Step Preview
description: Slider-driven animation of a masonry build sequence. Wire  the ordered meshes from Block Build Order and a Step  integer slider. Returns Built (0..step), Pending  (step..N), and Current (mesh at step).

inputs.name: Ordered Meshes
inputs.desc: Block meshes in build order. Pipe in the Ordered Meshes  output from Block Build Order.

inputs.name: Step
inputs.desc: Current step. 0 = nothing built yet; N = everything  built. Values outside [0, N] are clamped.

outputs.name: Built
outputs.desc: Meshes placed at or before Step (indices 0..step-1).

outputs.name: Pending
outputs.desc: Meshes still to place (indices step..N-1).

outputs.name: Current
outputs.desc: The mesh placed at this step (index step-1). Empty when  Step == 0 (nothing built yet).

outputs.name: Total
outputs.desc: Total mesh count, for sizing the slider.

name: Build-Order Stability Stream
description: Walks a masonry build order and runs the RBE convex-QP  stability check on each partial assembly. Reports the  first step at which the in-progress wall becomes unstable.

inputs.name: Assembly
inputs.desc: Full MasonryAssembly DTO.

inputs.name: Ordered Block Ids
inputs.desc: Block ids in build order. Output of Block Build Order.

inputs.name: Mu
inputs.desc: Coulomb friction coefficient. Default 0.84 (~40°).

inputs.name: Faces
inputs.desc: Pyramidal friction-cone face count. Default 4.

inputs.name: Penalty
inputs.desc: Penalty form (split f_n+/f_n-). Default false.

inputs.name: Stop On First Infeasible
inputs.desc: Stop streaming as soon as one step is infeasible. Default true.

inputs.name: Max Steps
inputs.desc: Cap on steps to evaluate. -1 = no cap (default).

inputs.name: Run
inputs.desc: Set true to run.

outputs.name: First Unstable Step
outputs.desc: 0-based index of the first infeasible step. -1 if every  evaluated step was stable.

outputs.name: First Unstable Block Id
outputs.desc: Block id placed at the first unstable step. Empty when  all evaluated steps are stable.

outputs.name: Verdict Per Step
outputs.desc: Per-step verdict (parallel to Ordered Block Ids up to the  evaluated count).

outputs.name: Objective Per Step
outputs.desc: Per-step QP objective value.

outputs.name: Report
outputs.desc: Multi-line summary log.

name: Cut Validation
description: Validates a cut: sum(post-piece volumes) ≈ sum(pre-slab  volumes) within tolerance. Flags slivers and dropouts.

inputs.name: Pre Slabs
inputs.desc: Slab DTOs before the cut.

inputs.name: Post Slabs
inputs.desc: Slab DTOs after the cut.

inputs.name: Relative Tolerance
inputs.desc: Acceptable relative volume mismatch. Default 1e-6.

inputs.name: Sliver Fraction
inputs.desc: Pieces whose volume is below this fraction of the total  are flagged as slivers. Default 1e-4.

inputs.name: Dropout Volume
inputs.desc: Pieces below this absolute volume are flagged as  dropouts (likely lost geometry). Default 1e-12.

inputs.name: Drop Slivers
inputs.desc: If true, also output a sliver-free subset of post slabs.  Default false.

outputs.name: Conserved
outputs.desc: True iff |postVol − preVol| / preVol ≤ Relative Tolerance.

outputs.name: Pre Volume
outputs.desc: Sum |signed volume| of pre slabs.

outputs.name: Post Volume
outputs.desc: Sum |signed volume| of post slabs.

outputs.name: Absolute Error
outputs.desc: |Vpre − Vpost|.

outputs.name: Relative Error
outputs.desc: AbsErr / Vpre.

outputs.name: Sliver Indices
outputs.desc: 0-based indices of post slabs flagged as slivers.

outputs.name: Dropout Indices
outputs.desc: 0-based indices of post slabs with effectively zero volume.

outputs.name: Cleaned Slabs
outputs.desc: Sliver-free subset of post slabs. Empty when Drop  Slivers is false.

outputs.name: Report
outputs.desc: One-line summary.

name: Fragment Merger
description: Agglomerates small fragments into their largest adjacent  host using upstream contact adjacency. Returns a merge  mapping (HostOf per piece + per-host accumulated volume);  geometry is NOT remeshed at this stage. Frahan-original method.

algorithm.title: Greedy smallest-first agglomeration over a contact-adjacency graph
algorithm.citation: Frahan-original

inputs.name: Slabs
inputs.desc: Slab DTOs (the candidate pieces).

inputs.name: Adjacency I
inputs.desc: First index of each adjacency pair.

inputs.name: Adjacency J
inputs.desc: Second index of each adjacency pair (parallel to Ai).

inputs.name: Threshold Fraction
inputs.desc: Fragments below threshold·meanVolume are merged. Default  1e-3 (0.1% of mean).

outputs.name: Host Of
outputs.desc: Per-input-piece, the index it ultimately merged into.  Self if it's a host.

outputs.name: Merged Volume
outputs.desc: Per-input-piece volume after merge (host accumulates;  non-host entries are 0).

outputs.name: Host Indices
outputs.desc: Indices of pieces that remained hosts.

outputs.name: Merged Count
outputs.desc: Number of fragments that got merged into a different host.

name: IFC Export (Building)
description: Write SEVERAL stone containers (walls, arches, vaults, columns) into one IFC4 building.  Stones come as a tree: one branch per container; Names and Containers list-match the branches.  Each stone becomes an IfcBuildingElementPart (or voussoir IfcMember) with a tessellated body.

inputs.name: Stones
inputs.desc: Stone meshes, ONE BRANCH PER CONTAINER

inputs.name: Names
inputs.desc: Container names (one per branch)

inputs.name: Containers
inputs.desc: Per branch: 0 Wall | 1 Cladding | 2 Arch | 3 Vault | 4 Column

inputs.name: Path
inputs.desc: Output .ifc path

inputs.name: Project
inputs.desc: IfcProject name Frahan Stone Building

inputs.name: Run
inputs.desc: Write the file

outputs.name: Path
outputs.desc: Written file

outputs.name: Report
outputs.desc: Export report

outputs.name: OK
outputs.desc: True if the file was written

name: IFC Export (Stone Assembly)
description: Write the stone assembly as IFC4: container element (wall / cladding / arch / vault / column)  with one building-element part per stone (tessellated body + Frahan_Stone property set).  xBIM Essentials, SI metres.

inputs.name: Stones
inputs.desc: Closed stone meshes in their placed positions

inputs.name: Name
inputs.desc: Container element name StoneWall

inputs.name: Path
inputs.desc: Output .ifc path

inputs.name: Container
inputs.desc: 0 Wall | 1 Cladding | 2 Arch | 3 Vault | 4 Column

inputs.name: Carve
inputs.desc: Per-stone carve ratio lambda (optional)

inputs.name: Stability
inputs.desc: Stability margin for the assembly (optional)

inputs.name: InterlockJ
inputs.desc: Interlock score J of the pattern (optional)

inputs.name: Run
inputs.desc: Write the file

outputs.name: Path
outputs.desc: Written file

outputs.name: Report
outputs.desc: Export report

outputs.name: OK
outputs.desc: True if the file was written

name: Masonry Assembly
description: Composes MasonryBlocks, MasonryInterfaces, and fixed-block boundary  conditions into a MasonryAssembly DTO. Interfaces must be supplied  explicitly; auto-detection is a future task.

inputs.name: Blocks
inputs.desc: MasonryBlock DTOs from Masonry Block.

inputs.name: Interfaces
inputs.desc: MasonryInterface DTOs. May be empty; auto-detection is a future task.

inputs.name: Fixed Block Ids
inputs.desc: Identifiers of blocks that are grounded (boundary conditions).  Empty list means all blocks are free.

outputs.name: Assembly
outputs.desc: MasonryAssembly DTO. Wire into Masonry Stability (RBE).

name: Masonry Block
description: Wraps a Rhino mesh into a MasonryBlock DTO. Quads are triangulated;  the mesh must have at least 3 vertices and at least one face.

inputs.name: Mesh
inputs.desc: Rhino mesh defining the block geometry. Quads are auto-triangulated.

inputs.name: Id
inputs.desc: Optional stable identifier. If blank, a fresh GUID is assigned.

inputs.name: Density
inputs.desc: Material density (kg/m^3 or any consistent unit). Must be > 0.

outputs.name: Block
outputs.desc: MasonryBlock DTO. Wire into Masonry Assembly.

outputs.name: Id
outputs.desc: Block identifier (the value passed in, or the auto-generated  GUID if Id was blank). Wire into Auto Interfaces' Block Ids  input or Masonry Assembly's Fixed Block Ids input — keeps  block identity consistent across the canvas.

name: Masonry Stability (RBE)
description: Convex-QP rigid-block-equilibrium stability check for a MasonryAssembly.  NOTE: RBE is the permissive check; CRA (Kao 2022) rejects self-stressed  states RBE accepts (H-model). Certify via Masonry Stability Check + CRA.  Asynchronous: assembles the equilibrium + friction QP and solves on a  pool thread.

algorithm.title: Rigid-Block Equilibrium QP
algorithm.citation: Kao et al. 2022, Computer-Aided Design 146:103216 Coupled Rigid-Block Analysis

inputs.name: Assembly
inputs.desc: MasonryAssembly from Masonry Assembly.

inputs.name: Mu
inputs.desc: Coulomb friction coefficient (default 0.84, ~40 deg).

inputs.name: Faces
inputs.desc: Number of pyramidal faces used to linearise the friction cone  (>= 3, default 4).

inputs.name: Penalty
inputs.desc: If true, use the penalty form (split normals f_n+/f_n-) for the  equilibrium matrix.

inputs.name: Run
inputs.desc: Set true to execute the solve.

outputs.name: Verdict
outputs.desc: Short verdict: 'stable', 'infeasible', 'no solver registered', or an  error class.

outputs.name: Objective
outputs.desc: QP objective value at the returned x (NaN when not Optimal).

outputs.name: ResidualNorm
outputs.desc: L2 norm of the equilibrium residual ||Aeq x - beq||.

outputs.name: SolverName
outputs.desc: Identifier of the solver used (e.g. 'ManagedQpSolver').

outputs.name: Report
outputs.desc: Human-readable diagnostic.

name: Masonry Stability Check
description: Rigid-block equilibrium (RBE) stability check for a stone assembly: contacts are  auto-detected, the Kao 2021/2022 compression-only + Coulomb-friction QP is solved,  and the verdict + per-interface friction utilization are reported. Friction uses a  conservative INSCRIBED K-face pyramid (mu_eff = mu*cos(pi/K)).  Refs: Kao et al. 2021 (J Mech Des) / 2022 (CAD 146:103216, compas_cra).

inputs.name: Stones
inputs.desc: Closed stone meshes in their placed positions (optional when an Assembly is supplied)

inputs.name: Mu
inputs.desc: Coulomb friction coefficient (0.84 ~ dry stone, 40 deg)

inputs.name: Faces
inputs.desc: Friction pyramid face count (>= 3; 8 recommended)

inputs.name: FixBelowZ
inputs.desc: Blocks whose lowest vertex is within this of the global min Z are fixed (ground)

inputs.name: Density
inputs.desc: Stone density (kg/m^3)

inputs.name: ContactTol
inputs.desc: Contact detection distance tolerance (model units)

inputs.name: AngleTol
inputs.desc: Contact detection face-angle tolerance (degrees). Raise to ~12-20 for stones on CURVED surfaces, where adjacent stones extrude along different normals and joint faces tilt apart.

inputs.name: Assembly
inputs.desc: OPTIONAL: a pre-built assembly (e.g. the Polygonal Wall Generator's Assembly output, with  exact generator-adjacency joints). When supplied, Stones/tolerances are ignored and the  check runs directly on it - much faster and tolerance-free.

inputs.name: CRA
inputs.desc: Use the COUPLED rigid-block analysis (Kao 2022 Eqs 8-14, alternating convex certificate)  instead of force-only RBE. CRA also checks that a kinematically consistent virtual motion  exists, rejecting self-stressed states RBE wrongly accepts (the H-model).

outputs.name: Stable
outputs.desc: True when an admissible compressive/friction-consistent force state exists (RBE-stable)

outputs.name: Report
outputs.desc: Verdict, counts, max compression, worst friction utilization, weakest interface

outputs.name: Utilization
outputs.desc: Per-interface max friction utilization (1.0 = cone saturated)

name: Match Block Transform
description: Library-based auto-match: given a list of canonical  library meshes and a list of placed (target) meshes,  find which library entry each target was transformed  from and recover the placement transform via Horn QAO.  Strictly more accurate than 3-random-vertex matching  because the fit is least-squares over all N vertex pairs.  Implements Horn QAO (Horn 1987).

algorithm.title: Closed-form absolute orientation (Horn QAO)
algorithm.citation: Horn 1987, Closed-form solution of absolute orientation using unit quaternions, JOSA A 4(4):629-642

inputs.name: Library Meshes
inputs.desc: Canonical mesh shapes (the 'pick' representations).

inputs.name: Target Meshes
inputs.desc: Placed meshes to match against the library.

inputs.name: RMS Threshold
inputs.desc: Maximum acceptable per-fit RMS for a high-confidence  match. Targets whose best library fit exceeds this  threshold are still emitted but tagged 'low confidence'.  Default 1e-3.

outputs.name: Transforms
outputs.desc: Per-target transform: library[matched] → target.

outputs.name: Matched Library Index
outputs.desc: Per-target library index. -1 when no candidate had a  matching vertex count.

outputs.name: RMS
outputs.desc: Per-target Horn QAO residual at the chosen library entry.

outputs.name: Status
outputs.desc: 'matched (rms=…)' / 'low confidence (rms=…)' / 'no match'.

name: Mesh Planar Polygon Extractor
description: Extracts the outer + hole loops from a mesh's boundary  and projects them into a 2D plane. Frahan-original method.

algorithm.title: Boundary-loop extraction + signed-area outer/hole classification
algorithm.citation: Frahan-original

inputs.name: Mesh
inputs.desc: Mesh whose boundary loops are extracted.

inputs.name: Plane
inputs.desc: Projection plane (origin + axes). Default world XY.

outputs.name: Outer
outputs.desc: Closed CCW polyline curve for the outermost loop.

outputs.name: Holes
outputs.desc: Closed CW polyline curves for each hole (parallel to  Hole Areas).

outputs.name: Outer Area
outputs.desc: Signed area of the outer loop in plane units².

outputs.name: Hole Areas
outputs.desc: Signed area of each hole.

name: Mesh Quality Report
description: Topology + geometry diagnostics for a Rhino mesh. Use as  a precondition for contact detection, packing, or cutting.  Metrics per Frey & Borouchaki 1999.

algorithm.title: Mesh-quality metrics
algorithm.citation: Frey & Borouchaki 1999, Surface mesh quality evaluation, Int. J. Numer. Methods Eng. 45(1):101-118

inputs.name: Mesh
inputs.desc: Mesh to analyse.

inputs.name: Dedup Tolerance
inputs.desc: Vertex-merge tolerance for duplicate detection. Default 1e-9.

inputs.name: Degenerate Area Tol
inputs.desc: Triangle-area threshold below which a triangle counts as  degenerate. Default 1e-12.

outputs.name: Is Clean Solid
outputs.desc: True iff closed AND manifold AND consistent normals AND no  degenerate triangles AND no duplicate vertices.

outputs.name: Manifold
outputs.desc: Every edge is incident to 1 or 2 triangles.

outputs.name: Closed
outputs.desc: Every edge is incident to exactly 2 triangles.

outputs.name: Consistent Normals
outputs.desc: No two triangles share an edge in the same winding direction.

outputs.name: Duplicate Vertices
outputs.desc: Vertices closer than the dedup tolerance to an earlier one.

outputs.name: Degenerate Triangles
outputs.desc: Triangles below the area threshold.

outputs.name: Boundary Edges
outputs.desc: Edges incident to exactly one triangle (open boundaries).

outputs.name: Non-manifold Edges
outputs.desc: Edges incident to three or more triangles.

outputs.name: Median Edge Length
outputs.desc: Median triangle-edge length. Useful as an adaptive-tolerance  scale factor.

outputs.name: Surface Area
outputs.desc: Sum of triangle areas.

outputs.name: Signed Volume
outputs.desc: Divergence-theorem volume. Negative means normals are  inward-facing on a closed mesh.

outputs.name: Report
outputs.desc: Single-line human-readable summary.

name: Pack Diagnostics
description: Splits an AshlarPackResult into Coverage / Course Count /  Leftovers / Notes / Placed Blocks for inspection.

inputs.name: Result
inputs.desc: AshlarPackResult from Ashlar Pack.

outputs.name: Coverage
outputs.desc: Wall area filled by placed blocks divided by total wall area, in [0, 1].

outputs.name: Course Count
outputs.desc: Number of courses laid.

outputs.name: Leftovers
outputs.desc: Slabs from the input inventory that were not placed.

outputs.name: Notes
outputs.desc: Diagnostic messages emitted by the layout engine.

outputs.name: Placed Blocks
outputs.desc: MasonryBlocks in the order they were laid.

name: Pack Preview
description: Builds one Rhino mesh per placed block in an AshlarPackResult  for visual preview on the canvas.

inputs.name: Result
inputs.desc: AshlarPackResult from Ashlar Pack.

outputs.name: Meshes
outputs.desc: One Rhino mesh per placed MasonryBlock.

name: Pick Place Frames
description: Per-block pick-and-place planes for a robot consumer.  Wire Place Transforms from Block Ground Transforms; the  component returns pick + approach-pick (shared across  all blocks) and place / approach-place / retract-place  (one per block).

inputs.name: Place Transforms
inputs.desc: Per-block placement transform (typically the output of  Block Ground Transforms, ordered by Build Order). Each  transform takes the canonical pose at Pick Plane to  the placed pose.

inputs.name: Pick Plane
inputs.desc: Where each canonical block is picked up. Default world XY.

inputs.name: Approach Vector
inputs.desc: World-frame direction the robot approaches FROM (i.e.,  moves opposite to when descending). Default world +Z so  the gripper hovers above pick / place poses.

inputs.name: Approach Distance
inputs.desc: Distance the gripper hovers above pick / place poses  before descending. Default 0.05 (5 cm in metres, or  5 mm in millimetres — match your unit system).

inputs.name: Retract Distance
inputs.desc: Distance the gripper retracts after release. Default 0.05.

outputs.name: Pick
outputs.desc: Shared pick pose (same as input Pick Plane).

outputs.name: Approach Pick
outputs.desc: Pick + approach offset. Hover here before descending to pick.

outputs.name: Place
outputs.desc: Per-block place pose = Pick · transform[i].

outputs.name: Approach Place
outputs.desc: Per-block approach-place = place + approach offset.

outputs.name: Retract Place
outputs.desc: Per-block retract-place = place + retract offset.

name: Polygon Sanitize
description: Drops duplicate / collinear vertices and sliver edges  from a closed polyline. Operates in 2D — points are  projected onto the supplied plane (default world XY).  Frahan-original method.

algorithm.title: Polygon vertex cleanup (dedup + collinearity drop)
algorithm.citation: Frahan-original

inputs.name: Polyline
inputs.desc: Closed polyline curve.

inputs.name: Plane
inputs.desc: 2D projection plane. Default world XY.

inputs.name: Dedup Tolerance
inputs.desc: Adjacent-vertex dedup tolerance. Default 1e-6.

inputs.name: Collinear Tolerance
inputs.desc: Triangle area threshold for collinear-chain dropping.  Default 1e-6.

outputs.name: Sanitized
outputs.desc: Cleaned closed polyline.

outputs.name: Verts Dropped
outputs.desc: Number of vertices removed.

outputs.name: Area
outputs.desc: Signed area of the sanitized polygon (in plane units²).

name: Polygonal Masonry Sequence
description: Installation-order DAG for a polygonal-masonry wall  (Kim 2024). Inputs are 2D chains and a wall rectangle.  Each chain must be monotone in x or a vertical connector.  Output is one closed polyline per stone, parallel install  order, depth from Code 1, and DAG edge line segments.

algorithm.title: Polygonal masonry install sequence
algorithm.citation: Kim 2024 ASME DETC2024-142563 Finding Installation Sequence of Polygonal Masonry through Design and Depth Search of a DAG

inputs.name: Chains
inputs.desc: Polylines or curves defining the wall partition. Each must  be monotone in x or a purely vertical connector. Chains  may share endpoints at meetings; they must not cross.

inputs.name: Wall
inputs.desc: Axis-aligned wall rectangle. Defines the bbox and the  wall boundary (paper sec. 5.3).

inputs.name: Hole Probes
inputs.desc: Optional. Each probe point marks the region containing it  as a hole; that region is removed before depth search  (paper sec. 5.4).

inputs.name: Epsilon
inputs.desc: Tolerance for vertex deduplication and predicates.

outputs.name: Stones
outputs.desc: One closed polyline per stone region (finite, non-hole).  Includes the two infinite top/bottom bands.

outputs.name: Install Order
outputs.desc: 1-based install index per stone, parallel to Stones.  1 = installed first (bottom), max = installed last.

outputs.name: Depth
outputs.desc: Reversed-Kahn depth per stone. Higher = installed earlier.  Sinks (last-installed) have depth 0.

outputs.name: DAG Edges
outputs.desc: One line segment per DAG edge from lower-order centroid to  higher-order centroid. Visualises the install constraint  graph (paper Figs. 5, 13, 14).

outputs.name: Region Count
outputs.desc: Number of finite stone regions (excludes the bbox  surroundings and any hole-marked regions).

name: Polygonal Masonry Sequence 3D
description: Install-order DAG for a 3D polyhedral-stone wall. Each  input Mesh is one stone; adjacency is detected from  shared mesh faces. Returns 1-based install order,  reversed-Kahn depth, and DAG edges as line segments  between cell centroids (Kim 2024 sec. 8 extension).

algorithm.title: 3D polygonal masonry install sequence
algorithm.citation: Kim 2024 DETC2024-142563 section 8 3D extension

inputs.name: Cells
inputs.desc: Closed polyhedral meshes; one per stone.

inputs.name: Hole Probes
inputs.desc: Optional. Each probe point marks the cell whose centroid  is closest as a hole; that cell is removed before the  depth search (sec. 5.4 analogue).

inputs.name: Face Tolerance
inputs.desc: Two cells count as adjacent when at least one of their  mesh faces matches within this Euclidean tolerance.

inputs.name: Z Threshold
inputs.desc: Adjacent cells whose representative-Z difference is  below this value are treated as side neighbours  (no ordering constraint).

outputs.name: Stones
outputs.desc: Cells in install order, parallel to Order / Depth.

outputs.name: Install Order
outputs.desc: 1-based install index per cell, parallel to Stones.

outputs.name: Depth
outputs.desc: Reversed-Kahn depth per cell.

outputs.name: DAG Edges
outputs.desc: Line segments from lower-Z cell centroid to higher-Z  cell centroid for every DAG edge.

outputs.name: Cell Count
outputs.desc: Number of stones included in the install plan  (excludes hole-marked cells).

name: Polygonal Wall (Generator)
description: Tile a surface (flat / curved / double-curved) with interlocking polygonal masonry stones  (power-diagram cells + Lloyd relaxation + Coursing slider + sliver cull). Outputs closed,  manifold, unified-normal stone meshes and an interlock score J.  Pattern math: Frahan.Masonry.Sequencing.PolygonalWallGenerator (Rhino-free Core).  Refs: Kim 2024 (ASME DETC2024-142563) sequencing substrate; Clifford & McGee 2018  (ACADIA, Cyclopean Cannibalism) interlock reading; Lloyd 1982 relaxation.

inputs.name: Surface
inputs.desc: Surface to tile (flat / curved / double-curved). If empty, a flat W x H panel in the XZ plane is used.

inputs.name: Width
inputs.desc: Panel width (m) when no Surface is supplied

inputs.name: Height
inputs.desc: Panel height (m) when no Surface is supplied

inputs.name: Coursing
inputs.desc: 0 = irregular (Inca)  ->  1 = coursed rubble

inputs.name: Courses
inputs.desc: Number of courses the coursing pulls toward

inputs.name: GridX
inputs.desc: Seed columns (stones along width)

inputs.name: GridY
inputs.desc: Seed rows (stones along height)

inputs.name: Depth
inputs.desc: Stone depth (m), extruded along the surface normal

inputs.name: Mortar
inputs.desc: Mortar joint fraction (cell shrink toward centroid, 0..0.45)

inputs.name: Seed
inputs.desc: Random seed

inputs.name: Lloyd
inputs.desc: Lloyd relaxation iterations (evens stone size/shape; 0 disables)

inputs.name: SizeGrade
inputs.desc: Size-grading strength 0..~0.6 (power-diagram weights)

outputs.name: Stones
outputs.desc: Closed, manifold, unified-outward-normal stone meshes

outputs.name: Count
outputs.desc: Number of stones

outputs.name: Interlock
outputs.desc: Interlock score in [0,1]: 1 - runningJoints/headJoints - 0.5*crossVertices/cells. Higher = better staggering.

outputs.name: Report
outputs.desc: Pattern metrics (coverage, area CV, slivers culled, joints)

outputs.name: Assembly
outputs.desc: The wall as a structural assembly with EXACT joint interfaces from the generator's own cell  adjacency (no contact re-detection; correct on any curvature). Feed straight into Masonry  Stability Check's Assembly input. Models the dry (mortarless), uniform-depth wall.

name: Robust Auto Interfaces
description: Detects block-to-block contacts via mesh-vertex proximity.  Robust to slight gaps, non-planar contact regions, and  irregular triangulation (scan-derived meshes). Use this  when 'Auto Interfaces' (polygon-based) misses contacts.  Frahan-original method.

algorithm.title: Auto interface detection
algorithm.citation: Frahan-original

inputs.name: Meshes
inputs.desc: Block meshes in their final placed positions. Standard Rhino  mesh wires; this component finds proximity-based contacts.

inputs.name: Block Ids
inputs.desc: One ID per mesh in the same order. Must match the IDs used  to construct MasonryBlocks.

inputs.name: Distance Tolerance
inputs.desc: Max distance between two surfaces to count as contact  (document units). Default 0.001 (1 mm). Raise for noisy  scan data; lower for exact-coord meshes.

inputs.name: Angle Tolerance Deg
inputs.desc: Contact points are grouped when their surface normals agree  within this angle. Default 5° — accommodates mild surface  curvature; tighten to 1° for sharply-faceted blocks.

inputs.name: Min Contact Points
inputs.desc: Minimum contact points required to emit a MasonryInterface.  Default 3 (= polygon triangle minimum). Raise to filter  out spurious single-vertex grazes.

outputs.name: Interfaces
outputs.desc: Detected MasonryInterfaces. Wire into Masonry Assembly.

outputs.name: Count
outputs.desc: Number of detected interfaces.

name: Rubble Wall Settle
description: NOTE: the settle v2 objective reaches +97% clearance, 23/24 stable  (see examples/27 cards); this component remains the validated v1.  Settles stone meshes into an upright Z-up rubble wall. Each  stone is PCA-oriented so its broad/flat face beds DOWN, then  dropped (gravity = -Z) into the per-(x,y)-cell dimples of the  course below, trying 4 orientation flips and a small X-slot  shift. Non-penetrating by construction. Reports a per-stone  COM-over-support stability flag and signed support clearance.  Apply each output mesh as-is; transforms are already baked in.

algorithm.title: COM-over-support stability
algorithm.citation: Heyman 1966 limit-state masonry (centre of thrust within the support)

inputs.name: Stones
inputs.desc: Stone inventory as Rhino meshes (e.g. ETH1100 dry-stone scans,  Quarry blocks, or hand-authored). Each is PCA-oriented for flat  bedding; the input meshes are not modified. Order is preserved  in the outputs.

inputs.name: Width
inputs.desc: Wall length along +X, in units of the mean stone X-extent.  > 0. Default 7.0 (the signed-off proportion). Larger spreads  stones into more, shorter courses; smaller piles them taller.

inputs.name: Stability Aware
inputs.desc: When true, each stone prefers the first seat whose COM projects  inside its contact support polygon (won't topple), then the  deepest. When false, always takes the deepest (densest) seat.  Default true.

inputs.name: Margin
inputs.desc: Required COM-over-support clearance (document units) for a seat  to count as stable. >= 0. Default 0.0 (COM merely inside the  support polygon).

outputs.name: Settled
outputs.desc: Placed stones, upright in the Z-up wall, one per input mesh in  input order. The PCA flat-bed orientation, flip, and settle  offsets are already applied.

outputs.name: Stable
outputs.desc: Per-stone COM-over-support flag: true if the projected COM lies  inside the contact support polygon by at least Margin.

outputs.name: Clearance
outputs.desc: Per-stone signed support clearance. > 0 = COM inside the support  polygon (distance to the nearest edge); <= 0 = would topple;  -1 = degenerate support (< 3 non-collinear contacts).

name: Stone-Cell Match (Λ)
description: Hungarian assignment of a stone inventory to target wall cells, minimising carved material.  Reports per-stone carve ratio λ, the workflow imposition index Λ (0 = as-found … 1 = pure  stock; Cyclopean Cannibalism datum ≈0.27) and gap ratios, and outputs the stones placed  into their cells. Refs: Clifford & McGee 2018 (ACADIA); Kuhn 1955 (Hungarian);  Frahan SLM+ROSES masonry review 2026-06-10.

inputs.name: Stones
inputs.desc: Stone inventory (closed meshes, found/scanned)

inputs.name: Cells
inputs.desc: Target cells (closed meshes, e.g. the Polygonal Wall Generator's stones at Mortar = 0)

inputs.name: TopK
inputs.desc: Prefilter candidates per cell that get the voxel cost

inputs.name: CostRes
inputs.desc: Voxel resolution for the assignment cost

inputs.name: RefineRes
inputs.desc: Voxel resolution for the final per-pair metrics

outputs.name: Placed
outputs.desc: Stones transformed into their assigned cells (cell order)

outputs.name: Carve
outputs.desc: Per-placement carve ratio lambda_i = carved/found volume (cell order)

outputs.name: Gap
outputs.desc: Per-placement gap ratio (cell volume the stone fails to fill)

outputs.name: Lambda
outputs.desc: Workflow imposition index: volume-weighted carve fraction (0..1)

outputs.name: StoneIndex
outputs.desc: Assigned stone index per placement (cell order)

outputs.name: Unused
outputs.desc: Inventory indices that were not used

outputs.name: Report
outputs.desc: Summary

name: Wall Frame
description: Bundles wall width / height / thickness into a WallFrame DTO.  Wire into the optional WallFrame input on Ashlar Pack so the  envelope can be reused across multiple packers.

inputs.name: Width
inputs.desc: Wall length along +X (Rhino-document units, typically meters).  Must be > 0. Example: 1.5 for a 1.5 m long wall.

inputs.name: Height
inputs.desc: Wall height along +Z. Must be > 0. Example: 1.0 for 1 m tall.

inputs.name: Thickness
inputs.desc: Wall thickness along +Y. Must be > 0. Default 0.20 — typical  single-leaf masonry. Use 0.40 for double-leaf.

outputs.name: Wall Frame
outputs.desc: WallFrame DTO. Wire into Ashlar Pack's Wall Frame input to  reuse the same envelope across multiple packers.

name: Bench From Mesh
description: Derive an axis-aligned Box bench + carry the original Mesh  for use with the existing 11 BCO components (which take Box  inputs) and with ClipBoxesByMesh (which filters their Box[]  outputs). Closes the §7.8 mesh-bench gap without editing any  existing BCO component. Designed for non-rectangular quarry  benches: trapezoidal, stepped, polygonal, surveyed from a  DXF + bench-height extrusion, or produced by a Phase H  scan reconstruction.

inputs.name: Mesh
inputs.desc: Bench mesh (any closed or open shape). The AABB is derived  from this for the Box output; the mesh itself is preserved  for downstream clipping.

inputs.name: Bench Height
inputs.desc: Optional override: if > 0, the Box output's Z height is  extended to this value (e.g. when the user wants the bench  AABB to span the full block height even though the mesh  only covers the working face). Default 0 = use mesh AABB Z  as-is.

outputs.name: Box
outputs.desc: Axis-aligned bounding Box of the mesh (with optional  Bench Height override applied to Z). Wire to the existing  BCO components' `Tested Area` or `Bench` input.

outputs.name: Mesh
outputs.desc: Pass-through of the input mesh, for downstream clipping.

outputs.name: Bench Boundary
outputs.desc: Opaque BenchBoundary value (Box + Mesh combined). Future  BCO-v2 components consume this directly.

outputs.name: Vertex Count
outputs.desc: Mesh vertex count (sanity check).

name: Clip Boxes By Mesh
description: Filter a Box[] grid from BCO output by mesh-boundary  containment. Drops cells that lie outside the actual bench  (the cells the AABB algorithm wrongly claimed as winnable).  Use after BCOExtract / HeteroExt / BCOMixedPack to get the  true recovery on a non-rectangular bench.  Frahan-original method.

algorithm.title: Mesh-containment box filter
algorithm.citation: Frahan-original

inputs.name: Boxes
inputs.desc: Box[] from a BCO output (Prime Boxes, Mixed Boxes, Zone  Boxes, etc.).

inputs.name: Mesh Bench
inputs.desc: Closed mesh of the actual bench geometry. Wire from  BenchFromMesh.Mesh.

inputs.name: Inside Fraction Threshold
inputs.desc: A box is kept when at least this fraction of its 8 corners  lie inside the mesh. 0 = keep all (back-compat); 0.5 =  majority must be inside; 1.0 = entire box must be inside.

inputs.name: Tolerance
inputs.desc: Inside/outside testing tolerance in model units.

outputs.name: Inside Boxes
outputs.desc: Cells whose containment fraction meets or exceeds the  threshold.

outputs.name: Outside Boxes
outputs.desc: Cells that fall below the threshold (the AABB algorithm  wrongly claimed these).

outputs.name: Inside Count
outputs.desc: Number of cells inside the mesh.

outputs.name: Outside Count
outputs.desc: Number of cells dropped.

outputs.name: Corrected Recovery
outputs.desc: Inside / total fraction in [0, 1]. Apply this multiplicatively  to recovery numbers reported by the BCO components when they  ran on the same AABB grid.

outputs.name: Report
outputs.desc: Human-readable summary.

name: Close Holes
description: Fill open boundary loops to make a watertight mesh. Backend: Managed  (RhinoCommon Mesh.FillHoles, fast on clean meshes), Geogram  (GEO::fill_holes, robust on dirty / scan meshes), or Auto (managed  first, geogram fallback if still open). Max Hole Area / Edges apply  to the Geogram path. Runs on a background thread (Run gate) so the  canvas never freezes.

algorithm.title: Geogram hole filling
algorithm.citation: GEO::fill_holes — triangulate open boundary loops up to an area / edge-count threshold

inputs.name: Mesh
inputs.desc: Mesh with holes (open boundary loops).

inputs.name: Max Hole Area
inputs.desc: Geogram path: largest hole AREA to fill (model units squared). 0 fills  nothing; a very large value (default 1e30) fills every hole.

inputs.name: Max Hole Edges
inputs.desc: Geogram path: max boundary edges per hole. 0 = no limit (area governs).

inputs.name: Repair After
inputs.desc: Geogram path: run a repair pass after filling. Default true.

inputs.name: Run
inputs.desc: Set true to close holes (background thread).

inputs.name: Backend
inputs.desc: 0 = Auto (managed first, geogram fallback); 1 = Managed (RhinoCommon,  fast); 2 = Geogram (robust on dirty meshes).

outputs.name: Mesh
outputs.desc: Hole-filled mesh.

outputs.name: Closed
outputs.desc: True if the output mesh is closed (watertight).

outputs.name: Report
outputs.desc: Before/after validity summary + backend used.

name: Cloud ICP
description: Register a source point cloud onto a target via coarse-to- fine trimmed ICP. Uses Geogram KD-tree + voxel downsample  (native shim, Phase I) when available; falls back to  managed brute-force / hash-grid otherwise. Scales to 10M+  points with the native shim. [Besl & McKay 1992]

algorithm.title: Trimmed ICP (coarse-to-fine)
algorithm.citation: Besl & McKay 1992 (Iterative Closest Point); Chetverikov et al. 2002 (Trimmed ICP)

inputs.name: Source Cloud
inputs.desc: Source point cloud to register.

inputs.name: Target Cloud
inputs.desc: Target point cloud (registration goal).

inputs.name: Initial Guess
inputs.desc: Optional initial transform. Identity if not wired.

inputs.name: Voxel Scales
inputs.desc: Coarse-to-fine voxel sizes (model units). Default {0.5, 0.1,  0.02} → 50 cm → 10 cm → 2 cm for metre-scale benches.

inputs.name: Max Iterations
inputs.desc: Max ICP iterations per voxel scale.

inputs.name: Trim Fraction
inputs.desc: Drop this fraction of worst-residual pairs each iteration.  0.2 = standard robust ICP. 0 keeps all.

outputs.name: Transform
outputs.desc: Cumulative source→target rigid transform.

outputs.name: Final RMS
outputs.desc: Final RMS distance between corresponding source-target pairs.

outputs.name: Iterations
outputs.desc: Total iterations across all voxel scales.

outputs.name: Converged
outputs.desc: True when the last iteration met the tolerance.

outputs.name: Correspondences
outputs.desc: Number of correspondences used in the final iteration  (after trim).

outputs.name: Report
outputs.desc: Summary line.

name: Estimate Cloud Normals
description: PCA + MST-oriented normals on an unstructured point cloud.  Wire upstream of Poisson reconstruction (ScanReconstruct  Mode = 2) or point-to-plane Cloud ICP. Runs on a background  thread (Run gate). Requires the Phase H/I rebuild of  frahan_cgal.dll; falls back to a Warning bubble if the shim  isn't built. [Hoppe et al. 1992]

algorithm.title: PCA normal estimation + MST orientation
algorithm.citation: Hoppe et al. 1992, surface reconstruction from unorganized points (PCA tangent planes + MST sign propagation)

inputs.name: Points
inputs.desc: Input cloud as a point list. Optional if Cloud is wired.

inputs.name: K Neighbours
inputs.desc: k for PCA fit (CGAL recommends 18-24 for dense clouds).  0 uses 18.

inputs.name: Cloud
inputs.desc: Input as a single native PointCloud (lag-free; preferred over the  Points list for large scans). If wired, the Points list is ignored.

inputs.name: Run
inputs.desc: Set true to estimate normals (on a background thread).  False = idle; the canvas never freezes.

outputs.name: Normals
outputs.desc: Per-point oriented normals; same order as input.

outputs.name: Report
outputs.desc: Summary.

outputs.name: Cloud
outputs.desc: The input points as a single native PointCloud WITH the estimated  normals baked in. Wire into Scan Reconstruct (Cloud) for a lag-free  Poisson path - no million-point list crosses the canvas.

name: Georeference
description: Rigid scan→world transform from N≥3 control-point pairs in a  global coordinate system. Supports WGS84 LLH degrees, UTM,  and pre-converted ENU metres. World points are converted to  ENU about the first control point's origin before solving.  Implements absolute orientation (Horn 1987).  Sibling: GeorefCRS handles the WGS84/UTM/ENU datum;  'GeorefPts' (Georeference (Align by Points)) is the local fit  via Horn when both datasets share a frame.

algorithm.title: Absolute orientation + UTM/EPSG transform
algorithm.citation: Horn, B.K.P. (1987). Closed-form solution of absolute orientation using unit quaternions. J. Opt. Soc. Am. A 4(4):629-642

inputs.name: World Control Points
inputs.desc: World-frame control points in the chosen Coord System.  LLH-WGS84-degrees: pack as (X=lon°, Y=lat°, Z=height-m).  UTM: pack as (X=easting-m, Y=northing-m, Z=elevation-m).  Local-ENU: pack as (X=east-m, Y=north-m, Z=up-m).

inputs.name: Scan-Frame Points
inputs.desc: Scan-frame points paired by INDEX with World Control Points.

inputs.name: Coord System
inputs.desc: 0 = LLH-WGS84-degrees, 1 = UTM, 2 = Local-ENU.

inputs.name: UTM Zone
inputs.desc: Optional UTM zone override (1..60). Ignored unless Coord  System = 1. Default 0 means auto-pick from origin.

inputs.name: UTM Northern Hemisphere
inputs.desc: True = northern hemisphere, false = southern. Ignored  unless Coord System = 1.

outputs.name: Transform
outputs.desc: Rigid transform mapping scan-frame onto the ENU frame  centred at the first control point. Apply to your scan to  place it in world-relative coordinates.

outputs.name: RMS Error
outputs.desc: Root-mean-square per-pair residual after the transform  (ENU metres).

outputs.name: ENU Origin (LLH)
outputs.desc: The LLH origin used for ENU conversion (X=lon°, Y=lat°, Z=h-m).  Wire this into a Panel to record the projection origin alongside  the .gh file.

outputs.name: Report
outputs.desc: Human-readable summary of the solve.

outputs.name: Per-Pair Residuals
outputs.desc: Per-pair residual distances after applying Transform (m).

name: Georeference (Align by Points)
description: Best-fit transform from 3+ corresponding control points (Horn's  absolute orientation). Aligns GPR / scan / quarry geometry into one  georeferenced frame: Source = control points in the frame you move,  Target = matching points in the reference frame. Rigid by default;  enable Scale for similarity. Feed the Transform into Cloud ICP's  Initial Guess for fine registration.  Sibling: GeorefPts is the local fit via Horn from matched points;  'GeorefCRS' (Georeference) handles WGS84/UTM/ENU datum conversion.

algorithm.title: Absolute orientation (Horn 1987)
algorithm.citation: Horn, 'Closed-form solution of absolute orientation using unit quaternions', JOSA A 4(4) 1987

inputs.name: Geometry
inputs.desc: Geometry to align (moved by the fitted transform).

inputs.name: Source
inputs.desc: Control points in the SOURCE frame (>= 3).

inputs.name: Target
inputs.desc: Matching control points in the TARGET / reference frame (>= 3, same order).

inputs.name: Scale
inputs.desc: Allow uniform scale (similarity transform). Default false = rigid.

outputs.name: Geometry
outputs.desc: Geometry mapped into the target frame.

outputs.name: Transform
outputs.desc: Fitted source -> target transform.

outputs.name: Inverse
outputs.desc: Inverse transform (target -> source).

outputs.name: RMS
outputs.desc: Root-mean-square control-point residual after the fit (model units).

outputs.name: Report
outputs.desc: Fit summary.

name: Load Cloud
description: Stream a point cloud from a file and voxel-downsample on the  fly. Supports PLY (binary_little_endian + ascii; points-only  and mesh-vertex clouds) and plain ASCII XYZ / PTS. Memory is  bounded by occupied voxels, not the input point count, so very  large clouds (28M+ points) load without materialising the full  set. Pure-managed; no native dependency. Runs on a background  thread (Run gate) so the canvas stays responsive. Use upstream of  Cloud ICP / Scale Calibrate.

algorithm.title: Streaming cloud read + voxel-grid downsample
algorithm.citation: Voxel-grid filter (one centroid per occupied cell)

inputs.name: File Path
inputs.desc: Path to a .ply / .xyz / .pts / .asc / .txt point-cloud file.

inputs.name: Voxel Size
inputs.desc: Edge length of the cubic downsample voxel in model units.  Default 0.05. If <= 0, no downsample (warns for huge files).

inputs.name: Run
inputs.desc: Set true to read the file (on a background thread). False = idle;  nothing is read, the canvas never freezes.

outputs.name: Points
outputs.desc: One centroid per occupied voxel (or all points when Voxel Size <= 0).

outputs.name: Input Count
outputs.desc: Total points read from the file.

outputs.name: Output Count
outputs.desc: Number of output points (occupied voxels).

outputs.name: Bounding Box
outputs.desc: Axis-aligned bounding box of the input cloud.

outputs.name: Cloud
outputs.desc: Downsampled cloud as a single native PointCloud - fast viewport  display and bake-ready (far cheaper than the Points list for big  clouds). Wire into a Point param to explode it back into points.

name: Load E57 Cloud
description: Read a registered terrestrial-LiDAR .e57 via an out-of-process  Python worker (pye57), voxel-downsample, and ingest the result in  chunks as a single PointCloud. The heavy parse runs in a subprocess  so a crash never takes down Rhino. Coordinates are shifted to the  origin (add the Shift output to georeference back). Runs on a  background thread (Run gate); needs python + pye57 + numpy on PATH  and frahan_e57_worker.py deployed beside the .gha.

algorithm.title: Out-of-process E57 read (Python worker) + voxel downsample, chunked into one PointCloud
algorithm.citation: pye57 worker -> binary PLY -> chunked PointCloud assembly

inputs.name: E57 File
inputs.desc: Path to a .e57 registered point-cloud file.

inputs.name: Voxel Size
inputs.desc: Edge length of the cubic downsample voxel in model units (metres).  Default 0.05. If <= 0, no downsample (warns; can be very large).

inputs.name: Python Exe
inputs.desc: Optional python interpreter (full path or bare name). Empty =  'python' on PATH.

inputs.name: Run
inputs.desc: Set true to run the worker + ingest (on a background thread).  False = idle; nothing runs, the canvas never freezes.

outputs.name: Cloud
outputs.desc: The downsampled scan as a single PointCloud (shifted to the origin).  Wire into a Point param to explode into points if needed.

outputs.name: Input Count
outputs.desc: Total points in the E57 (all scans).

outputs.name: Output Count
outputs.desc: Number of points after voxel downsample.

outputs.name: Bounding Box
outputs.desc: Axis-aligned box of the output cloud (shifted frame; bounds the Cloud).

outputs.name: Shift
outputs.desc: Global offset subtracted from the original coordinates. Add it back  to the Cloud (e.g. via Move) to restore the georeferenced position.

outputs.name: PLY Path
outputs.desc: Path to the voxel-downsampled binary PLY the worker wrote (reusable).

name: Load Metashape Dense Cloud
description: Recognise a Metashape .oc3 dense-cloud file and emit conversion  guidance. v1 does NOT parse the binary format; the user must  export the .oc3 to PLY in Metashape first, then load with the  Load Cloud component. v2 will add a Metashape Python worker  following the E57 out-of-process pattern.

algorithm.title: File-extension recognition + conversion-guidance emission
algorithm.citation: Frahan-original; stub pattern mirrors E57 / GSF bridge components

inputs.name: Oc3 File
inputs.desc: Path to an Agisoft Metashape .oc3 dense-cloud file. v1 recognises  the format and emits conversion guidance; no point data is  extracted.

outputs.name: Recognised
outputs.desc: True when the file exists and has a .oc3 extension.

outputs.name: File Size MB
outputs.desc: Size of the .oc3 in megabytes (informational).

outputs.name: Guidance
outputs.desc: Conversion guidance: open the .psx in Metashape, File > Export >  Export Dense Cloud to PLY, then load with the Load Cloud component.

name: Load Photo Set
description: Inventory a folder of photogrammetry photos into a typed PhotoSet.  v1 SCOPE: filesystem listing + bucket classification (used /  skipped / raw) + aggregate summary. No EXIF parsing (use  ExifTool externally if needed). The typed PhotoSet is what  downstream Frahan components consume.

algorithm.title: Folder enumeration + bucket classification + filesystem metadata aggregate
algorithm.citation: Frahan-original; trimmed-scope v1 deliberately skips EXIF (ExifTool / CloudCompare covers that)

inputs.name: Photo Folder
inputs.desc: Root folder containing photogrammetry photos. Subfolders named  'used', 'skipped', 'raw' (case-insensitive) bucket-classify the  entries.

inputs.name: Recurse Subfolders
inputs.desc: If true, recurse into subfolders. Default true (the MRAC convention).

outputs.name: Photo Set
outputs.desc: Typed PhotoSet record (Frahan.Core.ScanIngest.PhotoSet). Wire  into downstream Frahan ingest components.

outputs.name: Photo Count
outputs.desc: Total photo count.

outputs.name: Total Size MB
outputs.desc: Total size in megabytes.

outputs.name: Remarks
outputs.desc: Per-bucket counts + date range + extensions seen.

name: Marker Registration
description: Closed-form rigid alignment of N≥3 source/target point pairs  (Horn 1987 quaternion absolute orientation). Use for marker-  or reference-object-based scan-to-world registration.  Implements absolute orientation (Horn 1987).

algorithm.title: Absolute orientation (Horn 1987)
algorithm.citation: Horn, B.K.P. (1987). Closed-form solution of absolute orientation using unit quaternions. J. Opt. Soc. Am. A 4(4):629-642

inputs.name: Source Points
inputs.desc: Source-frame (e.g. scan) marker positions.  Must have N≥3 points paired by INDEX with Target Points.

inputs.name: Target Points
inputs.desc: Target-frame (e.g. world) marker positions.  Same count as Source Points; pairing is by index.

outputs.name: Transform
outputs.desc: Rigid transform mapping source onto target (apply to scan).

outputs.name: RMS Error
outputs.desc: Root-mean-square per-pair residual after applying Transform  (model-unit distance).

outputs.name: Per-Pair Residuals
outputs.desc: Distance from R·sᵢ+t to tᵢ for each input pair. Long-tail  values indicate bad markers — drop or re-survey them.

outputs.name: Pair Count
outputs.desc: Number of input pairs used.

name: Mesh AABB
description: Axis-aligned bounding box of a mesh. Outputs the box, its  X/Y/Z extents, and the centre point. Useful for verifying  block dimensions match a wall's expected course height.

inputs.name: Mesh
inputs.desc: Input mesh.

outputs.name: Box
outputs.desc: Axis-aligned bounding box.

outputs.name: Width
outputs.desc: Extent along +X (document units).

outputs.name: Depth
outputs.desc: Extent along +Y (document units).

outputs.name: Height
outputs.desc: Extent along +Z (document units).

outputs.name: Centre
outputs.desc: Centre of the bounding box.

name: Mesh Diagnostics
description: Read a Rhino Mesh and report vertex/face/triangle/quad counts,  IsClosed, IsManifold, HasConsistentWinding, AverageEdgeLength,  BoundingBoxVolume.  Frahan-original method.

algorithm.title: Mesh quality diagnostics
algorithm.citation: Frahan-original

inputs.name: Mesh
inputs.desc: Mesh to diagnose.

outputs.name: Vertex Count
outputs.desc: Number of vertices.

outputs.name: Face Count
outputs.desc: Number of faces.

outputs.name: Triangle Count
outputs.desc: Number of triangular faces.

outputs.name: Quad Count
outputs.desc: Number of quad faces.

outputs.name: Is Closed
outputs.desc: True if the mesh is closed (watertight).

outputs.name: Is Manifold
outputs.desc: True if the mesh is manifold.

outputs.name: Has Consistent Winding
outputs.desc: True if face windings are consistent.

outputs.name: Average Edge Length
outputs.desc: Mean of all unique edge lengths.

outputs.name: Bounding Box Volume
outputs.desc: Volume of axis-aligned bounding box.

outputs.name: Report
outputs.desc: Single-line summary.

name: Mesh PCA
description: Principal-component analysis of a mesh's vertex cloud. Returns  a Plane aligned to the natural axes (PC1 = longest, PC2 =  second, PC3 = shortest = plane normal), plus the three extent  lengths along each axis. Use to align rough quarry blocks.  Frahan-original method.

algorithm.title: Principal component analysis (covariance eigendecomposition)
algorithm.citation: Frahan-original

inputs.name: Mesh
inputs.desc: Input mesh.

outputs.name: Frame
outputs.desc: Plane at the centroid, X-axis = PC1 (longest), Y-axis = PC2,  Z-axis = PC3 (shortest, = plane normal).

outputs.name: Length 1
outputs.desc: Extent along PC1 (longest principal axis).

outputs.name: Length 2
outputs.desc: Extent along PC2.

outputs.name: Length 3
outputs.desc: Extent along PC3 (shortest, = thickness through the plane normal).

outputs.name: Centroid
outputs.desc: Centroid of the vertex cloud (unweighted average).

name: Mesh Repair
description: Run the Frahan mesh-repair pipeline (cull degenerate / weld / cull  unused / heal naked edges / unify normals / recompute normals) and  return the repaired mesh plus a per-step trace. [Botsch et al. 2010]

algorithm.title: Mesh-repair recipe
algorithm.citation: Botsch, Kobbelt, Pauly, Alliez, Levy 2010 Polygon Mesh Processing (AK Peters / CRC Press), ISBN 978-1568814261

inputs.name: Meshes
inputs.desc: Mesh(es) to repair. Originals are not mutated.

inputs.name: Weld Angle
inputs.desc: Weld vertices whose face normals fall within this angle (radians).  Default = pi/8 (~22.5 deg).

inputs.name: Heal Distance
inputs.desc: Maximum naked-edge gap to heal (model units). Default = 0.001.

outputs.name: Repaired
outputs.desc: Repaired mesh per input.

outputs.name: Trace
outputs.desc: Per-mesh repair trace (one multi-line string per input mesh).

outputs.name: Skipped
outputs.desc: Number of meshes skipped (null input or pipeline threw).

outputs.name: Summary
outputs.desc: One-line summary.

name: Move to Origin
description: Recenter geometry (mesh / cloud / curves / blocks) to the world  origin as a group. Fixes geometry built from UTM-coordinate scans  that lands far from the origin. Emits the applied Transform and its  inverse so you can map the result back into world space.

inputs.name: Geometry
inputs.desc: Geometry to recenter (any type; recentered together as one group).

inputs.name: Anchor
inputs.desc: Which point maps to the target: 0 = bounding-box center,  1 = base (center XY, min Z) [good for 'set on the ground'],  2 = bounding-box min corner. Default 1.

inputs.name: Target
inputs.desc: Where the anchor lands. Default world origin (0,0,0).

outputs.name: Geometry
outputs.desc: Recentered geometry.

outputs.name: Transform
outputs.desc: The translation applied (world -> origin).

outputs.name: Inverse
outputs.desc: Inverse translation (origin -> world); maps results back.

outputs.name: Anchor Point
outputs.desc: The source anchor point (in world coords).

name: Read LAS Cloud
description: Read a .las / .laz LiDAR or TLS point cloud and voxel-downsample  on the fly. Handles both uncompressed .las and compressed .laz.  Memory is bounded by occupied voxels, not the input point count,  so very large clouds (100M+ points) load without materialising  the full set. The LAS scale + offset are applied, so points are  in real-world coordinates. Runs on a background thread (Run gate);  the canvas stays responsive. Use upstream of Cloud ICP / Scale  Calibrate. [Isenburg 2013]

algorithm.title: LASzip LAS/LAZ decode
algorithm.citation: Isenburg 2013 (LASzip lossless LiDAR compression)

inputs.name: File Path
inputs.desc: Path to a .las (uncompressed) or .laz (compressed) point-cloud file.

inputs.name: Voxel Size
inputs.desc: Edge length of the cubic downsample voxel in model units.  Default 0.05. If <= 0, no downsample (warns for huge files).

inputs.name: Run
inputs.desc: Set true to read the file (on a background thread). False = idle;  nothing is read, the canvas never freezes.

outputs.name: Points
outputs.desc: One centroid per occupied voxel (or all points when Voxel Size <= 0).  Real-world coordinates (LAS scale + offset applied).

outputs.name: Input Count
outputs.desc: Total points read from the file.

outputs.name: Output Count
outputs.desc: Number of output points (occupied voxels).

outputs.name: Bounding Box
outputs.desc: Axis-aligned bounding box of the input cloud.

outputs.name: Cloud
outputs.desc: Downsampled cloud as a single native PointCloud - fast viewport  display and bake-ready (far cheaper than the Points list for big  clouds). Wire into a Point param to explode it back into points.

name: Read Metashape Project
description: Read an Agisoft Metashape .psx project into a typed  MetashapeProject record: sensor calibration, chunk transform,  camera + marker counts, and the resolved mesh.ply path. With  Extract Mesh true, the component unzips mesh.ply to a temp  dir and returns the on-disk path so Frahan's Load PLY Mesh  component can consume it directly.

algorithm.title: XML + nested-zip walk: .psx -> .files/project.zip -> 0/chunk.zip
algorithm.citation: Frahan-original; tolerant XML parser handles version skew

inputs.name: Psx File
inputs.desc: Path to an Agisoft Metashape .psx project descriptor. The  sibling .files/ directory must be present (Metashape's standard  save convention).

inputs.name: Chunk Id
inputs.desc: Which chunk to return. Default 0 (the active chunk in most  projects). Use -1 to return the project's active chunk per the  .psx active_id.

inputs.name: Extract Mesh
inputs.desc: If true, extract the chunk's mesh.ply to a temp dir and return  the on-disk path via Resolved Ply. Default true.

outputs.name: Metashape Project
outputs.desc: Typed MetashapeProject record (Frahan.Core.ScanIngest.MetashapeProject).

outputs.name: Doc Version
outputs.desc: Document schema version (e.g. 1.2.0).

outputs.name: Metashape Version
outputs.desc: Metashape application version if recoverable.

outputs.name: Chunk Count
outputs.desc: Number of chunks in the project.

outputs.name: Chunk Plane
outputs.desc: Chunk transform represented as a Rhino Plane (origin + axes).

outputs.name: Chunk Scale
outputs.desc: Chunk scale factor (Metashape internal units -> world units).

outputs.name: Camera Count
outputs.desc: Camera count in the selected chunk.

outputs.name: Marker World Positions
outputs.desc: Reference (world) marker positions, one per marker with reference data.

outputs.name: Marker Labels
outputs.desc: Marker labels parallel to Marker World Positions.

outputs.name: Resolved Ply
outputs.desc: On-disk path to mesh.ply (when Extract Mesh = true). Wire into  Frahan's Load PLY Mesh.

outputs.name: Remarks
outputs.desc: Per-pipeline diagnostics + parser flags.

name: Sanitize Mesh
description: Make a mesh valid so CGAL ops accept it: triangulate non-tri faces,  stitch coincident borders, remove degenerate faces, orient/unify  normals, drop unused vertices. Use upstream of CGAL boolean / cut  components and on Alpha-Shape / scan-reconstruction output that  comes out non-manifold or unwelded.

algorithm.title: CGAL PMP mesh repair
algorithm.citation: CGAL Polygon Mesh Processing: triangulate_faces + stitch_borders + remove_degenerate_faces + orient_to_bound_a_volume

inputs.name: Mesh
inputs.desc: Mesh to sanitize.

inputs.name: Backend
inputs.desc: 0 = CGAL (strict; what CGAL ops need), 1 = Geogram (robust repair),  2 = Auto (Geogram then CGAL). Default 0.

inputs.name: Run
inputs.desc: Set true to sanitize.

outputs.name: Mesh
outputs.desc: Sanitized mesh.

outputs.name: CGAL Ready
outputs.desc: True if the output is closed + manifold (CGAL booleans will accept it).

outputs.name: Report
outputs.desc: Before/after validity summary.

name: Scan Read
description: Loads a mesh from a .ply, .obj, or .stl file via pure-managed  parsers (no third-party native code). PLY: ASCII + binary_LE;  OBJ: v + f with vertex/tex/normal triplet syntax, multi-group  files emit one mesh per group; STL: ASCII + binary, vertex  welding at 1e-7 model units. Vertex colours preserved on PLY. [Turk 1994]

algorithm.title: PLY parse
algorithm.citation: Turk 1994 (PLY Polygon File Format)

inputs.name: File Path
inputs.desc: Absolute path to a .ply / .obj / .stl file.

inputs.name: Format
inputs.desc: 0 = Auto (detect from extension and magic bytes),  1 = PLY, 2 = OBJ, 3 = STL.

outputs.name: Meshes
outputs.desc: One mesh per group/object in the source file. PLY and STL  always produce a single mesh; OBJ may produce many.

outputs.name: Names
outputs.desc: Per-mesh name (OBJ group/object name, PLY/STL file stem).

outputs.name: Vertex Counts
outputs.desc: Per-mesh vertex count.

outputs.name: Triangle Counts
outputs.desc: Per-mesh triangle count.

outputs.name: Detected Format
outputs.desc: Format the dispatcher actually used (PLY / OBJ / STL).

name: Scan Reconstruct
description: Reconstruct a closed mesh from a point cloud. Three backends:  Alpha Shape (CGAL; tight; preserves edges), Poisson (Geogram- bundled PoissonRecon, CGAL fallback; smooth; needs oriented  normals), and Advancing-Front (CGAL; BPA-equivalent; tolerant of  unoriented input). Runs on a background thread (Run gate) so the  canvas never freezes. Requires the Phase H rebuild of  frahan_cgal.dll / frahan_geogram.dll. [Edelsbrunner & Mücke 1994]

algorithm.title: 3D Alpha Shapes
algorithm.citation: Edelsbrunner & Mücke 1994 (three-dimensional alpha shapes)

inputs.name: Points
inputs.desc: Input point cloud as a point list. Optional if Cloud is wired.

inputs.name: Normals
inputs.desc: Optional per-point oriented normals (required for Poisson;  ignored by Alpha Shape and Advancing-Front).

inputs.name: Mode
inputs.desc: 0 = Auto, 1 = AlphaShape, 2 = Poisson (Geogram), 3 = AdvancingFront,  4 = Poisson (CGAL). Auto picks AlphaShape with find_optimal_alpha(1)  and falls back to Advancing-Front. All run in an isolated worker  process, so a backend crash cannot take down Rhino.

inputs.name: Alpha
inputs.desc: Alpha value for AlphaShape mode. <= 0 uses CGAL's  find_optimal_alpha(1).

inputs.name: Poisson Depth
inputs.desc: Octree depth for Poisson mode. Typical 7-9. <= 0 uses 8.

inputs.name: Samples Per Node
inputs.desc: Poisson samples-per-leaf-node. <= 0 uses 1.5.

inputs.name: Radius Ratio
inputs.desc: Advancing-Front radius ratio. <= 0 uses CGAL default 5.0.

inputs.name: AF Beta
inputs.desc: Advancing-Front sharp-edge parameter. <= 0 uses 0.52.

inputs.name: Cloud
inputs.desc: Input as a single native PointCloud (lag-free; preferred for large  scans). If it carries normals (from Estimate Cloud Normals' Cloud  output), Poisson uses them. If wired, the Points / Normals lists are ignored.

inputs.name: Run
inputs.desc: Set true to reconstruct (on a background thread). False = idle;  nothing runs, the canvas never freezes.

outputs.name: Mesh
outputs.desc: Reconstructed mesh.

outputs.name: Used Mode
outputs.desc: Which backend actually ran (AlphaShape / Poisson /  AdvancingFront / None).

outputs.name: Report
outputs.desc: One-line summary: input count, output verts / tris, mode.

name: Scan Scale Calibrate
description: Derive a uniform scale Transform from a measured reference  curve in the scan and the curve's real-world length.  Optionally apply the transform to a list of input meshes.  Closes the unit-ambiguity gap in photogrammetry / scan workflows.  Frahan-original method.

algorithm.title: Known-distance scale calibration
algorithm.citation: Frahan-original

inputs.name: Measured Curve
inputs.desc: A curve in the scan frame whose real-world length is known  (e.g. picked between two corners of a printed scale bar).

inputs.name: Reference Length
inputs.desc: The real-world length the curve should represent, in the  target unit system (Z output).

inputs.name: Meshes
inputs.desc: Optional scan meshes to scale. When wired, the Scaled Meshes  output carries the transformed copies; otherwise that output  is empty.

inputs.name: Units
inputs.desc: Free-form unit label for the report output ("m", "mm",  "ft", etc.). Math is unit-agnostic; this is display only. m

outputs.name: Scale Transform
outputs.desc: Uniform scale transform centred at the world origin. Apply  to any scan-frame geometry to bring it into the target frame.

outputs.name: Scale Factor
outputs.desc: Uniform scale factor = Reference Length / Measured Length.

outputs.name: Measured Length
outputs.desc: Length of the measured curve in source-frame units.

outputs.name: Scaled Meshes
outputs.desc: Input meshes after applying the scale transform. Empty when  no Meshes input is wired.

outputs.name: Report
outputs.desc: Human-readable summary line.

name: Stone Prep (Scan)
description: One-button cleanup pipeline for scanned stones:  Repair → optional Decimate → StoneDescriptor. Wraps the  existing Frahan repair + Rhino quadric decimation + Stone  Descriptor builder. Pure managed.

inputs.name: Meshes
inputs.desc: Scanned stone meshes to clean.

inputs.name: Ids
inputs.desc: Per-stone id. Missing entries default to "stone-{index}".

inputs.name: Repair
inputs.desc: Run the Frahan MeshRepair pipeline (cull degenerate, weld,  fill small holes).

inputs.name: Decimate
inputs.desc: Run quadric edge-collapse decimation to reach Target  Triangle Count (via RhinoCommon's managed Mesh.Reduce).

inputs.name: Target Triangle Count
inputs.desc: Target triangle count for the Decimate stage. 0 disables  decimation regardless of the Decimate toggle.

outputs.name: Cleaned Meshes
outputs.desc: Per-stone cleaned mesh after Repair (+ optional Decimate).

outputs.name: Descriptors
outputs.desc: StoneDescriptor per stone (opaque; consumable by Pack3D and  downstream Frahan 3D-packing tools).

outputs.name: Mesh Volumes
outputs.desc: Per-stone signed mesh volume.

outputs.name: Compactness
outputs.desc: Per-stone compactness (MeshVolume / AabbVolume).

outputs.name: Triangle Counts
outputs.desc: Per-stone triangle count after the pipeline.

outputs.name: Trace
outputs.desc: Multi-line per-stone trace of every stage.

outputs.name: Skipped
outputs.desc: Number of stones skipped (null mesh, builder threw, etc.).

name: Voxel Downsample
description: Reduce a point cloud by averaging points within each voxel.  Native Geogram path (Phase I shim) when available; managed  hash-grid fallback otherwise. Use upstream of Cloud ICP for  interactive ~10M+-point clouds.

algorithm.title: Voxel-grid centroid downsample
algorithm.citation: Voxel-grid filter: one centroid per occupied cubic cell

inputs.name: Points
inputs.desc: Input cloud.

inputs.name: Voxel Size
inputs.desc: Edge length of the cubic voxel in model units.

outputs.name: Downsampled
outputs.desc: One centroid per non-empty voxel.

outputs.name: Reduction Factor
outputs.desc: Output count / input count.

outputs.name: Output Count
outputs.desc: Number of centroids.

name: Clean Scan Mesh
description: Peel long 'cap' triangles, near-vertical gap webs and slivers from a reconstructed  scan mesh, then drop tiny disconnected islands. Thresholds are relative to the median  edge length, so it works at any survey scale. Wraps Core TinPeelFilter (card A2).

algorithm.title: TIN border-peel terrain cleanup (median-edge / verticality / cap-angle + component size)
algorithm.citation: Fade2D land-survey peelOffIf; scale-relative thresholds (GeometryNumerics T2)

inputs.name: Mesh
inputs.desc: Reconstructed scan mesh to clean.

inputs.name: Long Edge k
inputs.desc: Remove border triangles whose longest edge exceeds k * median edge (3 = aggressive, 10 = careful).

inputs.name: Max Tilt
inputs.desc: Remove near-vertical border facets steeper than this (deg). Default 85.

inputs.name: Max Cap Angle
inputs.desc: Remove border facets whose angle opposite the border edge exceeds this (deg). Default 140.

inputs.name: Min Component
inputs.desc: Drop connected components smaller than this many triangles. Default 50.

outputs.name: Clean Mesh
outputs.desc: Peeled mesh (kept triangles only).

outputs.name: Peeled
outputs.desc: Triangles removed by the peel predicate.

outputs.name: Size Dropped
outputs.desc: Triangles dropped as tiny components.

outputs.name: Report
outputs.desc: Summary.

name: Construct GPR Preset
description: Build a custom GPR ingestion preset for ANY stone / antenna (the library ships only two  empirically tuned presets, marble_600 and granite_160). Set the EM velocity (or a relative  permittivity to derive it), the antenna frequency, and the reflector detection + continuity  gates. Wire the output into GPR Survey Grid > Custom Preset to override the named preset.  Tip: a stone sold as 'marble' may be a compact limestone - match the velocity/frequency to the  survey, not the trade name.

algorithm.title: Constructs a GPR preset: velocity (or eps_r), frequency, energy + continuity gates
algorithm.citation: EM velocity v = c/sqrt(eps_r), c = 0.2998 m/ns; depth = v*t/2. Continuity gate per USGS Mirror Lake WRIR 99-4018C.

inputs.name: Stone
inputs.desc: Stone / material label (e.g. limestone, marble, granite, travertine). custom

inputs.name: Frequency
inputs.desc: Antenna centre frequency (MHz). Sets the lambda/4 resolution.

inputs.name: Velocity
inputs.desc: EM velocity (m/ns); depth = v*t/2. If <= 0 it is derived from Eps_r.  Marble/limestone ~0.10, granite ~0.12, travertine ~0.11.

inputs.name: Eps_r
inputs.desc: Relative permittivity. Used to derive Velocity when Velocity <= 0  (v = 0.2998/sqrt(Eps_r)); otherwise Eps_r is recomputed from Velocity for consistency.

inputs.name: Energy Quantile
inputs.desc: Reflector detection threshold (0..1) on the Hilbert energy;  higher keeps only the strongest reflectors. Marble/granite empirical ~0.985.

inputs.name: Continuity Traces
inputs.desc: Reflector continuity gate in traces: a reflector must  persist this many traces to be kept. Marble veins are short (~27 traces ~0.65 m); granite shear zones  longer (~41 traces ~1 m).

inputs.name: Migrate
inputs.desc: f-k (Stolt) migration on each line (repositions dipping reflectors).

outputs.name: Preset
outputs.desc: Constructed GPR preset. Wire into GPR Survey Grid > Custom Preset.

name: Discontinuity Ingest
description: Read mapped structural discontinuities (joints / faults / bedding / measured planes / digitised  traces) from a vector file (.csv / .geojson / .dxf / .shp) into Rhino planes + trace curves +  per-feature dip / dip-direction / set id. The ingest twin of Discontinuity Sets (Async).  Bad rows are skipped with a warning.

algorithm.title: Discontinuity ingest
algorithm.citation: ISRM Suggested Methods (Brown 1981) dip/dip-direction; trace->plane TLS PCA

inputs.name: File
inputs.desc: Path to a discontinuity vector file: .csv (dip,dipdir[,x,y,z] | nx,ny,nz[,x,y,z] | a,b,c,d),  .geojson, .dxf (LINE / LWPOLYLINE / POLYLINE / 3DFACE), or .shp.

inputs.name: Origin
inputs.desc: Optional local offset added to every parsed coordinate (e.g. to bring a UTM survey back to a  Rhino-friendly origin). Default (0,0,0).

outputs.name: Planes
outputs.desc: One plane per oriented feature (origin = centroid, normal = lower-hemisphere pole).

outputs.name: Traces
outputs.desc: Digitised trace polylines (DXF / GeoJSON / SHP lines).

outputs.name: Dip
outputs.desc: Per-oriented-feature dip (deg, 0..90).

outputs.name: Dip dir
outputs.desc: Per-oriented-feature dip-direction (deg, 0..360).

outputs.name: Set id
outputs.desc: Per-oriented-feature set id (-1 if the file did not classify it).

outputs.name: Report
outputs.desc: Counts, CRS, and any skipped-row warnings.

name: Discontinuity Sets (Async)
description: Lag-free, 10M-capable point-cloud -> joint sets. Runs a clean-room out-of-process worker on a  background task (canvas never blocks). Feed a PointCloud or a .ply path. Outputs the cloud coloured  by joint set + per-set dip / dip-direction / spacing.

algorithm.title: Planar-facet extraction
algorithm.citation: FACETS (Dewez et al. 2016); Frahan clean-room C++ worker

inputs.name: Cloud
inputs.desc: Point cloud (PointCloud / mesh vertices). Optional if File is given.

inputs.name: File
inputs.desc: Path to a .ply cloud (use for very large clouds instead of Cloud).

inputs.name: K
inputs.desc: Neighbours for PCA normals.

inputs.name: Max angle
inputs.desc: Region-grow normal agreement (deg).

inputs.name: Min facet pts
inputs.desc: Minimum points per facet.

inputs.name: Bandwidth
inputs.desc: Mean-shift angular bandwidth (deg).

inputs.name: Min set facets
inputs.desc: Minimum facets per joint set.

inputs.name: Max points
inputs.desc: Work budget; clouds larger than this are stride-downsampled. Runs off-process so the canvas never blocks -- higher resolves more joint sets (6M ~ 10 s, full 8M ~ 15 s).

inputs.name: Run
inputs.desc: Set true to segment (runs off-process, async).

inputs.name: Keep facets
inputs.desc: Copy the worker's facets.csv (per-facet pole + set id) to a stable path and expose it on the 'Facets path' output, for the Stereonet + Block Size card.

outputs.name: Segmented
outputs.desc: Cloud coloured by joint set (unassigned = grey).

outputs.name: Set poles
outputs.desc: A pole line per joint set through the cloud centroid.

outputs.name: Dip
outputs.desc: Per-set dip (deg).

outputs.name: Dip dir
outputs.desc: Per-set dip-direction (deg).

outputs.name: Spacing
outputs.desc: Per-set mean normal spacing.

outputs.name: Facets/set
outputs.desc: Per-set facet count.

outputs.name: Report
outputs.desc: Summary + timings.

outputs.name: Share
outputs.desc: Per-set fraction of facet points (set dominance).

outputs.name: Facets path
outputs.desc: Path to the copied facets.csv (empty unless 'Keep facets' is true). Feed the Stereonet + Block Size card.

name: Discontinuity Sets (Cloud)
description: Extract planar facets from a rock-face point cloud and cluster their poles into joint sets  (managed FACETS + DSE). Outputs the cloud coloured by joint set plus per-set dip / dip-direction /  spacing. Subsample very large clouds first.

algorithm.title: Planar-facet extraction
algorithm.citation: FACETS (Dewez, Girardeau-Montaut et al. 2016); Frahan managed port

inputs.name: Cloud
inputs.desc: Rock-face point cloud (PointCloud, or points/mesh vertices).

inputs.name: K
inputs.desc: Neighbours for PCA normals.

inputs.name: Max angle
inputs.desc: Region-grow normal agreement (deg).

inputs.name: Min facet pts
inputs.desc: Minimum points per facet.

inputs.name: Bandwidth
inputs.desc: Mean-shift angular bandwidth for joint sets (deg).

inputs.name: Min set facets
inputs.desc: Minimum facets per joint set.

inputs.name: Run
inputs.desc: Set true to segment.

outputs.name: Segmented
outputs.desc: Cloud coloured by joint set (unassigned = grey).

outputs.name: Set poles
outputs.desc: A pole line per joint set through the cloud centroid.

outputs.name: Dip
outputs.desc: Per-set dip (deg).

outputs.name: Dip dir
outputs.desc: Per-set dip-direction (deg).

outputs.name: Spacing
outputs.desc: Per-set mean normal spacing.

outputs.name: Facets/set
outputs.desc: Per-set facet count.

outputs.name: Report
outputs.desc: Summary.

name: Extraction Order Optimizer
description: Order BenchBlocks by score = w_yield*yield - w_risk*risk -  w_access*access. Blocks under min yield are skipped. Frahan-original method.

algorithm.title: Weighted-sum greedy extraction-order sort
algorithm.citation: Frahan-original

inputs.name: Inventory
inputs.desc: QuarryInventory.

inputs.name: Estimates
inputs.desc: BlockYieldEstimate list.

inputs.name: Yield Weight
inputs.desc: Score weight on yield fraction.

inputs.name: Risk Weight
inputs.desc: Score weight on fracture risk.

inputs.name: Access Weight
inputs.desc: Score weight on access cost.

inputs.name: Min Yield
inputs.desc: Yield fraction 0..1 below which a block is skipped.

inputs.name: Access Normaliser
inputs.desc: Divisor for access cost.

outputs.name: Plan
outputs.desc: ExtractionPlan object.

outputs.name: Order Ids
outputs.desc: Block ids in extraction order.

outputs.name: Scores
outputs.desc: Score for each accepted block.

outputs.name: Skipped Ids
outputs.desc: Block ids skipped (low yield).

outputs.name: Total Recoverable (m^3)
outputs.desc: Sum of recoverable volumes.

outputs.name: Total Waste (m^3)
outputs.desc: Sum of waste volumes.

name: GPR Bedrock Surface
description: Build a bedrock / rock-face-top surface mesh from GPR reflector picks. Takes the  deepest continuous reflector per column as bedrock, resamples its depth onto a ground  mesh's vertices (k-NN IDW), and outputs a bedrock mesh (ground topology, z = ground z -  depth) for Overburden To Rock Face. Wraps Core BedrockSurface + TinMerge (A9 + A3). [Porsani 2006]

algorithm.title: GPR-to-bedrock surface: deepest continuous reflector + k-NN IDW resample onto a ground TIN
algorithm.citation: Porsani 2006 / Isakova 2021 (top-of-fresh-rock reflector); Shepard 1968 (IDW); scale-relative radius (GeometryNumerics T2)

inputs.name: Ground
inputs.desc: Ground / topographic TIN (supplies the (x,y) sample set + datum).

inputs.name: Picks
inputs.desc: GPR reflector pick points. Depth is taken from -Z unless Depths is supplied.

inputs.name: Depths
inputs.desc: Optional explicit depth (m) per pick (overrides -Z). Empty = use -Z.

inputs.name: Min Depth
inputs.desc: Ignore picks shallower than this (m) -- skip the weathered cover. Default 0.

inputs.name: Column Cell
inputs.desc: Bin (x,y) to this cell (m) when reducing to one bedrock pick per column. 0 = exact. Default 0.25.

inputs.name: Neighbors
inputs.desc: k nearest picks for the IDW resample. Default 6.

outputs.name: Bedrock Mesh
outputs.desc: Bedrock surface mesh (ground topology, z = ground z - interpolated depth) for Overburden To Rock Face.

outputs.name: Bedrock Points
outputs.desc: Scattered bedrock points (deepest reflector per column).

outputs.name: Unresolved
outputs.desc: Ground vertices with no pick within range (clipped).

outputs.name: Report
outputs.desc: Summary.

name: GPR Fracture Extract
