description: Process a GPR radargram and extract fracture reflectors. Reads IDS .dt /  MALA .rd3 / GSSI .dzt / pulseEKKO .dt1 / SEG-Y / CSV. Runs dewow -> background  removal -> time-zero mute -> gain -> f-k (Stolt) migration -> Hilbert energy ->  USGS >=40-trace continuity extraction. Choose a STONE x FREQUENCY preset for  tuned defaults (marble_600, granite_160, ...); override any knob (set < 0 to use  the preset). Outputs fracture picks, depths, confidence, and a depth-converted  energy mesh. Reads Geoscanners .gsf natively (GsfReader).  Workflows cross-checked against RGPR (the open R GPR-processing package) in the companion paper.

algorithm.title: GPR fracture extraction: f-k (Stolt) migration + Hilbert instantaneous energy + USGS continuity
algorithm.citation: Stolt 1978 (f-k migration); Taner 1979 (instantaneous attributes via Hilbert); USGS Mirror Lake WRIR 99-4018C (>=40-trace continuity); Porsani 2006 + Isakova 2021 (high-energy = fracture)

inputs.name: File
inputs.desc: Path to the GPR radargram file (.dt / .rd3 / .dzt / .dt1 / .sgy / .csv).

inputs.name: Preset
inputs.desc: Stone x frequency preset for tuned defaults:  marble_600, granite_160, travertine_390, andesite_390, limestone_200. granite_160

inputs.name: Velocity
inputs.desc: EM velocity (m/ns), depth = v*t/2. < 0 = use the preset value. Override with a  WARR/CMP-measured velocity when available (highest-leverage parameter).

inputs.name: Migrate
inputs.desc: f-k (Stolt) migration to reposition dipping reflectors / collapse diffractions.  Leave unset to use the preset.

inputs.name: Depth Equalize
inputs.desc: Per-depth energy normalisation so deep weak reflectors surface. Preset default.

inputs.name: Energy Quantile
inputs.desc: Energy quantile (0..1) above which a sample is a fracture candidate. < 0 = preset  (typically 0.985; lower it for broad CAVITY anomalies).

inputs.name: Continuity Traces
inputs.desc: USGS lateral-continuity window in traces (>= 40 keeps only continuous reflectors).  < 0 = preset (41).

inputs.name: Min Support
inputs.desc: Minimum like-picks within the continuity window to keep a pick. < 0 = preset (12).

inputs.name: Max Dip
inputs.desc: USGS dip gate (deg): continuity is followed along reflector dips up to this angle;  steeper events are rejected. 45 = the USGS crystalline-rock standard. < 0 = default 45.  Raise toward 60 to keep steeper shear zones; lower toward 20 for sub-horizontal only.

inputs.name: Trace Mode
inputs.desc: How discrete picks are grouped into continuous fracture lines: 0 = connected-components  (simple, merges crossings), 1 = orientation-gated (separates crossing fractures by local  dip). Default 0.

inputs.name: Perm Uncertainty
inputs.desc: Absolute uncertainty of the relative permittivity eps_r (e.g. 1.0 for eps_r 9+-1). Drives  the depth velocity error sigma_v/v = 0.5*dEr/eps_r, the dominant deep-fracture deviation.  Lower it (toward 0.3) when you have a CMP/WARR velocity calibration. Default 1.0.

inputs.name: Tolerance
inputs.desc: Target tolerance T (m) for the confidence metric = probability each pick's depth is within  +-T of the truth (Gaussian, 1-sigma). Default 0.02 (2 cm precision-cutting).

outputs.name: Fracture Picks
outputs.desc: Extracted fracture pick points at (distance, 0, -depth) in metres.

outputs.name: Depths
outputs.desc: Depth (m) of each pick.

outputs.name: Confidence
outputs.desc: Normalised energy (0..1) of each pick.

outputs.name: Energy Mesh
outputs.desc: Depth-converted energy section as a mesh (x=distance, z=-depth), vertex-coloured  by instantaneous energy (blue=intact -> red=fracture).

outputs.name: Bedrock Depth
outputs.desc: Depth (m) of the deepest continuous reflector = candidate bedrock / rock-face top  (feeds Overburden To Rock Face).

outputs.name: Fracture Id
outputs.desc: Continuous-fracture id per pick (aligned to Fracture Picks; 0 = unassigned). Feed into  'GPR Fractures on Mesh' Labels to drape each fracture onto a bench/block mesh.

outputs.name: Fracture Lines
outputs.desc: Continuous fracture trace polylines in the section plane (x, 0, -depth), one per  reflector (FractureTracer). Extrude / loft these into fracture surfaces.

outputs.name: Report
outputs.desc: Parameters used + extraction summary.

outputs.name: Depth Sigma
outputs.desc: Per-pick 1-sigma depth uncertainty (m), aligned to Fracture Picks: the GPR time->depth  deviation sqrt((depth*sigma_v/v)^2 + (lambda/4)^2). Grows with depth (velocity error)  off a lambda/4 resolution floor. Stage 1 of the GPR->fracture->mesh tolerance ladder.

outputs.name: Confidence within T
outputs.desc: OPTIMISATION METRIC: mean over the picks of P(|depth deviation| <= T) = erf(T/(sigma*sqrt2)).  0..1 (= the fraction of the fracture trace within +-T of truth). Raise it by calibrating  velocity (Perm Uncertainty down) or higher frequency. Section-level (no inter-line term).

name: GPR Fracture Surfaces 3D
description: Cluster a multi-line GPR pick cloud into fractures, krige each into a 3D surface,  and colour it by the GPR->fracture->mesh deviation-from-truth (tolerance ladder).  Outputs the confidence-within-tolerance metric. Managed (C# kriging; no Python).

inputs.name: Fracture Picks
inputs.desc: 3D fracture-pick cloud across the survey (x = distance, y = line offset, z = -depth).  Combine the picks of several GPR section lines (e.g. from GPR Fracture Extract).

inputs.name: Num Fractures
inputs.desc: Number of fractures to cluster the picks into by depth. < 0 = auto (depth-gap split).

inputs.name: Grid Res
inputs.desc: Surface grid resolution per axis. Default 36.

inputs.name: Velocity
inputs.desc: EM velocity (m/ns); depth = v*t/2. Default 0.1.

inputs.name: Frequency
inputs.desc: Antenna centre frequency (MHz); sets lambda/4  vertical resolution. Default 600.

inputs.name: Eps_r
inputs.desc: Relative permittivity. Default 9.

inputs.name: Perm Uncertainty
inputs.desc: Absolute eps_r uncertainty (e.g. 1.0 for 9+-1) -> velocity error sigma_v/v=0.5*dEr/eps_r  (the dominant deep-fracture deviation). Lower with a CMP/WARR calibration. Default 1.0.

inputs.name: Tolerance
inputs.desc: Target tolerance T (m) for the confidence metric P(|deviation| <= T). Default 0.02.

inputs.name: Assume Open
inputs.desc: Treat the fractures as OPEN (fluid/air-filled) when scoring detectability. Surface GPR  mainly images OPEN fractures; sealed ones are largely missed (Molron 2020). Default true.

inputs.name: Time-Zero
inputs.desc: Direct-wave time-zero pick window (ns) -> rectangular sigma_t0=((t0)/2)/sqrt3 added to  sigma_recon (Xie 2021; dominant near the surface). 0 = off. Default 0.

inputs.name: Detect Base
inputs.desc: STONE-SPECIFIC base imaging efficiency for detectability (0..1): the detected fraction  for ideal open sub-horizontal fractures. Crystalline/granite ~0.80-0.91 (Molron 2020 /  Dorn 2012, low loss); attenuating/clay-prone stone (marble, limestone) is lower. Default  0.80 (granite, MEASURED Molron 2020). Per-stone (GprDetectionCalibration): limestone 0.90,  sandstone 0.80, marble/travertine 0.75, andesite 0.50, tuff 0.38. ONLY stone-specific detection  knob; velocity/eps_r/frequency still set sigma_recon + the (now depth-aware) size floor.

inputs.name: Through Picks
inputs.desc: EXACT interpolation: collapse each fracture's picks to one PEAK pick per cell (keep the  highest-energy reflector) and krige with a near-zero nugget so the surface passes THROUGH  every peak pick (posterior sigma ~0 at picks) and spans the full survey footprint as one  continuous dipping sheet. False = smoothing fit (the old behaviour). Default true.

inputs.name: Pick Energy
inputs.desc: OPTIONAL per-pick energy/confidence (0..1), aligned to Fracture Picks (wire the Confidence  output of GPR Survey Grid / GPR Fracture Extract). With Through Picks on, the PEAK (highest-  energy) pick is kept per cell. Omit to keep the pick nearest the local trend.

inputs.name: Peak Dedup
inputs.desc: K2 (default true): collapse each cell to its single PEAK reflector before kriging -> smoothest  sheet, lowest residual, rides the strong reflectors. False = K1: keep EVERY pick as a hard  constraint -> maximum fidelity to the raw cloud, marginally lower posterior sigma, but the  surface buckles where near-coincident picks disagree. Only applies when Through Picks is on.

outputs.name: Fracture Surfaces
outputs.desc: One kriged 3D fracture surface mesh per fracture, vertex-coloured by the total  deviation-from-truth sigma (green <= T -> red).

outputs.name: Confidence
outputs.desc: OPTIMISATION METRIC per fracture: mean over the surface of P(|deviation| <= T)  (0..1).

outputs.name: Mean Sigma
outputs.desc: Mean total deviation sigma (m) per fracture surface.

outputs.name: Overall Confidence
outputs.desc: Area-mean confidence across all fractures (0..1) -- the single number to optimise.

outputs.name: Report
outputs.desc: Per-fracture tolerance-ladder summary.

outputs.name: Detectability
outputs.desc: DETECTION rung per fracture (0..1): probability surface GPR images it, from its mean dip,  openness and area (Molron 2020 / Dorn 2012). Low = a fracture that may be MISSED.

outputs.name: Effective Confidence
outputs.desc: Detection-adjusted overall confidence = Overall Confidence x detection completeness.  Accounts for fractures that may be missed, not just mislocated. The honest yield-safety number.

name: GPR Fractures on Mesh
description: Overlay GPR reflector picks onto a target bench/block mesh: drape  each pick onto the surface, connect picks into per-fracture trace  curves, and (optionally) build fracture sheets from the surface down  to the reflector depth for use with Cut By Fractures / BlockCutOpt.  Feed Pick Points from GPR Radargram Mesh; put both in the same frame  with Move to Origin first.

algorithm.title: GPR reflector drape + fracture-sheet build
algorithm.citation: Frahan-original: project reflector picks onto a target mesh; loft surface-to-reflector ribbons per fracture

inputs.name: Mesh
inputs.desc: Target bench / block mesh (same coordinate frame as the picks).

inputs.name: Picks
inputs.desc: GPR reflector pick points (from GPR Radargram Mesh).

inputs.name: Labels
inputs.desc: Optional fracture label per pick (groups picks into distinct fractures).  If absent or mismatched, all picks form one fracture.

inputs.name: Project
inputs.desc: How a pick maps to the mesh: 0 = closest point on mesh, 1 = drop along -Z,  2 = raise along +Z. Default 0.

inputs.name: Make Sheets
inputs.desc: Also build fracture sheets (ribbons from the draped surface point down to the  reflector pick) as meshes, for Cut By Fractures / BlockCutOpt. Default false.

outputs.name: Draped
outputs.desc: Picks projected onto the mesh surface.

outputs.name: Fracture Curves
outputs.desc: One polyline per fracture, on the mesh surface.

outputs.name: Fracture Sheets
outputs.desc: Surface->reflector ribbon mesh per fracture (when Make Sheets).

outputs.name: Report
outputs.desc: Summary.

name: GPR Radargram Reader
description: SUPERSEDED BY: GPR File Loader + GPR Fracture Extract, which read  vendor formats natively and run the validated processing chain.  Kept loadable for old canvases.  Read a Frahan-format GPR radargram (traces CSV + optional  picks CSV). Coordinates in metres. SEG-Y / DZT / RD3 must  be converted externally (RGPR).

inputs.name: Id
inputs.desc: Radargram identifier. scan-1

inputs.name: Traces CSV
inputs.desc: Path to traces CSV (x,y,dz,a0,a1,...).

inputs.name: Picks CSV
inputs.desc: Path to picks CSV (x,y,depth,conf,label). Empty = none.

outputs.name: Radargram
outputs.desc: GprRadargram object.

outputs.name: Trace XY
outputs.desc: One point per trace at (x, y, 0).

outputs.name: Pick Points
outputs.desc: One point per pick at (x, y, -depth).

outputs.name: Pick Confidence
outputs.desc: Confidence per pick (0..1).

outputs.name: Trace Count
outputs.desc: Number of traces.

outputs.name: Pick Count
outputs.desc: Number of picks.

name: GPR Survey Grid
description: Ingest a whole GPR survey GRID in ONE component. Give it the LIST of scan-line files  (.dt / .rd3 / .dzt / .dt1 / .sgy / .csv) and a Line Spacing (or explicit Line Positions);  it runs the validated GPR chain (dewow -> background -> time-zero -> gain -> f-k migration  -> Hilbert energy -> USGS continuity) on each line and lays line i at y = position[i]  (default i*spacing). Outputs one 3D fracture-pick cloud (x=distance, y=line offset, z=-depth)  plus per-pick energy -- feed Picks -> 'GPR Fracture Surfaces 3D' Fracture Picks and Confidence  -> its Pick Energy. Replaces N single-line components + Merge.

algorithm.title: GPR survey-grid ingest: per-line f-k migration + Hilbert energy + USGS continuity, laid out by line offset
algorithm.citation: Stolt 1978 (f-k migration); Taner 1979 (instantaneous attributes); USGS Mirror Lake WRIR 99-4018C (>=40-trace continuity)

inputs.name: Files
inputs.desc: The GRID of GPR scan-line files (one per survey line). .dt / .rd3 / .dzt / .dt1 / .sgy / .csv /  .gsf (Geoscanners Akula -- now read natively).

inputs.name: Preset
inputs.desc: Stone x frequency preset for tuned defaults applied to every line:  marble_600, granite_160, travertine_390, andesite_390, limestone_200. granite_160

inputs.name: Line Spacing
inputs.desc: Distance (m) between consecutive parallel scan lines. Line i is placed at y = i * spacing.  Ignored where Line Positions supplies an explicit y. Default 2.0.

inputs.name: Line Positions
inputs.desc: OPTIONAL explicit y offset (m) per line, aligned to Files (use real survey line coordinates  when you have them). Overrides Line Spacing when its count matches the file count.

inputs.name: Velocity
inputs.desc: EM velocity (m/ns), depth = v*t/2. < 0 = use the preset value. Override with a WARR/CMP- measured velocity when available (highest-leverage parameter).

inputs.name: Energy Quantile
inputs.desc: Energy quantile (0..1) above which a sample is a fracture candidate. < 0 = preset (~0.985).

inputs.name: Max Dip
inputs.desc: USGS dip gate (deg). < 0 = default 45 (crystalline-rock standard).

inputs.name: Migrate
inputs.desc: f-k (Stolt) migration on every line. Default true.

inputs.name: Orientation
inputs.desc: OPTIONAL per-line axis for a BIDIRECTIONAL grid: 0 = longitudinal (line runs along X, lines  stacked in Y), 1 = transverse / cross-line (runs along Y, stacked in X). Empty = auto-detect  from the filename (contains 'TA' -> transverse, else longitudinal); a single value applies to  all. With BOTH axes present the picks form a true crossing grid and each axis is spaced to fit  the other axis' extent (the cross-lines MEASURE the perpendicular dip instead of interpolating  it); with one axis it falls back to Line Spacing (parallel lines).

inputs.name: Custom Preset
inputs.desc: OPTIONAL constructed GPR preset (from 'Construct GPR Preset'). If provided, it OVERRIDES the named  Preset string -- use it for any stone/antenna the two built-in empirical presets do not cover.

outputs.name: Fracture Picks
outputs.desc: One 3D fracture-pick cloud across the whole survey: (distance, line offset, -depth) in metres.  Wire into GPR Fracture Surfaces 3D > Fracture Picks.

outputs.name: Line Id
outputs.desc: Survey line index (0-based) of each pick.

outputs.name: Confidence
outputs.desc: Normalised energy (0..1) of each pick. Wire into GPR Fracture Surfaces 3D > Pick Energy so the  PEAK reflector is kept per cell.

outputs.name: Depths
outputs.desc: Depth (m) of each pick.

outputs.name: Energy Sections
outputs.desc: Per-line depth-converted energy section meshes, each laid at its survey y (x=distance,  y=line offset, z=-depth), vertex-coloured by instantaneous energy.

outputs.name: Bedrock Depth
outputs.desc: Deepest continuous reflector (m) per line = candidate bedrock / rock-face top.

outputs.name: Report
outputs.desc: Per-line ingest summary.

name: GeoFractNet Inference
description: Load pre-computed GeoFractNet fracture predictions from CSV  and emit a BlockCutOpt-ready fracture Mesh clipped to a bench  AABB. Inference itself runs externally (net48 cannot host  PyTorch).

inputs.name: CSV Path
inputs.desc: Path to GeoFractNet predictions CSV.

inputs.name: Bench AABB
inputs.desc: Bounding box to clip fracture planes to.

inputs.name: Min Confidence
inputs.desc: Drop predictions below this confidence (0..1).

outputs.name: Fractures
outputs.desc: Fracture mesh ready for BlockCutOpt.

outputs.name: Planes
outputs.desc: One Rhino Plane per fracture.

outputs.name: Confidence
outputs.desc: Per-fracture confidence.

outputs.name: Set Id
outputs.desc: Per-fracture set id.

outputs.name: Triangle Count
outputs.desc: Triangles in the fracture mesh.

name: Joint Set
description: Authors a structural-geology joint set: dip direction (azimuth  of steepest descent, 0 = North), dip angle, mean spacing along  the normal, optional orientation scatter. Wire into Quarry DFN. Implements joint-set DFN authoring (ISRM/Priest 1993).

algorithm.title: Joint-set DFN authoring
algorithm.citation: ISRM Suggested Methods + Priest 1993 joint-set DFN

inputs.name: Dip Direction
inputs.desc: Azimuth of the steepest descent line, clockwise from North (+Y), in [0, 360).

inputs.name: Dip
inputs.desc: Dip angle from horizontal, in [0, 90]. 0 = horizontal joint, 90 = vertical.

inputs.name: Spacing
inputs.desc: Mean spacing along the normal (same units as the quarry block). > 0.

outputs.name: Joint Set
outputs.desc: JointSet DTO. Wire into Quarry DFN.

name: Joint Sets to DFN
description: Bridge joint sets (dip / dip-direction / spacing) into a discrete fracture network mesh clipped  to a bench box, ready for the Block Cut Optimiser. Uses only the joint-set statistics, not the  scan mesh, so an incomplete scan still works. Deterministic by seed.

algorithm.title: Joint-set DFN
algorithm.citation: Priest 1993 discrete fracture network from joint-set statistics

inputs.name: Dip
inputs.desc: Per-set dip (deg, 0..90).

inputs.name: Dip dir
inputs.desc: Per-set dip-direction (deg, 0..360).

inputs.name: Spacing
inputs.desc: Per-set mean normal spacing (cloud units). Sets with spacing<=0 are skipped.

inputs.name: Spacing scale
inputs.desc: Multiplies spacing into the bench's units (e.g. 100 to take a cm-scale detail scan to bench metres). Default 1.

inputs.name: Bench
inputs.desc: Bench / blank bounding box the DFN is clipped to (and that you also feed to the Block Cut Optimiser as Tested Area).

inputs.name: Scatter
inputs.desc: Per-set orientation scatter (deg, Fisher dispersion). One value applies to all sets. Default 0 = planar.

inputs.name: Seed
inputs.desc: Random seed (deterministic given the same inputs).

inputs.name: Exp spacing
inputs.desc: Negative-exponential spacing (Priest) instead of constant.

outputs.name: DFN
outputs.desc: Fracture-network mesh (triangulated planes clipped to the bench). Feed to Block Cut Optimiser 'Fractures'.

outputs.name: Tested area
outputs.desc: The bench box, passed through. Feed to Block Cut Optimiser 'Tested Area'.

outputs.name: Fractures
outputs.desc: Number of fracture planes clipped into the bench.

outputs.name: Sets used
outputs.desc: Joint sets actually used (spacing>0, valid orientation).

outputs.name: Report
outputs.desc: Per-set summary + DFN stats + any skipped sets.

name: Overburden To Rock Face
description: Soil volume to strip to reach the rock face: the volume between a GROUND  surface mesh and a BEDROCK surface mesh. Bedrock z is sampled vertically  under each ground vertex (common-TIN bridge); volume by exact TIN-prism  differencing (Core OverburdenVolume). Cut = soil to remove; Loose = swell- adjusted haul volume. 2.5D volume only -- get the 3D exposed face from Scan  Reconstruct for block extraction.

algorithm.title: Cut-and-fill volume by TIN prism differencing
algorithm.citation: Route-surveying prismoidal volume; difference triangulation (geom.at / Fade2D land survey)

inputs.name: Ground
inputs.desc: Ground / topographic surface mesh (e.g. from Scan Reconstruct on a LiDAR /  photogrammetry cloud). Its triangulation is used as the common TIN.

inputs.name: Bedrock
inputs.desc: Bedrock / rock-face surface mesh (e.g. reconstructed from GPR / ERT / seismic  depth picks). Sampled vertically under each ground vertex.

inputs.name: Swell
inputs.desc: Swell fraction for the loose/haul volume (e.g. 0.25 = +25%). 0 = report bank volume only.

outputs.name: Overburden (bank)
outputs.desc: Cut volume = soil above the bedrock surface, in model units^3 (bank / in-situ).

outputs.name: Loose (haul)
outputs.desc: Swell-adjusted volume to haul = V*(1+Swell).

outputs.name: Fill
outputs.desc: Volume where bedrock is ABOVE ground (rock already exposed / above the surface).

outputs.name: Net
outputs.desc: Cut - Fill (signed).

outputs.name: Plan Area
outputs.desc: Total projected (x,y) area covered by the common TIN.

outputs.name: Depth Mesh
outputs.desc: Ground mesh vertex-coloured by overburden depth (blue=thin -> red=deep) for the visual pass.

outputs.name: Report
outputs.desc: Human-readable summary.

name: Photo Detect → PLY
description: v1 reads pre-detected fracture TRACES from a CSV (x1, y1, x2, y2  in world metres) and emits the vertical-extruded PLY consumable by  BlockCutOpt. The on-image fracture detector is not yet wired (the  Origin/GSD/Flip-Y inputs are placeholders for it). Pair with GFNInfer  to write the CSV from a GeoFractNet run, or hand-author the CSV from  QGIS / AutoCAD digitisation.

inputs.name: CSV Path
inputs.desc: Trace CSV file (x1, y1, x2, y2 in metres).

inputs.name: Origin X (m)
inputs.desc: World X of pixel (0, 0). Unused by CSV backend.

inputs.name: Origin Y (m)
inputs.desc: World Y of pixel (0, 0). Unused by CSV backend.

inputs.name: GSD (m/px)
inputs.desc: Ground sampling distance. Unused by CSV backend.

inputs.name: Z Min (m)
inputs.desc: Bottom of vertical extrusion.

inputs.name: Z Max (m)
inputs.desc: Top of vertical extrusion.

inputs.name: Flip Y
inputs.desc: Pixel Y points down. Unused by CSV backend.

outputs.name: Fractures
outputs.desc: Rhino Mesh of vertically-extruded fracture triangles.

outputs.name: Trace Count
outputs.desc: Number of traces parsed.

outputs.name: Triangle Count
outputs.desc: Number of triangles emitted.

outputs.name: Backend
outputs.desc: Detector backend used.

name: Quarry DFN
description: Extracts dimension-stone blocks from a quarry mesh following  a Discrete Fracture Network defined by joint sets.  Geomechanically faithful (Priest 1993 / ISRM Suggested Methods). Implements DFN block extraction (ISRM/Priest 1993; Azarafza 2016).

algorithm.title: Discrete Fracture Network block extraction
algorithm.citation: ISRM Suggested Methods + Priest 1993 joint-set DFN

inputs.name: Quarry
inputs.desc: Convex quarry mesh (e.g. a cube of rock).

inputs.name: Joint Sets
inputs.desc: JointSet DTOs (from Joint Set component).

inputs.name: Seed
inputs.desc: Random seed (controls spacing offset within meanSpacing).

outputs.name: Blocks
outputs.desc: Extracted block meshes.

outputs.name: Slabs
outputs.desc: Same blocks as Slab DTOs (for downstream Frahan plumbing).

name: Quarry Inventory
description: Aggregate a list of bench-block AABBs into a QuarryInventory.  All units in metres.

inputs.name: Bench Id
inputs.desc: Bench identifier. bench-1

inputs.name: Block Boxes
inputs.desc: Axis-aligned bench-block footprints (m).

inputs.name: Block Ids
inputs.desc: Optional block ids. If empty, auto-generated.

inputs.name: Geology Grade
inputs.desc: Per-block geology grade 0..1 (default 1.0).

inputs.name: Access Cost
inputs.desc: Per-block access cost (default 0).

outputs.name: Inventory
outputs.desc: QuarryInventory object.

outputs.name: Count
outputs.desc: Number of blocks.

outputs.name: Total Volume (m^3)
outputs.desc: Sum of gross volumes.

outputs.name: Avg Grade
outputs.desc: Volume-weighted average geology grade.

name: Quarry Report
description: Aggregate Inventory + ExtractionPlan + SawBedSchedule into  a Markdown summary plus headline numbers.

inputs.name: Inventory
inputs.desc: QuarryInventory.

inputs.name: Plan
inputs.desc: ExtractionPlan.

inputs.name: Schedule
inputs.desc: SawBedSchedule.

outputs.name: Report
outputs.desc: QuarryReport object.

outputs.name: Markdown
outputs.desc: Report rendered as Markdown.

outputs.name: Yield (m^3)
outputs.desc: Total recoverable yield.

outputs.name: Waste (m^3)
outputs.desc: Total waste.

outputs.name: Recovery %
outputs.desc: Overall recovery percent.

outputs.name: Makespan (min)
outputs.desc: Schedule makespan.

name: Quarry Yield Estimator
description: Per-block yield estimate via BlockCutOpt as a sub-routine.  Returns one BlockYieldEstimate per BenchBlock.

inputs.name: Inventory
inputs.desc: QuarryInventory from Frahan Quarry Inventory.

inputs.name: Fractures
inputs.desc: Fracture mesh (BlockCutOpt format).

inputs.name: Product X (m)
inputs.desc: Dimension-block target X.

inputs.name: Product Y (m)
inputs.desc: Dimension-block target Y.

inputs.name: Product Z (m)
inputs.desc: Dimension-block target Z.

inputs.name: Kerf (m)
inputs.desc: Saw kerf.

inputs.name: Psi Step (deg)
inputs.desc: Angular search step.

inputs.name: Dx Max
inputs.desc: Half-range of dx (m).

inputs.name: Dx Step
inputs.desc: Dx step (m).

inputs.name: Dy Max
inputs.desc: Half-range of dy (m).

inputs.name: Dy Step
inputs.desc: Dy step (m).

inputs.name: Risk Normaliser
inputs.desc: Fracture-triangle count divisor for risk 0..1.

outputs.name: Estimates
outputs.desc: BlockYieldEstimate per BenchBlock.

outputs.name: Block Ids
outputs.desc: Block ids matching the estimates list.

outputs.name: Recovery %
outputs.desc: Per-block recovery percent.

outputs.name: Fracture Risk
outputs.desc: Per-block fracture risk 0..1.

outputs.name: Cutting Time (min)
outputs.desc: Per-block estimated cutting time.

name: Saw-Bed Schedule
description: Greedy LPT schedule of accepted blocks onto N saw beds.  Returns per-bed timelines and the total makespan. Implements greedy LPT scheduling (Graham 1969).

algorithm.title: Greedy LPT list scheduling
algorithm.citation: Graham 1969, Bounds on multiprocessing timing anomalies, SIAM J. Appl. Math. 17(2):416-429

inputs.name: Plan
inputs.desc: ExtractionPlan.

inputs.name: Bed Count
inputs.desc: Number of saw beds (>= 1).

inputs.name: Setup (min)
inputs.desc: Fixed inter-block setup time per bed.

outputs.name: Schedule
outputs.desc: SawBedSchedule object.

outputs.name: Bed Summary
outputs.desc: One line per bed.

outputs.name: Makespan (min)
outputs.desc: Schedule makespan.

outputs.name: Slot Count
outputs.desc: Total scheduled slots.

name: Scan to Block Inventory
description: Convert a 3D-scanned raw block mesh into a typed QuarryBlock  (bounds + usable volume + frame + dimensions + label) the  downstream GeoCut / GeoPack / BlockPackTree chain can nest  project parts into. Orientation: 0 = mesh frame, 1 = PCA  (longest principal axis → X), 2 = world Z. Method: 0 = OBB,  1 = inscribed AABB after PCA align, 2 = ConvexHull.

inputs.name: Scan Mesh
inputs.desc: Raw 3D-scanned block as a mesh (from a handheld scanner via  Scan Reconstruct, or a .3dm import).

inputs.name: Orient
inputs.desc: Orientation policy. 0 = keep the mesh's existing frame.  1 = align by PCA (longest principal axis → X). 2 = align by  world Z (top face flat).

inputs.name: Usable Inset
inputs.desc: Inset (model units) used to compute the usable interior  volume — accounts for kerf + scan noise + edge defects.  Negative values are clamped to 0.

inputs.name: Method
inputs.desc: Block-extraction method. 0 = OBB (oriented bounding box,  fast, deterministic). 1 = inscribed AABB after PCA align.  2 = ConvexHull.

inputs.name: Label
inputs.desc: Per-block label / provenance string carried into the typed  QuarryBlock and downstream metadata.

outputs.name: Bounds
outputs.desc: Oriented bounding-box mesh (visualisation + downstream  usable-volume input for the raw-Mesh wire path).

outputs.name: Frame
outputs.desc: Block's oriented base frame (origin + X/Y/Z axes from PCA /  world align).

outputs.name: Dimensions
outputs.desc: Block's principal dimensions (X = longest, Y = next,  Z = thinnest) in model units.

outputs.name: Volume
outputs.desc: Usable interior volume (model units cubed); accounts for  Usable Inset.

outputs.name: Report
outputs.desc: One-line summary: "Block <label> X x Y x Z units, volume V  units^3, method M".

name: Stereonet + Block Size
description: Self-presenting card: equal-area lower-hemisphere stereonet (great circles + set poles + facet-pole  density) plus an in-situ block-size readout (Jv, Palmstrom Vb, RQD, Deq). Feed the per-set Dip / Dip dir /  Spacing / Share (and optional Facets path) from Discontinuity Sets (Async). Set Unit scale to convert  spacing to metres; block-size numbers are a proxy.

algorithm.title: Equal-area stereonet
algorithm.citation: Schmidt/Lambert lower-hemisphere projection; Wulff toggle

inputs.name: Dip
inputs.desc: Per-set dip (deg).

inputs.name: Dip dir
inputs.desc: Per-set dip-direction (deg).

inputs.name: Spacing
inputs.desc: Per-set mean normal spacing (cloud units).

inputs.name: Share
inputs.desc: Per-set point share (optional; picks the 3 dominant sets).

inputs.name: Facets path
inputs.desc: Optional facets.csv path (from 'Keep facets') for the pole-density cloud.

inputs.name: Plane
inputs.desc: Base plane for the net (origin + X=East, Y=North). Default World XY.

inputs.name: Radius
inputs.desc: Net radius (model units).

inputs.name: Unit scale
inputs.desc: Multiplies spacing into metres for the block-size math (e.g. 1 if already metres).

inputs.name: Equal area
inputs.desc: True = equal-area (Schmidt); false = equal-angle (Wulff).

outputs.name: Net
outputs.desc: Primitive circle + N/E/S/W ticks.

outputs.name: Great circles
outputs.desc: Cyclographic trace per set.

outputs.name: Set poles
outputs.desc: Projected pole per set.

outputs.name: Facet poles
outputs.desc: Projected facet-pole density cloud (if Facets path given).

outputs.name: Jv
outputs.desc: Volumetric joint count (joints/m^3 proxy).

outputs.name: Vb
outputs.desc: Palmstrom block volume (m^3; NaN if < 3 sets).

outputs.name: RQD
outputs.desc: RQD proxy (0..100).

outputs.name: Deq
outputs.desc: Equivalent block diameter (m).

outputs.name: Report
outputs.desc: Per-set table + block-size readout + unit notes.

name: Stochastic DFN (Baecher)
description: Stochastic finite-persistence discrete fracture network (Baecher disc model): Poisson centres,  Fisher-sampled poles (dispersion kappa), lognormal persistence. Intensity from spacing (P10=1/sp).  Deterministic by seed; vary the seed for a Monte-Carlo block-yield distribution. Output DFN mesh +  bench feed the BlockCutOpt packers.

algorithm.title: Baecher stochastic DFN
algorithm.citation: Baecher et al. 1977 finite-disc DFN; Fisher (1953) orientation; lognormal persistence

inputs.name: Dip
inputs.desc: Per-set dip (deg).

inputs.name: Dip dir
inputs.desc: Per-set dip-direction (deg).

inputs.name: Spacing
inputs.desc: Per-set normal spacing (m) -> intensity P10 = 1/spacing.

inputs.name: Kappa
inputs.desc: Per-set Fisher dispersion (one value applies to all). Higher = tighter.

inputs.name: Persistence
inputs.desc: Per-set mean fracture diameter (m) (one value applies to all).

inputs.name: Persistence CV
inputs.desc: Lognormal coefficient of variation of persistence.

inputs.name: Bench
inputs.desc: Domain box the fractures are generated in (also feed to BlockCutOpt Tested Area).

inputs.name: Seed
inputs.desc: Realisation seed (vary for Monte-Carlo).

outputs.name: DFN
outputs.desc: Stochastic finite-disc fracture mesh. Feed to BlockCutOpt 'Fractures'.

outputs.name: Tested area
outputs.desc: The bench box, passed through to BlockCutOpt 'Tested Area'.

outputs.name: Fractures
outputs.desc: Number of fracture discs generated.

outputs.name: P32
outputs.desc: Fracture area per unit volume (1/m).

outputs.name: Report
outputs.desc: Per-set disc counts + intensity + notes.

name: Synthetic TN Granite
description: Generate a deterministic synthetic discrete fracture network  for Tamil Nadu granite (three joint sets: NE-SW, NW-SE,  sub-horizontal bedding). Outputs a CSV of 2D traces at  z=midheight + a PLY of 3D fracture polygons + the fracture  Mesh in-process. Lets you regression-test BlockCutOpt  without a field dataset. Implements synthetic joint-set DFN generation (ISRM/Priest 1993; Goodman & Shi 1985).

algorithm.title: Synthetic joint-set DFN generator
algorithm.citation: ISRM Suggested Methods + Priest 1993 joint-set DFN

inputs.name: Bench
inputs.desc: Bench bounding box (m).

inputs.name: Seed
inputs.desc: Reproducibility seed.

inputs.name: CSV Path
inputs.desc: Output trace CSV path.

inputs.name: PLY Path
inputs.desc: Output fracture-polygon PLY path.

inputs.name: Write Files
inputs.desc: False = compute in memory only.

outputs.name: Fractures
outputs.desc: In-process fracture Mesh (consumable by BCO components).

outputs.name: Plane Count
outputs.desc: Number of fracture planes generated.

outputs.name: Trace Count
outputs.desc: Number of 2D traces at z=midheight.

outputs.name: Triangle Count
outputs.desc: Number of triangles in the PLY.

outputs.name: CSV Written
outputs.desc: CSV file path actually written (empty when W=false).

outputs.name: PLY Written
outputs.desc: PLY file path actually written (empty when W=false).

name: Packing Plan Report
description: Aggregate PackingMetricsReport + residual voids + edge-match scores  into one PackingPlanReport. All inputs come from upstream Frahan  components (Pack3D, Residual Voids, Fragment Edge Match).

inputs.name: Packing Metrics
inputs.desc: PackingMetricsReport (opaque) from Frahan Pack3D / Frahan Packing Metrics.

inputs.name: Residual Voids
inputs.desc: ResidualVoid list (opaque) from Frahan Residual Voids component.  Optional; defaults to empty.

inputs.name: Edge Match Scores
inputs.desc: Per-fragment-per-edge best match scores as a nested list  (IReadOnlyList<IReadOnlyList<double>>) wrapped opaque, or a flat  list of doubles (one entry per fragment-edge). Optional; defaults to empty.

inputs.name: Edge Match Tree
inputs.desc: Per-fragment-per-edge best match scores as a DataTree<Number>.  One branch per fragment, items in each branch are that fragment's  per-edge scores. If both Edge Match Scores (E) and Edge Match Tree  (Et) are wired, the tree takes precedence. Optional.

outputs.name: Plan Report
outputs.desc: PackingPlanReport (opaque) for downstream serialisation / further reporting.

outputs.name: Total Residual Void Area
outputs.desc: Sum of approximate areas across all residual voids.

outputs.name: Avg Best Edge Match Score
outputs.desc: Mean of best-match scores across all fragment edges. Zero if no edges supplied.

outputs.name: Summary
outputs.desc: One-line human-readable summary (PackingPlanReport.ToString()).

name: Packing Report
description: Compute summary metrics for a 3D PackResult: placements, failures,  fill ratio, average placement score, item-volume stats, per-reason  failure counts.

inputs.name: Pack Result
inputs.desc: PackResult from a 3D pack solver (opaque).

outputs.name: Placement Count
outputs.desc: Number of placed items.

outputs.name: Failure Count
outputs.desc: Number of failed items.

outputs.name: Failure Ratio
outputs.desc: Failures / (Placements + Failures).

outputs.name: Packed Volume
outputs.desc: Sum of placed item volumes.

outputs.name: Container Volume
outputs.desc: Container volume.

outputs.name: Fill Ratio
outputs.desc: Packed / Container.

outputs.name: Average Score
outputs.desc: Average placement score.

outputs.name: Max Item Height
outputs.desc: Max top-of-item Z across placements.

outputs.name: Item Volume Min/Max/Avg
outputs.desc: Three-element list: [min, max, avg].

outputs.name: Failure Reasons
outputs.desc: One '<reason>: <count>' line per distinct failure reason.

outputs.name: Report
outputs.desc: Single-line summary.

name: Report / Export
description: Audience-tailored report terminal. Pick Audience (0 engineer, 1 artist,  2 geologist). Consumes Frahan report records + optional pipe-delimited  Sections; emits Markdown + CSV. Engineer release is refused without a  declared CRS/datum. With Run + File Path it writes the .md / .csv files.

inputs.name: Reports
inputs.desc: Frahan report records (PackingReport, MeshDiagnostics, FabricationPrep,  BlockCutOpt, ChartFlatness). Optional; wire any number.

inputs.name: Sections
inputs.desc: Optional extra rows, one per line, pipe-delimited:  'Section|Label|Value|Unit|Flag' for a value row, or 'Section|note|Text'  for a note. Use this to add a block schedule, fracture sets, etc.

inputs.name: Audience
inputs.desc: 0 = Engineer (mining-plan), 1 = Artist (carving-guide), 2 = Geologist (brief).

inputs.name: Format
inputs.desc: 0 = Markdown, 1 = CSV, 2 = Both.

inputs.name: Site
inputs.desc: Site name (provenance).

inputs.name: Scan File
inputs.desc: Source scan file (provenance).

inputs.name: Date
inputs.desc: Report date (provenance).

inputs.name: CRS / Datum
inputs.desc: Coordinate reference system + datum. REQUIRED for an engineer release.

inputs.name: Units
inputs.desc: Document units (provenance). m

inputs.name: Solver Version
inputs.desc: Solver / mesh version (provenance).

inputs.name: File Path
inputs.desc: Optional output base path WITHOUT extension (e.g. C:\out\mining_plan).  Writes <path>.md and/or <path>.csv when Run is true.

inputs.name: Run
inputs.desc: Set true to write the report file(s) to File Path.

outputs.name: Markdown
outputs.desc: Audience-tailored Markdown report.

outputs.name: CSV
outputs.desc: Audience-tailored CSV report.

outputs.name: Refused
outputs.desc: True when the release was refused (engineer without a CRS).

outputs.name: Warnings
outputs.desc: Warnings + surfaced confidence flags.

outputs.name: Files Written
outputs.desc: Paths written when Run is true.

name: Carving Stages
description: Roughing-pass shells from a rough block / flat top down to the  finished sculpture (digital pointing machine). Mode 0 Radial  (smoothed normals), 1 Push-In (Front Direction), 2 Flat Top (bbox  face; best for reliefs, no Block needed); a Block input clamps stages  to an arbitrary block mesh. CACHED + Run-gated: recomputes only when  its inputs change and re-emits the cached result otherwise, so editing  a List Item index or other components never re-runs it or freezes the  canvas. Synchronous; preview off (pick a stage downstream).  Frahan-original method.

algorithm.title: Staged offset-shell roughing
algorithm.citation: Frahan-original

inputs.name: Target
inputs.desc: Finished sculpture / relief mesh (the final surface).

inputs.name: Stages
inputs.desc: Number of roughing passes (>= 1).

inputs.name: Max Offset
inputs.desc: Free-offset modes (0/1, no Block): outward offset of the roughest shell.

inputs.name: Finish Allowance
inputs.desc: Free-offset modes: offset left on the final pass (0 = exact surface).

inputs.name: Feature Boost
inputs.desc: Free-offset modes: extra stock at the strongest protrusion (ears/noses), x the offset. 0 = uniform.

inputs.name: Mode
inputs.desc: 0 = Radial (smoothed normals); 1 = Push-In (along Front Direction); 2 = Flat Top (bbox face along Front Direction - best for reliefs, no Block needed).

inputs.name: Front Direction
inputs.desc: Push-In / Flat-Top direction (e.g. +Z for a flat top); also the axis a Block sits along.

inputs.name: Block
inputs.desc: Raw stone block (optional). When given, stages are clamped to the block surface (roughest at the block, finish at the target).

inputs.name: Run
inputs.desc: Compute (when inputs change). False = keep showing the cached result. Recompute only fires when an input actually changes.

outputs.name: Stages
outputs.desc: Roughing shells, roughest first -> finish last.

outputs.name: Offsets
outputs.desc: Per-stage offset distance (free modes) or reach fraction 1..0 (Block / Flat Top).

outputs.name: Feature Weight
outputs.desc: Per-vertex protrusion weight 0..1.

outputs.name: Count
outputs.desc: Number of stage meshes produced.

name: Enlarge Sculpture
description: Digital pointing-machine scaling: enlarge a scanned maquette mesh to  a target size (Mode 0 factor, 1 target-longest, 2 target-height, 3  non-uniform XYZ). Scales from the base centre by default so a plinth  stays grounded. Wire the output into Fit In Block. Frahan-original method (digital pointing-machine; affine scale-from-base).

algorithm.title: Parametric enlargement
algorithm.citation: Frahan-original

inputs.name: Mesh
inputs.desc: Scanned maquette mesh.

inputs.name: Mode
inputs.desc: 0 = Factor, 1 = Target Longest, 2 = Target Height (Z), 3 = Non-uniform XYZ.

inputs.name: Value
inputs.desc: Mode 0: scale factor. Mode 1: target longest dimension. Mode 2: target  height. Ignored in Mode 3.

inputs.name: Target XYZ
inputs.desc: Mode 3 only: target size on each axis (model units).

inputs.name: Anchor
inputs.desc: Scale origin. Default: base centre (bbox centre X/Y at min Z).

outputs.name: Mesh
outputs.desc: Enlarged mesh.

outputs.name: Scale Factors
outputs.desc: Per-axis scale factors applied.

outputs.name: Final Size
outputs.desc: Bounding size of the enlarged mesh (x,y,z).

outputs.name: Volume
outputs.desc: Volume of the enlarged mesh (0 if not closed).

name: Fit In Block
description: Check whether a raw block can hold a (enlarged) sculpture, allowing a  kerf/roughing margin. Reports fit, per-axis clearance, and the max  scale that still fits. v1 uses bounding extents matched largest-to- largest; optionally centres the piece in the block. Frahan-original method.

algorithm.title: Bounding-extents containment + max-scale fit
algorithm.citation: Frahan-original

inputs.name: Sculpture
inputs.desc: Sculpture mesh (e.g. from Enlarge Sculpture).

inputs.name: Block
inputs.desc: Raw block mesh (available stock).

inputs.name: Margin
inputs.desc: Clearance per side subtracted from the block (kerf + roughing allowance + handling).

inputs.name: Place
inputs.desc: Centre the sculpture inside the block (translation only in v1).

outputs.name: Fits
outputs.desc: True if the block holds the sculpture (with margin).

outputs.name: Clearance
outputs.desc: Per sorted-axis slack (block - sculpture), largest axis first. Negative = overflow.

outputs.name: Max Scale To Fit
outputs.desc: Largest uniform scale of the sculpture that still fits (>=1 means it already fits).

outputs.name: Placed
outputs.desc: Sculpture centred in the block (if Place = true).

outputs.name: Report
outputs.desc: Human-readable fit summary.

name: Chart Flatness Report
description: Classify per-face area ratios against a flatness threshold.  Threshold is interpreted as max(ratio, 1/ratio); 0.5 and 2.0 are  equally distorted from 1.0. Frahan-original method.

algorithm.title: Per-face area-ratio distortion classification
algorithm.citation: Frahan-original

inputs.name: Per-Face Area Ratios
inputs.desc: List of per-face area ratios from ChartDistortionAnalyzer.

inputs.name: Threshold
inputs.desc: Flatness threshold (1.0 = no distortion allowed; 1.5 = 50% allowed; 2.0 = 2x).

outputs.name: Total Face Count
outputs.desc: Total faces classified.

outputs.name: Above Threshold Count
outputs.desc: Faces above the threshold.

outputs.name: Above Threshold Ratio
outputs.desc: Above / Total.

outputs.name: Worst Face Index
outputs.desc: Index of the most-distorted face.

outputs.name: Worst Area Ratio
outputs.desc: Normalised distortion of the worst face.

outputs.name: Per-Face Above Flag
outputs.desc: Per-face boolean: true if above threshold.

outputs.name: Report
outputs.desc: Single-line summary.

name: Pack On Surface
description: Packs 2D shapes onto a surface chart with the deterministic hole-aware nester (exact NFP  bottom-left-fill, multi-start, 0-overlap), then lifts packed curves to the 3D surface via  barycentric mapping. Runs async: the canvas stays live. [Floater 2003]

algorithm.title: Barycentric 2D-to-3D mapping
algorithm.citation: Floater 2003, Computer Aided Geometric Design 20(1):19-27 Mean value coordinates

inputs.name: Surface Map
inputs.desc: FrahanSurfaceChart from the Surface Chart component.

inputs.name: Parts
inputs.desc: Closed planar 2D part curves to pack (in the flat chart XY plane).

inputs.name: Spacing
inputs.desc: Clearance between parts and between parts and the sheet boundary (model units).

inputs.name: Rotations
inputs.desc: Allowed rotation angles in degrees. The hole-aware engine uses the COUNT of angles as its  uniform base rotation count (default list 0/90/180/270 -> 4), extended with contact angles.

inputs.name: Tolerance
inputs.desc: Geometric tolerance for the 3D barycentric mapping and containment checks.

inputs.name: Sort Mode
inputs.desc: IGNORED by the hole-aware engine (kept for compatibility). It multi-starts over  area/max-dim/width/height orders automatically - see MultiStart.

inputs.name: Corner Mode
inputs.desc: IGNORED by the hole-aware engine (kept for compatibility). Placement is always bottom-left-fill.

inputs.name: Seed
inputs.desc: IGNORED by the hole-aware engine (kept for compatibility). The engine is deterministic.

inputs.name: Max Candidates
inputs.desc: IGNORED by the hole-aware engine (kept for compatibility). The exact NFP enumerates feasible  placements directly.

inputs.name: Run
inputs.desc: Set to True to execute packing. False shows the idle message and cancels any running solve.

inputs.name: ContactRotations
inputs.desc: Longest-edge count per polygon used to build contact (edge-alignment) rotation angles. Default 6.

inputs.name: Resolution
inputs.desc: Solver sampling resolution for smooth part curves (16..200, default 24). Collision proxy only;  packed output is the exact original curve. Solve time grows ~quadratically.

inputs.name: MultiStart
inputs.desc: Deterministic part orders the engine tries, keeping the densest valid layout (1..4, default 4).  1 = single largest-first pass. Higher raises density at ~linear cost, never reduces placements.

outputs.name: Packed 3D
outputs.desc: Packed part curves lifted to the 3D surface.

outputs.name: Packed 2D
outputs.desc: Packed part curves in the flat chart plane (real units).

outputs.name: Unplaced
outputs.desc: Curves that could not be placed in the chart.

outputs.name: Failed 3D
outputs.desc: Number of packed curves that failed 3D barycentric mapping (likely cross a UV seam).

outputs.name: Report
outputs.desc: Packing and mapping report.

name: Pack Surfaces
description: Packs 2D shapes across one or more surface charts with the deterministic hole-aware  nester (exact NFP bottom-left-fill, multi-start, 0-overlap), then maps them onto the 3D  surfaces. Runs async: the canvas stays live and the result pops in when ready. Outputs  Full Transform to place original flat parts on the surface without distortion.

inputs.name: Surface Maps
inputs.desc: One or more FrahanSurfaceChart objects from the Surface Chart component. Each becomes one  sheet (greedy overflow chart 0 -> chart 1 -> ...).

inputs.name: Parts
inputs.desc: Closed planar 2D part curves to pack.

inputs.name: Spacing
inputs.desc: Clearance between parts and chart boundaries (model units).

inputs.name: Rotations
inputs.desc: Allowed rotation angles in degrees. The hole-aware engine uses the COUNT of angles as its  uniform base rotation count (default list 0/90/180/270 -> 4) and extends it with  contact (edge-alignment) angles. Pass more angles to raise the base count.

inputs.name: Tolerance
inputs.desc: Geometric tolerance for the 3D barycentric mapping and containment checks.

inputs.name: Sort Mode
inputs.desc: IGNORED by the hole-aware engine (kept for compatibility). The engine multi-starts over  area/max-dim/width/height orders automatically and keeps the best - see MultiStart.

inputs.name: Corner Mode
inputs.desc: IGNORED by the hole-aware engine (kept for compatibility). Placement is always bottom-left-fill.

inputs.name: Seed
inputs.desc: IGNORED by the hole-aware engine (kept for compatibility). The engine is deterministic:  identical inputs always reproduce the same layout.

inputs.name: Max Candidates
inputs.desc: IGNORED by the hole-aware engine (kept for compatibility). The exact NFP enumerates feasible  placements directly.

inputs.name: Run
inputs.desc: Set to True to execute packing. False shows the idle message and cancels any running solve.

inputs.name: ContactRotations
inputs.desc: Longest-edge count per polygon used to build contact (edge-alignment) rotation angles so  parts seat flush. Default 6.

inputs.name: Resolution
inputs.desc: Solver sampling resolution for smooth part curves (16..200, default 24). This only sets the  collision proxy - packed output is always the exact original curve. Solve time grows  ~quadratically; raise only for tight concave notches.

inputs.name: MultiStart
inputs.desc: Deterministic part orders the engine tries per chart, keeping the densest valid layout  (1..4, default 4). 1 = single largest-first pass. Higher raises density at ~linear cost and  never reduces placements or validity.

outputs.name: Packed 3D
outputs.desc: Packed curves lifted to the 3D surface via barycentric mapping (shape follows surface).

outputs.name: Placement Planes
outputs.desc: Rigid placement frame on the 3D surface per packed part.  Origin = centroid on surface, X/Y = surface tangent axes, Z = surface normal.

outputs.name: Transforms 3D
outputs.desc: Transform from PACKED 2D position to the 3D surface placement frame.  Apply to Packed 2D curves to get rigid (non-deformed) parts on the surface.

outputs.name: Full Transform
outputs.desc: Composed transform: original flat part -> 3D surface in one step.  Apply to the ORIGINAL part geometry (before packing) using Part Index to select it.

outputs.name: Max Deviation
outputs.desc: Maximum gap (model units) between the flat part and the curved surface  at the four bounding-box corners. Small = nearly flat. Large = needs shimming.

outputs.name: Packed 2D
outputs.desc: Packed curves in each chart's native coordinate space.

outputs.name: Chart Index
outputs.desc: Which Surface Map (0-based) each packed part was placed on.

outputs.name: Part Index
outputs.desc: 0-based index into the original Parts input list for each packed part.  Use with List Item to select the matching original part, then apply Full Transform.

outputs.name: Unplaced
outputs.desc: Curves that could not be placed on any chart.

outputs.name: Report
outputs.desc: Packing and mapping report.

name: Panel Tile Surface
description: Discretize a freeform facade surface into PLANAR stone-cladding panels: divide the surface  U x V, project each quad to its best-fit plane (stone cannot bend), and output BOTH the 3D  panels on the surface AND their flat cut outlines (laid in World XY) ready to nest on slabs  with Sheet Nest (Hole-Aware). Reports per-panel planarity (corner deviation from the panel  plane) and area - raise U/V where planarity is too high for the curvature.

algorithm.title: Planar-panel discretization of a surface for stone cladding (planarized U x V quads + flat cut outlines)
algorithm.citation: Each UV quad is projected to its best-fit plane (Plane.FitPlaneToPoints); the flat outline mapped to World XY is the cut tile.

inputs.name: Surface
inputs.desc: Facade surface to panelize (a single surface; a Brep face is coerced).

inputs.name: U Count
inputs.desc: Number of panels across the surface U direction.

inputs.name: V Count
inputs.desc: Number of panels across the surface V direction.

inputs.name: Joint
inputs.desc: Grout / joint gap: each panel is inset by this toward its  centre (m). Default 0.005.

inputs.name: Planarize
inputs.desc: Project each quad to its best-fit plane so every panel  is a FLAT cuttable tile (stone cannot bend). False = leave the warped surface quad. Default true.

outputs.name: Panels
outputs.desc: The 3D (planarized) cladding panels positioned on the surface.

outputs.name: Cut Tiles
outputs.desc: Flat closed outline per panel, mapped to the World XY plane,  ready to nest on slabs (wire into Sheet Nest (Hole-Aware) > Parts).

outputs.name: Planarity
outputs.desc: Max corner deviation from the panel plane (m) per panel.  High = the surface is too curved for that panel size; raise U / V.

outputs.name: Area
outputs.desc: Panel area (m2) per panel.

outputs.name: Report
outputs.desc: Panel count, total area, and worst planarity.

name: Surface Chart
description: Unwraps a 3D mesh to a 2D UV chart using Boundary-First Flattening (BFF).  BFF must be downloaded separately and the exe path provided as input. [Sawhney & Crane 2017]

algorithm.title: BFF boundary-first flattening
algorithm.citation: Sawhney and Crane 2017, ACM TOG 36(4):109

inputs.name: Surface
inputs.desc: Mesh to unwrap. Accepts any Rhino mesh — cleaned automatically before BFF.

inputs.name: BFF Exe Path
inputs.desc: Optional. Path to bff-command-line.exe.  Leave unconnected to auto-detect next to the .gha file.

inputs.name: Cones
inputs.desc: Number of cone singularities (0 = auto, 1–8 for complex surfaces).

inputs.name: Normalize UVs
inputs.desc: Normalize output UVs to [0,1]. Required for chart scale computation.

inputs.name: Timeout (s)
inputs.desc: Maximum seconds to wait for BFF before aborting.

inputs.name: Run
inputs.desc: Set to True to execute unwrapping.

outputs.name: Flat Mesh
outputs.desc: 2D unwrapped mesh in UV space (Z=0 plane). Scale by ChartScale for real dimensions.

outputs.name: Surface Map
outputs.desc: FrahanSurfaceChart object. Wire into the Pack On Surface component.

outputs.name: Boundary
outputs.desc: Outer boundary polyline of the flat chart scaled to real units.

outputs.name: Distortion
outputs.desc: Max/min edge stretch ratio. Values far from 1.0 indicate mapping distortion.

outputs.name: Report
outputs.desc: Quality metrics, warnings, and timing.

name: Trencadís Catalog Pack
description: Trencadís catalog packer: partition each sheet into CVD-Lloyd  cells, then optimally assign catalog parts to cells via the  Hungarian algorithm. Best when piece count matches target  coverage and you want each piece placed exactly once.

algorithm.title: CVD-Lloyd interior seeding
algorithm.citation: Lloyd 1982 centroidal Voronoi diagram relaxation

inputs.name: Parts
inputs.desc: Catalog of irregular shard curves to place. Each piece will be  placed exactly once.

inputs.name: Sheet Outlines
inputs.desc: Closed planar sheet boundary curves.

inputs.name: Sheet Holes
inputs.desc: Hole curves as a tree. Branch {0} = sheet 0, etc.

inputs.name: Tolerance
inputs.desc: Geometric tolerance.

inputs.name: Seed
inputs.desc: 0 = deterministic.

inputs.name: Run
inputs.desc: Set true to run the catalog assignment.

inputs.name: Lloyd Iterations
inputs.desc: CVD-Lloyd relaxation iterations. Higher = more uniform cells.

inputs.name: Grout
inputs.desc: Inward offset applied to each piece AFTER trim, to leave the  characteristic trencadís mortar gap. 0 = no grout. Default 0.02.

outputs.name: Placed Pieces
outputs.desc: Catalog parts placed at their assigned cell centroids.

outputs.name: Cell Seeds
outputs.desc: CVD-Lloyd seed centroids — one per assigned cell.

outputs.name: Source Indices
outputs.desc: Catalog index for each placed piece.

outputs.name: Sheet Indices
outputs.desc: Sheet index for each placed piece.

outputs.name: Cell Areas
outputs.desc: Approximate area of each assigned cell. Useful to spot  outliers where the piece is much smaller / bigger than the  cell.

outputs.name: Report
outputs.desc: Catalog packing report.

name: Trencadís Dynamic Settle
description: Light Kangaroo 2 settle for trimmed trencadís packing.  Each piece is one centroid particle. SphereCollide pushes  overlapping centroids apart, Anchor pulls back to post-  packing centroid, OnCurve sticks boundary-adjacent pieces  to the sheet edge. Pieces translate rigidly so shape and  edge lengths are exactly preserved.

algorithm.title: Trencadis dynamic settle
algorithm.citation: F-2D-002.F8 Frahan-original

inputs.name: Pieces
inputs.desc: Trimmed pieces from a trencadís packer. Centroid of each  input curve becomes the anchor target — i.e. the answer  point produced by the boundary packing algorithm upstream.

inputs.name: Sheet Outlines
inputs.desc: Closed planar sheet outlines. OnCurve targets.

inputs.name: Sheet Holes
inputs.desc: Hole curves as a tree. OnCurve targets too.

inputs.name: Apply Physics
inputs.desc: Master toggle. False = pure pass-through (output = input).  True = solve.

inputs.name: Iterations
inputs.desc: Maximum solver step count. Solver early-exits when kinetic  energy drops below 1e-6. Default 100.

inputs.name: Anchor Strength
inputs.desc: Pull strength on each centroid back toward its post-packing  position. 0 = disabled. Default 0.05.

inputs.name: Collide Strength
inputs.desc: SphereCollide goal strength. Default 1.0.

inputs.name: Boundary Pull
inputs.desc: OnCurve goal strength for centroids within 1.5× mean radius  of any boundary curve. 0 = disabled. Default 0.5.

inputs.name: Collide Radius Factor
inputs.desc: Multiplier on the mean per-piece bounding radius used as  the SphereCollide radius. >1 = more space between pieces.  <1 = pieces can overlap (centroid distance allowed to be  less than mean radius). Default 1.0.

inputs.name: Strict Containment
inputs.desc: Hard per-vertex boundary collider. When True, after each  Kangaroo step the proposed centroid translation is binary- searched to find the largest fraction that keeps EVERY  vertex of the piece inside at least one Sheet Outline and  outside every Sheet Hole. Pieces whose initial vertices  are already outside the boundary won't move (fraction = 0).  When False, only the soft OnCurve boundary pull on centroids  is applied (legacy behaviour). Default True.

outputs.name: Settled Pieces
outputs.desc: Pieces translated by (settledCentroid − originalCentroid).  Shape and edge lengths preserved exactly via rigid translation.

outputs.name: Translations
outputs.desc: Per-piece translation vector applied during settle.

outputs.name: Final Centroids
outputs.desc: Per-piece centroid after settling.

outputs.name: Final vSum
outputs.desc: Final kinetic-energy sum. < 1e-6 indicates well converged.

outputs.name: Residual Overlap
outputs.desc: Sum of polygon-pair intersection areas after settle. This  is the dynamic tolerance — how much overlap remains in the  tolerance-based packing once Kangaroo has resolved as much  as it can. 0 = clean fit.

outputs.name: Report
outputs.desc: Settle report.

name: Trencadís EdgeMatch
description: Trencadís packer driven by the EdgeMatch beam-search solver.  Each sheet outline becomes an anchored frame; parts are placed  by their complementary edges against the frame and against  previously-placed parts. Output is deterministic for fixed  input order.

algorithm.title: EdgeMatch-powered Trencadis pack
algorithm.citation: Frahan-original alternative to Battiato 2013 CVD+GVF stack

inputs.name: Parts
inputs.desc: Closed planar shard curves to pack.

inputs.name: Sheet Outlines
inputs.desc: Closed sheet boundary curves. Each becomes an anchored frame  for one independent EdgeMatch run.

inputs.name: Joint Width
inputs.desc: Allowed mean edge-to-edge gap (document units). Mapped onto  EdgeMatch's residual threshold: matches further than this are  rejected. Default 0.5.

inputs.name: Sample Spacing
inputs.desc: Arc-length sample spacing along each contour. Match scanner  resolution.

inputs.name: Break Angle
inputs.desc: Curvature break-point threshold in degrees per window.

inputs.name: Min Segment Length
inputs.desc: Below this chord length a segment is treated as noise.

inputs.name: Beam Width
inputs.desc: Concurrent beam states retained per iteration. 16 recommended  for Trencadís (more local minima than wood).

inputs.name: Max Iterations
inputs.desc: Outer-loop iteration cap.

inputs.name: Run
inputs.desc: Execute the solver.

inputs.name: Non-Crossing
inputs.desc: Order-preserving rim correspondence. FALSE (default) = free  nearest-point ICP (unchanged behaviour). TRUE = monotone,  non-crossing point pairing between shard edges; more robust on  wiggly / noisy fracture edges where free matching tangles.

outputs.name: Placed Pieces
outputs.desc: Shard contours transformed into their solved placements.  Sheet outlines are not included (they are the identity frame).

outputs.name: Transforms
outputs.desc: Per-piece rigid transform.

outputs.name: Source Indices
outputs.desc: Original Parts list index for each placed piece.

outputs.name: Sheet Indices
outputs.desc: Sheet list index this piece was placed onto.

outputs.name: Unplaced
outputs.desc: Source-curve copies of parts that did not find a match on any sheet.

outputs.name: Residuals
outputs.desc: Per-placement ICP residual.

outputs.name: Total Residual
outputs.desc: Sum of all per-placement residuals across all sheets.

outputs.name: Report
outputs.desc: Human-readable per-sheet summary.

name: Trencadís Pack
description: Trencadís ('broken-tile') 2D mosaic packer. Places irregular  pieces with bounded overlap, then boolean-differences  the overlapping bits so pieces butt edge-to-edge with  characteristic chipped fits. Optional grout offset leaves  the mortar gap. Run-gated (set Run=true to pack).

algorithm.title: Trencadis greedy pack basic
algorithm.citation: Gaudi Park Guell broken-tile mosaic technique

inputs.name: Parts
inputs.desc: Closed planar shard curves to pack. Irregular shapes welcome —  trencadís is at its best with non-uniform pieces.

inputs.name: Sheet Outlines
inputs.desc: Closed planar sheet boundary curves. The mosaic is built  inside these.

inputs.name: Sheet Holes
inputs.desc: Hole curves as a tree. Branch {0} = sheet 0, {1} = sheet 1, etc.

inputs.name: Spacing
inputs.desc: Pre-trim part-to-part clearance. The trim post-pass removes  everything inside this clearance, so think of it as the  MAXIMUM grout gap (actual gap = Grout, see below).

inputs.name: Rotations
inputs.desc: Allowed rotation angles in degrees. Default 0/45/90/135 to  encourage varied edge orientation typical of trencadís work.

inputs.name: Tolerance
inputs.desc: Geometric tolerance for containment / collision / boolean  difference.

inputs.name: Seed
inputs.desc: 0 = deterministic; non-zero changes tie-breaking randomisation  of placement order.

inputs.name: Run
inputs.desc: Set to true to execute packing.

inputs.name: Max Candidates
inputs.desc: Candidate budget per part per rotation. Trencadís typically  needs MORE candidates than V506 because trim acceptance  expands the feasible set. 0 = default (600).

inputs.name: Trim Tolerance
inputs.desc: Maximum allowed part-to-part overlap depth (document units)  during placement. Larger = more aggressive chipping, denser  pack. Default 0.2; for meter-scale 0.1–1.0.

inputs.name: Grout
inputs.desc: Inward offset applied to each piece AFTER trim, to leave the  characteristic trencadís mortar gap. 0 = no grout (raw  edge-to-edge). Default 0.02.

inputs.name: Boundary Mode
inputs.desc: 0 = off (interior fill only).  1 = boundary-aware bias: shards with edges matching the sheet  or hole edges are placed first AND auto-rotated to align with  the matched boundary tangent. All candidate sources used.  2 = strict two-phase ring/interior — boundary-worthy shards  use only boundary-anchor candidates first (true ring), then  non-boundary shards fill the interior. Falls back to all  candidates if a phase is saturated.  3 = uniform curve division — divide each boundary curve by  arc length; place each shard with longest edge tangent to  the curve at its assigned position.

inputs.name: Min Boundary Affinity
inputs.desc: Edge-match score at or above which a shard is considered  boundary-worthy. Range [0, 1]. Only applies when Boundary  Mode > 0.

inputs.name: Cut Budget
inputs.desc: Battiato 2013 §4 cumulative-cut cap as a fraction of each  shard's area. T_N = budget on a NEW shard's total chipping  across all neighbours; T_P = budget on a PLACED shard  (derived as Cut/2); single-cut caps S_N (Cut/2) and S_P  (Cut/4) cap any one chip. Default 0.35 matches Battiato's  recommended T_N. Lower → less aggressive chipping, more  shard-shape preservation. 0 → no cuts allowed (strict  no-overlap; defeats the trencadís technique).

inputs.name: Use CVD Seeds
inputs.desc: Initialize per-sheet placement using CVD-Lloyd seed points  (blue-noise distribution). Improves coverage uniformity vs  the bbox-corner default starting point.

inputs.name: Use GVF Orientation
inputs.desc: Compute Gradient Vector Flow over each sheet to bias shard  rotation toward the local boundary tangent. Pieces follow  curves like Gaudí's columns. Slower than the discrete  rotation list alone but produces the flow-line look.

inputs.name: GVF Smoothness
inputs.desc: GVF μ (smoothness regularizer). Lower (0.05–0.15) → field  follows boundary closely, sharper. Higher (0.3–0.5) →  smoother propagation into interior. Default 0.2 (Battiato  2008).

outputs.name: Trencadís Pieces
outputs.desc: Final shard curves after trim + grout. Use these for  downstream rendering / fabrication.

outputs.name: Pre-Trim Pieces
outputs.desc: Placed shards BEFORE the trim post-pass. Useful for  debugging which piece chipped which.

outputs.name: Transforms
outputs.desc: Placement transforms (per source curve).

outputs.name: Source Indices
outputs.desc: Original input curve index for each placed shard.

outputs.name: Sheet Indices
outputs.desc: Sheet index used for each placed shard.

outputs.name: Trim Adjacency
outputs.desc: DataTree per shard: branch i lists the SOURCE indices of  earlier-placed shards that chipped Trencadís Pieces[i].

outputs.name: Unplaced
outputs.desc: Shards that could not be placed even with trim tolerance.

outputs.name: Failure Reasons
outputs.desc: Reason for each unplaced shard.

outputs.name: Sheet Preview
outputs.desc: Outer sheet and hole preview curves.

outputs.name: Report
outputs.desc: Trencadís packing report (counts, timings, trim events).

name: Trencadís Pipeline
description: All-in-one trencadís pipeline. Deterministic boundary  pack first; if residual overlap remains, Kangaroo 2  settle fills the gaps. Exposes solver controls (kinetic  energy threshold, momentum) for cases where the  deterministic pass alone is insufficient.

algorithm.title: Trencadis greedy pack
algorithm.citation: Gaudi Park Guell broken-tile mosaic technique

inputs.name: Parts
inputs.desc: Closed planar shard curves to pack.

inputs.name: Sheet Outlines
inputs.desc: Closed planar sheet outlines.

inputs.name: Sheet Holes
inputs.desc: Hole curves as a tree.

inputs.name: Run
inputs.desc: Master toggle. False = no output.

inputs.name: Apply Physics
inputs.desc: Toggle for the dynamic settle stage. Right-click → Show  Physics Tuning to expose strength / convergence / momentum  controls.

inputs.name: Live Animate
inputs.desc: Step-by-step animated settle with viewport overlay. Right- click → Show Animation Tuning to expose frame controls.

outputs.name: Settled Pieces
outputs.desc: Final pieces.

outputs.name: Packed Pieces
outputs.desc: Boundary-packed pieces BEFORE physics.

outputs.name: Translations
outputs.desc: Per-piece translation applied during settle.

outputs.name: Final Centroids
outputs.desc: Per-piece centroid after the pipeline.

outputs.name: Source Indices
outputs.desc: Original input curve index per placed shard.

outputs.name: Final vSum
outputs.desc: Final kinetic-energy sum.

outputs.name: Residual Overlap
outputs.desc: Sum of polygon-pair intersection areas after pipeline.

outputs.name: Report
outputs.desc: Pipeline report.

outputs.name: Pre-Trim Pieces
outputs.desc: Pre-trim placed curves directly out of the boundary  packer (TrencadisFill.PackedCurves). One curve per  placed source. Compare against Packed Pieces (Cp) to see  what was trimmed off near the sheet edge / hole edges.

outputs.name: Transforms
outputs.desc: Per-placed-piece rigid transform from the source curve's  frame to its world placement. One Transform per piece,  parallel to Packed Pieces (Cp) and Source Indices (Src).  Apply to any source-frame geometry (drill points, hatch  patterns) to bring it into the placed frame.

outputs.name: Trim Adjacency
outputs.desc: Per-piece tree where branch {i} holds the source-indices  of OTHER pieces that trimmed piece i during the deterministic  boundary pass. Empty branch = piece i was not trimmed  against any other piece. Useful for auditing chain-cut  relationships in trencadís layouts.

name: Arch Voussoirs
description: Generate a stereotomic arch as N radial voussoir cells (8-vertex  wedge solids; bed joints normal to the intrados). Profiles:  Semicircular / Segmental / Pointed / Catenary. Outputs the cut-stone  cells plus a typed VoussoirAssembly for Voussoir Stone Matcher + the  rubble match-and-trim (example 21). Grounded in  wiki/research/stereotomy_voussoir_from_rubble.md.

algorithm.title: RadialVoussoirCells
algorithm.citation: Frahan-original: intrados curve -> arc-length stations -> 8-vertex wedge solids with radial bed joints (extrados = intrados offset by ring thickness along the outward normal)

inputs.name: Profile
inputs.desc: Intrados profile: 0 = Semicircular, 1 = Segmental, 2 = Pointed  (equilateral), 3 = Catenary. Default 0.

inputs.name: Intrados Radius
inputs.desc: Intrados radius (m). For Catenary this is the span. Default 2.0.

inputs.name: Ring Thickness
inputs.desc: Radial thickness intrados->extrados (m). Default 0.55.

inputs.name: Width
inputs.desc: Out-of-plane voussoir width (m). Default 0.6.

inputs.name: Count
inputs.desc: Number of voussoirs. Default 11.

inputs.name: Included Angle
inputs.desc: Included angle for the Segmental profile (deg, 0..180). Ignored by  the other profiles. Default 120.

inputs.name: Rise
inputs.desc: Apex rise for the Catenary profile (m). 0 = use Intrados Radius.  Ignored by the other profiles. Default 0.

inputs.name: Base Point
inputs.desc: Origin to translate the arch to (built in world XZ, width along Y,  springers on z=0). Default world origin.

outputs.name: Cells
outputs.desc: The voussoir cut-stone cells (closed wedge solids), in install order.

outputs.name: Assembly
outputs.desc: Typed VoussoirAssembly. Wire into Voussoir Stone Matcher (D5F10010).

outputs.name: Bed Planes
outputs.desc: Per-voussoir lower bed-joint plane (radial). The springer's is the springing plane.

outputs.name: Centroids
outputs.desc: Per-voussoir centroid.

outputs.name: Volumes
outputs.desc: Per-voussoir volume (m^3).

outputs.name: Keystone
outputs.desc: Index of the keystone voussoir (nearest the apex).

outputs.name: Intrados
outputs.desc: The intrados (soffit) curve.

outputs.name: Report
outputs.desc: Build summary (profile, span, rise, total volume, closedness).

name: Pendentive Vault Voussoirs
description: Generate a pendentive (sail) dome (sphere over a square) tessellated  on a grid into voussoir cells along the sphere's lines of curvature,  each extruded radially by the shell thickness. Outputs the cut-stone  cells plus a typed VoussoirAssembly for Voussoir Stone Matcher + the  rubble match-and-trim (example 22). Grounded in  wiki/research/stereotomy_voussoir_from_rubble.md.

algorithm.title: PendentiveDomeCells
algorithm.citation: Frahan-original: square plan grid lifted onto a sphere (z=sqrt(R^2-x^2-y^2)) then extruded radially by the shell thickness -> 8-vertex cells along lines of curvature

inputs.name: Sphere Radius
inputs.desc: Sphere radius (m). Default 2.5.

inputs.name: Square Half Width
inputs.desc: Half the side of the square plan (m). Must satisfy 2*h^2 < R^2 so the  corners lie on the sphere. Default 1.6.

inputs.name: Shell Thickness
inputs.desc: Radial shell thickness intrados->extrados (m). Default 0.4.

inputs.name: Grid U
inputs.desc: Cells across the plan in U. Default 6.

inputs.name: Grid V
inputs.desc: Cells across the plan in V. Default 6.

inputs.name: Drop To Ground
inputs.desc: Translate so the springing corners rest on z=0. Default true.

inputs.name: Base Point
inputs.desc: Origin to translate the vault to. Default world origin.

outputs.name: Cells
outputs.desc: The voussoir cut-stone cells (closed solids), one per grid cell.

outputs.name: Assembly
outputs.desc: Typed VoussoirAssembly. Wire into Voussoir Stone Matcher (D5F10010).

outputs.name: Bed Planes
outputs.desc: Per-cell intrados (bed) plane: centre + outward sphere radial.

outputs.name: Centroids
outputs.desc: Per-cell centroid.

outputs.name: Volumes
outputs.desc: Per-cell volume (m^3).

outputs.name: Report
outputs.desc: Build summary (R, span, thickness, grid, springing/apex, total volume).

name: Voussoir Ingest
description: Read a list of voussoir meshes (from the Voussoir GH plugin or  Frahan Stereotomic Vault Mode) as a typed VoussoirAssembly.  Per-voussoir record carries OBB + volume + centroid + bed/head  planes + load axis + joint class. Emits MatchItem[] for downstream  MatcherContextBuilder (the substrate spine). First step of the  top-down voussoir-to-stone workflow per philosophy doc §10.6.

algorithm.title: Bed-Head plane detection via largest-face heuristic
algorithm.citation: Frahan-original: sort voussoir faces by area; bed = largest, head = second-largest

inputs.name: Voussoirs
inputs.desc: List of voussoir geometries representing the designed stereotomic  assembly. Accepts EITHER Mesh OR Brep. The Voussoir GH plugin by  Varela (FAUP Porto STBIM) emits BREP per voussoir as a GH data  tree -- this component handles both natively. Closed solids  preferred; Breps are meshed via Mesh.CreateFromBrep at default  MeshingParameters quality.

inputs.name: Joint Classes
inputs.desc: Optional per-voussoir position-role tags: 'bed' / 'head' / 'key' /  'ground' / 'void' (default 'void'). Same count as Voussoirs (or  empty / single-value for default).

inputs.name: Thrust Curve
inputs.desc: Optional funicular thrust curve (from TNA form-finding or hand  drafting). Drives LoadAxis per voussoir via closest-point-tangent.

inputs.name: Lithology Hints
inputs.desc: Optional per-voussoir lithology constraint (e.g. 'Vermont Marble').  Used as a categorical constraint in the matcher.

inputs.name: Ground Anchor Indices
inputs.desc: Optional indices of springer / abutment voussoirs (start points  of the install DAG). Empty = auto-detect via lowest centroid Z.

inputs.name: Adjacency Threshold
inputs.desc: Fraction of face-diagonal for adjacency detection. Default 0.05  (5% of object span). Faces within this distance count as a shared joint.

inputs.name: Provenance
inputs.desc: Optional provenance string for the assembly (e.g. 'Voussoir plugin v2.3 output').

outputs.name: Assembly
outputs.desc: The typed VoussoirAssembly. Wire into VoussoirStoneMatcher +  VoussoirPackIntoBlock downstream.

outputs.name: Match Items
outputs.desc: List of MatchItem (substrate-compatible). Wire into MatcherContextBuilder  as the Demand side. Numeric props: Volume, MaxDim, MinDim, Height.  Categorical: JointClass, LithologyHint.

outputs.name: OBBs
outputs.desc: Per-voussoir oriented bounding boxes (AABB v1).

outputs.name: Volumes
outputs.desc: Per-voussoir mesh volume (absolute).

outputs.name: Centroids
outputs.desc: Per-voussoir geometric centroid.

outputs.name: Bed Planes
outputs.desc: Per-voussoir bed-joint plane (largest-area face heuristic v1).

outputs.name: Head Planes
outputs.desc: Per-voussoir head-joint plane (second-largest-area face heuristic v1).

outputs.name: Load Axes
outputs.desc: Per-voussoir compressive-load direction (thrust-curve tangent if  supplied, else OBB longest-axis).

outputs.name: Adjacency Pairs
outputs.desc: Pairs of voussoir indices that share a joint face (flat list:  [i0, j0, i1, j1, ...]).

outputs.name: Remarks
outputs.desc: Per-voussoir + assembly-level diagnostic notes.

name: Voussoir Pack Into Block
description: Pack as many voussoirs as possible into a single quarried block.  Greedy first-fit-decreasing on AABB extents (v1; v2 routes through  BlockPackTree DLBF + CGAL exact-shape fit). Outputs: placed  voussoir indices + per-voussoir transforms + cut-plane plan +  achieved yield ratio. Use case: extract all voussoirs of a vault  from one large quarry block (Quarra Two Horse Relief pattern).

algorithm.title: Greedy first-fit-decreasing 3D bin-pack
algorithm.citation: Frahan-original v1 packing; foundational reference: Garey-Johnson 1979 FFD

inputs.name: Assembly
inputs.desc: VoussoirAssembly from VoussoirIngestComponent (D5F1000F).

inputs.name: Block
inputs.desc: A single quarried block (accepts QuarryBlock typed record from  ScanToBlockInventoryComponent F2D0BC20, OR a raw Mesh).

inputs.name: Spacing
inputs.desc: Gap between adjacent voussoirs (mm), used as the saw-kerf +  carving allowance. Default 5.0 mm.

inputs.name: Grid Step
inputs.desc: Grid step for the candidate-position scan (mm). Smaller = denser  search = slower. Default 25.0 mm.

inputs.name: Allow Skip
inputs.desc: If true, voussoirs that cannot be placed are skipped + reported.  If false, fail loudly when any voussoir cannot be placed. Default true.

outputs.name: Placed Voussoirs
outputs.desc: Per-voussoir transformed mesh (placed in the block's local frame).  Null where the voussoir could not be placed.

outputs.name: Transforms
outputs.desc: Per-voussoir placement transform (identity where unplaced).

outputs.name: Fit Voussoir Indices
outputs.desc: Indices of voussoirs that were successfully placed (sorted by  placement order = volume descending).

outputs.name: Skip Voussoir Indices
outputs.desc: Indices of voussoirs that could NOT be placed (under-provisioned).

outputs.name: Yield Ratio
outputs.desc: Sum-of-placed-volumes / block-volume. >= 0.4 is typically  production-acceptable per UCL Devadass 2025 §2.7.

outputs.name: Cut Planes
outputs.desc: Cut-plan: one plane per adjacent-voussoir-pair joint inside the  block. The cutting sequence is implied by placement order.

outputs.name: Remarks
outputs.desc: Diagnostic notes -- voussoir count placed/skipped, yield,  block fill rate.

name: Voussoir Stone Matcher
description: Assign each voussoir to a quarry stone via Kuhn 1955 Hungarian  bipartite assignment. Voussoirs are demand; stones are supply;  feasibility = stone OBB contains voussoir OBB + safety margin +  yield_ratio >= MinYield; cost = w_yield * (1 - yield_ratio) +  w_carving * (carving_vol / voussoir_vol). The canonical top-down  voussoir-to-stone matcher per wiki/research/voussoir_stereotomy_integration.md  Phase 2 + philosophy doc §10.6. First production use of the  MatcherRegistry substrate.

algorithm.title: Tomczak2023Matching
algorithm.citation: Tomczak/Haakonsen/Luczkowski 2023 Environ. Res. Infrastruct. Sustain. 3:035005 DOI 10.1088/2634-4505/acf341 -- Figure 2 5-stage matching pipeline

inputs.name: Assembly
inputs.desc: VoussoirAssembly from VoussoirIngestComponent (D5F1000F).

inputs.name: Quarry Stones
inputs.desc: List of quarry-block candidates. Accepts either:  (a) QuarryBlock typed records from ScanToBlockInventoryComponent  (F2D0BC20), or (b) raw Mesh inputs (in which case AABB+volume  are computed inline). Mixed lists are accepted.

inputs.name: Min Yield
inputs.desc: Minimum yield ratio (voussoir_vol / stone_vol) for a feasible pair.  Default 0.4 (40%). Stones below this are excluded as wasteful.

inputs.name: Safety Margin
inputs.desc: Safety margin added to voussoir OBB extent before containment test  (mm). Default 5.0.

inputs.name: Yield Weight
inputs.desc: Cost weight for the yield term `1 - yield_ratio`. Default 1.0.

inputs.name: Carving Weight
inputs.desc: Cost weight for the carving term `(stone_vol - voussoir_vol) / voussoir_vol`.  Default 0.5.

inputs.name: Allow Empty
inputs.desc: If true, unassigned voussoirs are reported (under-provisioned case).  If false, fail loudly when any voussoir would remain unassigned.  Default true.

outputs.name: Assignment
outputs.desc: Per-voussoir stone index (-1 = unassigned).

outputs.name: Placed Stones
outputs.desc: Per-voussoir assigned stone mesh (null where unassigned).

outputs.name: Yield Ratios
outputs.desc: Per-voussoir yield ratio (voussoir_vol / stone_vol). 0 if unassigned.

outputs.name: Carving Volumes
outputs.desc: Per-voussoir carving volume (stone_vol - voussoir_vol). 0 if unassigned.

outputs.name: Per-Pair Cost
outputs.desc: Per-voussoir total cost (yield + carving weighted sum).  +Inf where unassigned / infeasible.

outputs.name: Unassigned Voussoirs
outputs.desc: Indices of voussoirs that received no stone (under-provisioned).

outputs.name: Unused Stones
outputs.desc: Indices of stones that were not consumed.

outputs.name: Total Cost
outputs.desc: Sum of per-pair costs across the assignment.

outputs.name: Remarks
outputs.desc: Diagnostic notes -- strategy, infeasibility reasons, M*N matrix size.
